steps.m: Three special letters: Alif, Lam, Ra
steps.n: These are three separate Arabic letters written in their basic form

steps.m: Three special letters: Alif, Lam, Ra (that begin this chapter)
steps.n: The letters are written connected with a special mark (مد) over the Alif. These mysterious letters appear at the start of some Quran chapters. While scholars have suggested many meanings, their exact purpose is known only to Allah.
This is the basic meaning that all words from this root relate to
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make the root letters into 'he wrote' in past tense
Changed the pattern to make it into a noun that represents the result of writing
Added tanwin (double damma) to show this is indefinite and in the nominative case. This word is the subject of the verb أُحْكِمَتْ (were perfected) in this ayah.

steps.m: to make wise decisions or judgments
steps.n: This root relates to wisdom and judgment
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding hamza at the start makes the action affect something else - like making something become firm instead of just being firm
Changed to passive voice - shows something received the action of being made firm

steps.m: it (feminine) was made firm/precise (referring to ءَايٰتُهُۥ/its verses)
steps.n: The ت at the end shows this is feminine, matching with 'verses' (ءَايٰتُهُۥ) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: to show, to be a sign
steps.n: This root relates to signs and clear marks
Adding ة turns this into a feminine noun meaning 'a sign'
Adding ٌ shows this is indefinite (a sign) and in the subject position
The word changes from one sign/verse to many signs/verses by adding ات (aat) at the end - this is a common way to make things plural in Arabic
The ُ (u) at the end shows this word is in the nominative case, like when it's the subject of a sentence
Adding هُ at the end makes it mean 'his signs' - the هُ shows possession
Adding ۥ is a pronunciation helper that shows how to read the هُ clearly

steps.m: then (connects this ayah to the previous one)
steps.n: This word connects ideas in order, like saying 'first this, then that'. Here it connects how the verses were 'made firm' and then 'explained in detail'.
This root relates to separating or detailing things
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger. Added basic vowels to connect letters.
Changing the vowels shows this is happening to something (passive voice) rather than someone doing it

steps.m: it (feminine) was explained in detail (referring to ءَايٰتُهُۥ - His verses)
steps.n: The تْ ending shows this is talking about a feminine thing (the verses) in the past
This is a preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or belonging to something. In this ayah, it connects 'a book' (كِتٰبٌ) to 'presence/side' (لَّدُنْ) to show where the book came from.
This word is usually used to mean 'from the presence of'
The vowels help show this word means 'from near' or 'from the presence of'
The sukun at the end shows this is the complete form of the word

steps.m: from near/from the presence of (of حَكِيمٍ خَبِيرٍ)
steps.n: The letter ل gets doubled (with shaddah) to connect smoothly with the previous word. Shows this word is linked to 'All-Wise, All-Aware' in the ayah.
This root relates to wisdom and judgment
Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone/something that has complete wisdom. In this ayah, it describes كِتٰبٌ (the book/scripture).

steps.m: wise one (describes كِتٰبٌ, connected to خَبِيرٍ)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is connected to خَبِيرٍ (All-Aware) that follows it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who is fully aware/informed (describing حَكِيمٍ)
steps.n: Adding ي between second and third root letters with kasra before it creates an intensive form meaning 'one who has complete knowledge'. This word describes حَكِيمٍ (wise) in this ayah.

steps.m: fully aware/informed (describing حَكِيمٍ and showing it follows مِن)
steps.n: The tanween kasratain ending shows this word follows the word مِن (from) in this ayah
Starting with the first particle أن which means 'that'
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation
Combined with لا (not) and merged the letters, creating a shadda on the ل. This word introduces what should not be done in the following verb تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ (you worship).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changes from 'he worshipped' to 'you worship' by adding تَ at the start to show 'you' and changing the vowels to show present time
Added plural masculine suffix وا to indicate multiple people doing the action
This is the base form of the exception particle. It shows that what comes after it is different or excluded from what came before.

steps.m: except (what comes after this word is excluded from تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ - worship)
steps.n: The shadda and alif are added to strengthen the meaning and connect it to what follows. In this ayah, it's saying 'don't worship (anything) except Allah.'
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it a unique name for The One True God

steps.m: Allah (as the object being worshipped in أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being worshipped in this sentence
This is a special word used to make statements stronger, like adding 'truly' in English
Added نِى at the end to show 'I' am speaking. This word connects to اللَّهَ (Allah) in this ayah, showing who is being addressed.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you all (recipients of نَذِيرٌ وَبَشِيرٌ)
steps.n: Added كم (you all) to show who is being addressed. This word connects to نَذِيرٌ وَبَشِيرٌ (warner and bringer of good news) in the ayah, showing who the warner and good news are for.
Basic preposition meaning 'from' (connected to نَذِيرٌ - warner)
Adds pronoun ه (him/it) to show what something comes from
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on م for stronger pronunciation when connecting to previous word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he warned' to 'warner' by adding ي - like changing 'warn' to 'warner' in English
Added tanween dammah to show this is any warner, not a specific one
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (giving news) to a noun meaning someone who gives news

steps.m: a giver of good news (paired with نَذِيرٌ)
steps.n: Added tanween to show it's indefinite and matches the case of نَذِيرٌ that came before it

steps.m: and a giver of good news
steps.n: Added و to connect this word with نَذِيرٌ (warner) that came before it
This is a basic particle that introduces a clause or command. Here it connects to the verb ٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ (seek forgiveness).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding است at the beginning changes the meaning to 'seeking' the action
Changes the verb from past to present tense

steps.m: all of you seek forgiveness! (command to رَبَّكُمْ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added وا to make it a command to multiple people. The word is connected to رَبَّكُمْ (your Lord) who they are seeking forgiveness from.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter ب to show doing something with more effort/intensity
Added كُمْ to show this refers to your (plural) Lord. This word is the object of the verb اسْتَغْفِرُوا (seek forgiveness from)
Starting with the basic particle that shows one thing happens after another

steps.m: then (connects تُوبُوٓا۟ 'turn in repentance' to what came before)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects the command to turn in repentance with the previous command to seek forgiveness, showing these should happen one after the other.
Changed from root word to a command telling one person to repent - made by adding damma (u) and making middle letter silent
Added وا at the end to make it a command for multiple people
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِ at the end to show who the action is directed to (him)
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb

The يُ at the beginning shows this happens in the present/future, and the shadda makes it intensive - meaning he gives a lot of enjoyment. This verb connects to متاعا (enjoyment) which comes after it to emphasize the meaning.

steps.m: he will give enjoyment (in response to something)
steps.n: The sukun shows this is a response to something - in this case, it's what will happen if people seek forgiveness (mentioned earlier in the verse)

steps.m: he will give you all enjoyment (if you seek forgiveness)
steps.n: Adds كُم to show who receives the enjoyment - referring to all the people being addressed
The root letters form a noun meaning temporary enjoyment or provision
Basic vowels (fatha) added between letters to make the root readable as a noun meaning provision
Added emphasis to first letter to show intensity
Added long 'aa' sound to stretch out the word

steps.m: a temporary provision (object of يُمَتِّعْكُم - He will provide you)
steps.n: Changed from a general noun to a specific object (by adding -an) to show it's the thing being provided
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic adjective

steps.m: good/beautiful (describes the enjoyment متٰعًا that Allah will give)
steps.n: Added -an ending to show this word describes how something is (it describes the enjoyment as being good)
This is a preposition that shows direction or endpoint. It connects the verb 'turn in repentance' (تُوبُوٓا۟) with its destination 'a term' (أَجَلٍ).
Added a small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a special kind of alif at the end of the word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added a small vertical line (ٓ) to show the sound is stretched slightly when reading. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to set a time limit or deadline
steps.n: Forms a noun meaning a set time or term
The tanween at the end makes it 'a' deadline rather than 'the' deadline
Adding the basic vowel marks to make the word readable
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to show something is receiving the action, doubled the middle letter for emphasis, and changed the final و to ى because it's at the end
The م has a shaddah (doubled) because it comes after a silent letter, making it stronger when pronounced. The meaning stays the same.
Added tanween to show this is describing something indefinite
Connected the root letters together
Added يُ at the start to make it mean 'he brings' (present tense). The root أتي changes shape when you add يُ, which is why we see ؤ in the middle.

steps.m: and he gives/brings (connected to يُمَتِّعْكُم - he provides you)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the start. The final ي is dropped because this is a special grammar form. This verb is connected to يُمَتِّعْكُم (he provides you) earlier in the verse.
This word shows totality or completeness
The shadda (double letter) makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of يُؤْتِ (he gives)
This is the basic form of the word that shows possession or ownership

steps.m: owner of, possessor of (describes فَضْلٍ - bounty)
steps.n: Changed the ending to show this word is connected to what comes after it (genitive case). Here it's connected to the word فَضْلٍ (bounty), making the phrase 'possessor of bounty'
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning 'bounty' or 'favor' from the basic meaning of exceeding or surpassing
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is an indefinite noun being possessed by ذِى (owner of)
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and form a noun meaning 'bounty' or 'favor'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُؤْتِ (he gives)
Added هُۥ at the end to show this bounty belongs to him. This word is what is being given in the phrase 'he gives his bounty'
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if you turn away'
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about seeking forgiveness
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added تَ at start and doubled ل to show doing action to oneself. The ي changed to ى because it's at the end.
Added وْا at the end to show many people did the action
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds emphasis to what follows
Adding ي at the end shows this is about me (first person)

steps.m: then indeed I (connected to تَوَلَّوْا۟ - they turn away)
steps.n: Adding فَ at the beginning shows this follows from the previous statement about 'if they turn away'
Connected letters with vowels. The middle weak letter و changes to ا
Changed to first person present tense by adding أ at start and ُ at end
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' - it comes from the concept of being high/elevated
The ى changes to يْ when adding a pronoun suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon you all (connected to أَخَافُ - I fear)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This word connects to أَخَافُ (I fear) to say what is being feared for them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to transform the verb into a noun meaning 'punishment'. This word is what is being feared in the phrase 'I fear for you'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic word for 'day'
Added the tanween kasra to show this is 'a day' that is owned by or connected to something (in this case, it's part of يَوْمٍ كَبِيرٍ meaning 'a great day')
Added fatha on first letter, kasra on second letter, and ي between second and third letters to make an adjective meaning 'great'
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this describes يَوْمٍ (day) in the phrase 'punishment of a great day'
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects with اللَّهِ (Allah) to show that the return is towards Allah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the middle letter to show uniqueness. The ِ at the end shows this connects to the next word. This is the proper name for the one true God in Islam.

steps.m: place or time of return (related to إِلَى ٱللَّهِ - to Allah)
steps.n: Added مَ at the start and ِ in the middle to show where or when something happens. This word connects to إِلَى ٱللَّهِ (to Allah) to show where the return is to.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this belongs to or relates to multiple people being spoken to
كُمْ means 'your' when talking to multiple people
This is a basic pronoun used for male singular or things. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah who is described as قَدِيرٌ (capable/able)
وَ at the start means 'and'
This is a basic preposition showing position or authority over something. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning.
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
The kasra shows this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, making the phrase mean 'every thing'

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Adding vowels to make it a noun meaning 'thing'
Changed spelling of ي to ى but pronunciation stays the same
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end because this word belongs to كُلِّ (every/all) in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from a past-tense verb (he had power) to a strong describing word showing Allah has complete power

steps.m: all-powerful (describes اللَّهِ and shows it's being used as a description)
steps.n: The double mark (tanwin) at the end shows this word is describing Allah
Starting with the basic negative particle with a fatha mark for pronunciation
Adding أَ at the beginning changes this from a negative particle to an attention-getting word
The alif is elongated (with مد) to add emphasis to this attention-getting word
This particle adds emphasis to statements

steps.m: indeed they (emphasizes the pronoun 'they' before يَثْنُونَ)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ which means 'they/them' to show who is being emphasized. This connects to the verb يَثْنُونَ (they fold) that comes after it.

steps.m: to fold, bend, or turn away

steps.n: Added يَ prefix to show present tense action, with sukun on first root letter and kasra on second root letter

steps.m: they turn away (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in إِنَّهُمْ)
steps.n: Added ون to make it plural ('they' instead of 'he'). The ي changes to و before adding ون - this is a common pattern in Arabic verbs

steps.m: chest, breast (the place where things come forth from)

steps.n: The root ص-د-ر became plural 'chests' (صُدُور) and got a fatha ending because it's receiving the action of 'they fold'
Adding vowels to make basic past tense verb meaning 'he hid'
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to show someone doing something to themselves
Added وا at the end to show many people doing the action

steps.m: so that they may hide themselves
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose - why they're doing the action
This is a combination of the preposition مِنْ (from) and the pronoun هُ (him/it)
This letter gets attention, like saying 'hey!' to make someone listen
Adding لَا makes it stronger - like saying 'Listen up!' before telling important news

steps.m: to set a time or period

steps.n: Added vowels to make a noun meaning a specific time or period
Added fatha at the end to show this word describes when the next action (يَسْتَغْشُونَ - they cover themselves) happens
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to show seeking or wanting to do something to oneself

steps.m: they seek to cover themselves (subject of يَثْنُونَ)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. This word connects to يَثْنُونَ (they fold) in the ayah, showing the same group doing both actions.
The root meaning 'to clothe' became a simple noun meaning 'clothing' by adding the vowels 'a' and 'silent w'
Changed to broken plural form by adding ي and ا around the middle root letter

steps.m: their clothes (object of يَسْتَغْشُونَ - they cover)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show possession and fatha before it because it's the object of the verb يَسْتَغْشُونَ (they cover)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and adding ُ at the end. This word connects to what follows in the ayah - it tells us that Allah knows what they (the disbelievers) are concealing.
This is a connecting word that links two related actions. In this ayah, it connects 'they conceal' (يُسِرُّونَ) and 'they announce' (يُعْلِنُونَ), showing that Allah knows both types of actions.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added shadda to show the action is done with intensity
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowel marks
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Basic interrogative/relative pronoun meaning 'what'. Here it connects to the verb يُعْلِنُونَ (they reveal) to ask about what they reveal.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they make public (what they do, in contrast to يُسِرُّونَ 'what they conceal')
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word contrasts with يُسِرُّونَ (what they conceal) in the ayah.
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.

steps.m: indeed he/it (subject of يَعْلَمُ - he knows)
steps.n: Added the attached pronoun هُ (he/it) to show who the emphasis is about. This word connects to يَعْلَمُ (he knows) in the ayah, telling us who knows.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who knows everything completely (attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Changes from 'he knew' to 'all-knowing one' - a special form showing Allah knows everything completely

steps.m: All-Knowing (as an inherent quality of Allah)
steps.n: Adds two small dashes (tanween) above the word to show it's describing Allah's quality of being All-Knowing
The base word ذات is used to show possession or attributes, especially for feminine nouns. It's like saying 'the one with' or 'having' in English.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (الصُّدُورِ - the chests/hearts)

steps.m: with/by that which (is in الصُّدُورِ - the chests/hearts)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ at the beginning to show 'with' or 'by'. Together with the next word الصُّدُورِ, it means 'with what is in the hearts/chests'.
Connected the root letters together to make the basic singular word
Changes the vowel pattern to make it plural, showing there are many chests
Adds 'the' to specify these particular chests, and doubles the first letter because it comes after ال
Shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else (ذَاتِ) in the verse

steps.m: and what/not - a combination of 'and' plus a word that can mean 'what' when asking questions or 'not' when negating
steps.n: This is a compound particle made of و (and) plus ما (what/not). It's very common in the Quran.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects to the word دَآبَّةٍ (creature) to show what the verse is talking about.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Changed verb 'to move' into a noun meaning 'something that moves' by adding an alif after first letter and making it a feminine noun with ta marboota
The alif and hamza combined into a longer sound (madda) to show something that happens a lot
Added feminine ending to create noun for specific instance
Added genitive case ending because it comes after the preposition مِن
This is a basic preposition that shows location. In this ayah, it tells us where دَآبَّةٍ (creature) is located.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Added ال (written as ٱلْ) to make it specific
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in)

steps.m: except (shows that رِزْقُهَا 'its provision' is different from other things)
steps.n: This is a special word that shows something is different or excluded. In this ayah, it shows that Allah provides for every creature's provision without exception.
This is a basic preposition that shows something depends on or is the responsibility of something else. Here it shows that the provision (رِزْقُهَا) depends upon Allah (اللَّهِ).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال (the) and changed the word to become the unique name of the one true God. The kasra at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to provide with what is needed to live

steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Adds damma at the end to show this is the main word before something is attached to it

steps.m: its provision (referring to the creature دَآبَّةٍ mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Adds ها at the end to show this provision belongs to the creature (دَآبَّةٍ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past-tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show it's happening now. The ُ at the end shows he is doing the action.

steps.m: and he knows (connected to رِزْقُهَا - its provision)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this with the previous phrase about provision (رِزْقُهَا). Allah knows both the provision and the resting/dwelling places of creatures.

steps.m: to settle or remain fixed in place

steps.n: Doubling the ر makes the meaning stronger - really settled down
The مُسْتَ prefix and extra ّ (shadda) turn the verb 'settle' into a noun meaning 'place where something settles'
Added ها at the end to show this belongs to or relates to something feminine (referring back to دَآبَّةٍ 'creature' in the ayah)

steps.m: to store, deposit, leave something in safekeeping

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُسْتَ at the beginning to show this is a place where something is stored. The و gets stretched out because it's between two parts of the word

steps.m: its place of storing (referring back to دَآبَّةٍ 'creature')
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show this belongs to or relates to the creature mentioned earlier in دَآبَّةٍ
Added و at beginning to connect this word to مُسْتَقَرَّهَا (its place of rest) mentioned just before it
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
The word stays the same - it means 'all/every' and is the doer of an action

steps.m: in (connects كِتٰبٍ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects the word كِتٰبٍ (book/record) to what came before it, showing everything mentioned is recorded in this book.

steps.m: in (connects كِتٰبٍ to what came before)
steps.n: The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels and added alif to transform from an action to a thing. This pattern is used to make nouns from verbs.
The word got two small changes: 1) Added two dots under (ٍ) to show it comes after 'in', 2) Changed how the long 'aa' is written with a line on top (ٰ)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to kasra to show who/what does the action
Added shadda to emphasize the clarity
Added tanween kasra to show this describes the word كِتٰبٍ (book/record) in the ayah
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation: damma (ُ) on ه and fatha (َ) on و. This pronoun points to Allah in this context, as He is the one who created.
Added و with fatha (وَ) at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this statement to what came before it.
This is the base demonstrative word that points to someone or something. In this ayah, it connects to the verb خَلَقَ (created) to tell us who did the creating.

steps.m: The one who (connects to خَلَقَ - created)
steps.n: Adding ٱلَّ at the beginning makes this word point to something specific that was mentioned before (in this case, referring back to هُوَ - He).
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word describes what الَّذِى (the one who) did.
Adding fatha marks creates the basic past tense verb
Root word meaning 'to be high/elevated' became 'heavens' by making it a noun and adding -aat to make it plural
Adding ال makes it 'the heavens' instead of just 'heavens'
Changed to 'of the heavens' by adding kasra (-i) at the end to show it's the object being created

Added fatha marks and changed the middle letter و to ا to show this happened in the past

steps.m: to work, to act, to do deeds

Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed'

steps.m: and indeed if (connects to previous statement)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect to previous statement, and changed إ to ئ because it comes after لَ
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb

Changed from past tense 'he said' to a command meaning 'you say!' by changing the middle vowel from 'aa' to 'u'

Changed to passive participle showing who receives the action

steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word بَعْدِ (after) to the previous context, helping to say 'from after death'.

The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is possessed by the word الْمَوْتِ (death) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'after death'
Changed from the root meaning 'die' into the noun 'death' by adding the vowels َ (fatha) and ْ (sukun)
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to mean 'the'
Added ِ at the end because this word comes after the word بَعْدِ (after) in the phrase 'after death'

Adds لَ at beginning to show emphasis, like adding 'surely' in English

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form. This word shows who will be speaking in لَيَقُولَنَّ (they will surely say).

The ا elongates the final vowel sound for emphasis
This is a conditional particle that introduces what the disbelievers will say. In this ayah, it's part of a longer phrase meaning 'this is nothing but...'
Basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' - used to point to something nearby
Added small alif (ٰ) above ه as a pronunciation guide common in Quranic writing

steps.n: Changed final alif to alif madd (آ) - this is a common Quranic writing style that doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it points forward to what the disbelievers say next about the resurrection being 'obvious magic'.

steps.m: except (negates what comes before it in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that doesn't come from a root. It's made up of إِ (hamza with kasra) and لَّا (lam with shadda and alif). In this ayah, it's used to negate what was said before, showing that something is 'nothing except' what comes after.
The basic concept relates to supernatural or magical practices
Changed the verb into a noun by adding the vowels. This word is what the disbelievers are claiming about the message in this ayah.

steps.m: mere magic (something claimed by الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is an indefinite noun, meaning they're calling it just some magic rather than something divine.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The مُ prefix makes this an active participle - something or someone that makes things clear
The shadda adds emphasis to the meaning

steps.m: a clear one (describing سِحْرٌ/magic in this ayah)
steps.n: The tanween shows this word is describing the magic (سِحْرٌ) mentioned in the ayah
Starting with the basic conditional particle إِن that shows something depends on something else
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Changed إِ to ئِ because it comes after a letter with a vowel. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

This is a preposition that shows something moving away or being separated from something else
Added the pronoun هُمُ (them) to show who the action is moving away from. This word connects to مَصْرُوفًا (turned away) in the ayah to show what is being turned away from them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding alif after second root letter turns the verb into a noun meaning the result of the action
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific punishment rather than punishment in general
Adding fatha at the end shows this is what is being delayed in the phrase 'if We delay the punishment from them'
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. Here it connects 'punishment' (عَذَابَ) to 'a community' (أُمَّةٍ), showing the punishment is delayed until a certain time.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound at the end. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word to show the sound is stretched slightly. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Root أمم becomes plural أُمم (nations) by adding the damma marks
Changed from plural (nations) to singular (one nation) by removing the double م and adding ة at the end
Added tanwin kasra to show this community is being described by the word مَّعْدُودَةٍ (numbered/limited) that follows it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

Shows this word is describing أُمَّةٍ (period/time) in the ayah

The middle weak letter و changes to ا in this form
Changed to present/future tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لَ at beginning to add emphasis

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end

steps.m: holds it back (referring to العَذَابَ - the punishment)
steps.n: Added هُۥٓ at the end to show what is being held back. This word is connected to العَذَابَ (the punishment) mentioned earlier in the ayah, showing what is being held back.

steps.n: This word is used to get attention before an important statement. Here it introduces what the disbelievers will say about the delayed punishment. It's like saying 'Listen up!' or 'Pay attention!' before telling something important.

steps.m: day (object of يَأْتِيهِمْ - comes to them)

Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now. The ْ (sukun) and ِ (kasra) help with pronunciation.

steps.m: comes to them (referring to العَذَابَ/the punishment)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show who the action affects. This word is talking about how the punishment comes to them.
This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.

steps.m: to turn away or divert something
turned away/diverted (something that was acted upon)

steps.m: he went away (subject of لَيَقُولَنَّ - he will surely say)

Changed from verb 'to be happy' into describing word 'happy' by adding vowels and tanween

This is the basic form meaning 'who' - it's a special word that connects descriptions to the thing being described

Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people. In this ayah, it refers to the people who were patient (صَبَرُوا۟)

steps.m: to be patient or persevere through difficulty

steps.m: to do, to act, to work

Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action with being patient (صَبَرُوا۟). The word is followed by ٱلصَّالِحٰتِ which tells us they did righteous deeds.
Basic vowels added to make it mean 'he/it was good' in the past tense
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who has this quality
Makes it plural feminine to refer to good deeds/actions rather than people
Makes the word definite. The ص gets a shadda because it's a sun letter that assimilates with the ل of ال
Added kasra at the end because this word is the object of the verb عَمِلُوا (they did/performed)
This is the basic form of the word used to point to multiple people or things

steps.m: those (far away, referring to the patient believers mentioned before)

steps.m: and a reward (doer, described by كَبِيرٌ)
steps.n: Adding the tanween (double damma) makes it indefinite (like 'a' in English) and shows it's the subject. This word is described by كَبِيرٌ (great) which follows it.

The فَ at the beginning shows this is a result or consequence of something before it
The كَ at the end shows this is addressing 'you' (masculine singular)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under ر to show this describes someone who does the action

steps.m: a leaver/one who leaves (subject of لَعَلَّكَ)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is indefinite. This word describes what 'you' might be in the phrase 'perhaps you are...' (لَعَلَّكَ)

steps.m: to separate or divide something into parts

steps.n: This turns the idea of dividing into a word for 'a part' or 'some' of something

steps.m: some/part (of what is revealed to you)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of تَارِكٌ (leaving). It means you might be leaving some of what is being revealed to you.

Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb

Added alif maksura marker to show the long vowel sound at the end
Added a small mark to show this is where to pause when reading

Added ك at the end to show this is directed to 'you'. The ي is added between to make the connection smooth.
Basic meaning of narrowness or constriction

Added و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it

Added possessive pronoun كَ (your) and nominative case marker. The word صَدْرُكَ is the subject of ضَآئِقٌۢ (distressed/constrained) in this ayah.

This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces what they might say, connecting it to the previous part about what you might give up. It works like 'that' in English when reporting what someone says.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The middle weak letter و changes to ا in the past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action
Basic combination of لَوْ (if) and لَا (not) to create a wish or regret meaning
The final alif is elongated (made longer) to add emphasis to the desire or regret being expressed
Basic meaning of descending
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding أُ at start and changing vowels. This shows the action was done to something rather than something doing the action.
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun
Added هِ at the end to show 'him/it' - the thing being referred to

steps.m: to store or save up valuable things
steps.n: Basic meaning of storing wealth
Root letters combined with fatha marks to make a noun meaning 'treasure'
Adding tanween dammah to make the noun indefinite

steps.m: or (connecting to لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ كَنزٌ - 'why has not a treasure been sent down upon him')
steps.n: Basic conjunction particle meaning 'or' that connects alternative possibilities. Here it connects two things people might say: 'why hasn't a treasure been sent down' OR 'why hasn't an angel come with him'

steps.m: to arrive or come to a place
steps.n: Basic meaning of coming
Added fatha vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he arrived'
Changed to common hollow verb pattern with alif in middle
Added optional alif with madda (آ) which is just another way to write اء when they come together
Basic preposition meaning 'with' or 'along with'
Added pronoun هُ (him) to show who is being accompanied
Added small و mark (ۥ) after هُ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added fathas to create the noun pattern for beings with special qualities. Angels are beings with divine authority.
Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite (any angel) and is the subject doing the action of coming (جَآءَ) in this ayah.
Basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows

steps.m: only/but (introducing what follows in the ayah: أَنتَ نَذِيرٌ - you are a warner)
steps.n: Adding مَآ changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction/limitation. The elongated alif (آ) adds emphasis to this restriction.
This is a basic pronoun meaning 'you' (when talking to one male). In this ayah, it's connected to نَذِيرٌ (warner) - saying 'you are only a warner.'

steps.m: to warn or alert someone about danger
steps.n: Basic meaning of warning
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: warner (someone whose job is to warn others)
steps.n: Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to show this is a person who does the warning

steps.m: a warner (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this ayah)
steps.n: Added two dots on top at the end (tanween) to show this is 'a' warner rather than 'the' warner
This is a unique word that comes from the root meaning 'divine/deity' but has become the proper name for God in Arabic. The doubled ل (lam) with shadda makes it special.
The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Allah is the main subject doing something in this sentence.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it, like saying 'and'.
Basic form showing the concept of being physically or metaphorically higher
Adding ى changes it from a concept into a preposition that shows relationship between things

steps.m: upon/over (connecting to كُلِّ شَىْءٍ - all things)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) shows this is the final form. Here it connects to 'كُلِّ شَىْءٍ' (all things) to mean 'over all things'
Basic meaning of totality or completeness
Added damma and shadda to make it mean 'all' or 'every'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, making it mean 'of all things'
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added the genitive case ending ٍ to show this thing is owned or controlled by something else. The ي changes to ى but this doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to entrust or put in charge
steps.n: Basic meaning of trusting or relying
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed pattern to show someone who is trusted with responsibility. This word describes Allah in this ayah.

steps.m: or (connecting to previous statement in يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces a new possibility or alternative. Here it connects to what people are saying (يَقُولُونَ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و.
Added ونَ to show multiple males are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added ٱ at start and ت after first letter to show he did this action by himself

steps.m: he made it up (object of يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what was made up. This whole phrase is what 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ) in the verse.
Added damma on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it into a basic command form

steps.m: say! (a command from Allah ﷻ to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: The middle letter و is dropped because it's between a damma and sukun, which is a common rule for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)
Basic command form telling one person to bring something
Changes command from singular to plural by adding وا with damma before it
Adds فَ at beginning to show this command follows from what was said before

steps.m: to be ten or form a group of ten

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic number word
Added the preposition بِ which connects this number to the word سُوَرٍ (chapters) that follows it
Added kasra ending because this word comes after the preposition بِ and describes سُوَرٍ (chapters)
Changes to plural form by adding vowel marks
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the phrase 'with ten' (بِعَشْرِ)

steps.m: to be similar or equal to something

steps.n: Added vowels to make a noun meaning 'something similar'
The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to سُوَرٍ (chapters) in the ayah

steps.m: similar to it (referring to the Quran)
steps.n: Added هِ (meaning 'it') at the end and doubled the م at start to connect words smoothly
Added ۦ after هِ to help with pronunciation, showing the 'i' sound clearly. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding مُ at the start and ت after ف changes this from an action ('he made up') to describing something that was made up by someone. Like changing 'invent' to 'invented'
Adding ات makes it plural feminine, referring to multiple fabricated things (in this case, referring to the سور 'chapters' mentioned in the ayah)
The tanwin kasra shows this describes the سور (chapters) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to call out to someone or invite them

steps.n: Command form made by dropping the last letter and adding a stop mark (sukun) and 'oo' sound (damma). Like saying 'Call!' to one person.
Added helper alif at start to make pronunciation easier
Added و to connect this command with the previous phrase فَأْتُوا۟ (then bring)
Changed to plural command by adding وا at the end with a small circle above (shows it's plural). The initial اُ becomes ٱ when connecting.
This is a basic word that refers to people in questions or conditions. It's like saying 'who' or 'whoever' in English.
Added kasra at the end because it's connected to the next word اسْتَطَعْتُم (you were able). This helps the words flow together when reading.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or requesting the ability
The 'aa' sound (ا) was removed and the last letter got a stop mark (sukun) to show it's in jussive form
Added تُم at the end to show 'you all' did this action in the past
Basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This often happens when مِن comes after certain words or at the start of a phrase.

steps.m: to be below or less than something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'below/less than'
Added kasra (ِ) to show this word connects to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added ال and merged it with the word, doubling the ل with a shadda. This makes it a proper name for the One God.

steps.m: of Allah (possessed by/connected to مِن دُونِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the phrase مِن دُونِ (besides/other than)

This is a conditional particle that means 'if'. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if you are truthful' (كُنتُمْ صٰدِقِينَ), connecting it to the previous challenge to bring ten similar surahs.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
The middle weak letter و drops out, and vowels change to match the common pattern for this type of verb

steps.m: you all were (addressing multiple people in the past)
steps.n: Added تُمْ at the end to show this is talking about multiple people in the past
Basic past tense verb form - added short vowels (fatha) to make 'he told the truth'
Added alif after first letter and kasra after second letter to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to make it plural. The alif is written as a small mark above the ص. This word describes the subject of كُنتُم (you all were).
Starting with the basic conditional particle إِنْ that introduces conditions
Combining the words 'if' (إِنْ) and 'not' (لَمْ) into one word that means 'if not'

steps.m: And if not (connects to يَسْتَجِيبُوا۟ - they respond)
steps.n: The فَ (fa) was added at the start to mean 'then/so', connecting this condition to what happens next
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added سْتَ to show seeking or requesting the action
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
This is a basic particle that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: to/for you all (responding to يَسْتَجِيبُوا - they respond)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people. This word connects to يَسْتَجِيبُوا (they respond) in the ayah, showing who they are responding to.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he knew' to a command by adding alif at start and putting sukoon at end - like saying 'Know this!'
Added وا to make it a command to many people instead of just one
Added فَ at beginning to connect it with what came before, showing this is a result or consequence
Starting with the base particle مَا
Adding أَنَّ changes this into an emphatic particle that confirms what comes after it
The long alif at the end is for emphasis in Quranic recitation. This word introduces what was sent down (أُنزِلَ) in this verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive form to show the action was done to something rather than by something
Changed the verb (to know) into a noun (knowledge) by adding specific vowel patterns

steps.m: with knowledge (connected to أُنزِلَ - it was sent down)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show this knowledge is being used as a tool or means, and ِ at end to connect it to the next word (اللَّهِ). This word explains how something was sent down - with/by means of knowledge.
Added emphasis to middle letter to show the strength and uniqueness of this name
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word بِعِلْمِ (with knowledge), showing that the knowledge belongs to Allah
Combines وَ (and) with أَن (that)
This is the basic particle used for negation
Adding shadda makes the negation stronger and more emphatic
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer, but this doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changed the أ to إِ and added vowels to make it a noun meaning 'something that is worshipped'

steps.m: god/deity (object of لَّآ meaning 'there is no')
steps.n: Added alif with overbar (ٰ) and fatha at the end. This word is being negated by لَّآ (there is no) in this ayah.
This is the base form of the exceptive particle that means 'except'
Added shadda and alif to create emphasis, making the exception stronger and more definitive
This is a pronoun that refers back to someone already mentioned. In this ayah, it's the subject of مُّسْلِمُونَ (submitting/Muslim).
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. هَلْ is used to ask yes/no questions, like adding a question mark in English.

steps.m: so/then are? (asking if something follows from previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this question to the previous statement. It shows this question follows from what was said before.
Added م with damma (ُم) at the end to change from talking to one person to talking to many people

steps.m: to submit, surrender (especially to God's will)

steps.n: Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle makes this describe someone who does the action of submitting
The ُ at the end shows this word is in the subject position in the sentence

steps.m: those who submit completely to Allah (plural)
steps.n: Adding ون makes it plural (many people), and the doubled م (مّ) adds emphasis to their submission
This is a word that introduces someone we don't know specifically - like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it refers to the person who wants/desires (يُرِيدُ) something.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense. This word is the subject of يُرِيدُ (he wants) in this ayah.
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form
The middle letter و changes to ي and the vowels change to match present tense pattern

steps.m: he wants/desires (describes who wants الْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا - the worldly life)
steps.n: Added يُ at the beginning to show present tense and who is doing the action. This word tells us who wants 'the worldly life' mentioned next in the ayah.
Root letters ح-ي-ي became the basic verb 'to live' by adding the vowel mark 'a' sound between letters
Added و and ة to make it into a noun meaning 'life'
Added ال to make it specific ('the life') and َ at the end because it's the object of the verb يُرِيدُ (wants/desires)
Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning 'nearness'
Changed و to يا and added feminine ending to make the word mean 'this world/worldly life' (literally: the nearest life)
Added ال to make it definite. This word describes 'life' (ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ) in this ayah, showing what kind of life is being talked about.
Changed the word into a noun meaning the beauty or attractive things themselves (like jewelry, nice clothes, etc.)

steps.m: its adornment (referring to الْحَيَوٰةَ الدُّنْيَا - worldly life)
steps.n: Added ها which shows this adornment belongs to the worldly life mentioned earlier

steps.m: and its adornment (referring to الْحَيَوٰةَ الدُّنْيَا - worldly life)
steps.n: Added و (meaning 'and') to connect this to the previous phrase about worldly life
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Doubling of middle root letter indicates intensification of the basic meaning 'to fulfill'

steps.m: we fulfill completely (to them their deeds)
steps.n: Added نُ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. This verb connects to إِلَيْهِمْ أَعْمٰلَهُمْ (to them their deeds) in the ayah.
This is a preposition showing direction or destination
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for attaching the pronoun

steps.m: to them (receiving the actions in نُوَفِّ)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'them' (for a group). This word shows who receives the actions in نُوَفِّ (we will pay in full)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'

steps.m: their deeds (referring to those who desire worldly life mentioned in أَعْمٰلَهُمْ)
steps.n: Changed from 'to make work' to 'deeds' and added 'their' at the end

steps.m: in/within (preposition showing location in الْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it's talking about being in worldly life.

steps.m: in it (referring to الْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا - worldly life)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to 'worldly life' mentioned earlier in the ayah. This connects this word to what was talked about before.
This is a pronoun that refers to a group of males. It connects to the verb يُبْخَسُونَ (they are not decreased) in this ayah.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This shows these people are the same ones mentioned earlier who want worldly life.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. Since it's a particle, it doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: in it/her (referring to الْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا - worldly life)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to 'worldly life' mentioned earlier in the verse. This connects the preposition to what it's talking about.
This is a basic negative particle that negates the verb that follows it. Here it negates يُبْخَسُونَ (they are diminished) to mean 'they are not diminished'.
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation

steps.m: to decrease or reduce the value of something unfairly

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed to passive present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they are being reduced unfairly (subject of لا)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are having this done to them. This word comes after لا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they are not being reduced unfairly'
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun that points to multiple things or people
The small alif (ٓ) shows that we're pointing to something far away rather than near

steps.m: those (pointing to people far away) (subject of لَيْسَ)
steps.n: The ئِكَ ending makes it specifically point to people rather than things. This word is the subject of لَيْسَ (is not) in this ayah.
Added ال to make it specific, like changing 'who' to 'the one who'. The shadda (ّ) shows emphasis on the ذ.

steps.m: those who/which (referring to multiple people in أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ - 'those')
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people. This matches with أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ (those) which comes before it in the ayah.
Adding vowels to make it a usable past tense verb meaning 'is not'
This is a prefix that shows direction or purpose
Added هُمْ which means 'them' (for a group of men or mixed group)
This is a basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it shows what will happen to the disbelievers in the Hereafter.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding آ at the start and changing the middle vowel makes it mean 'last' or 'final' (like 'more late/later')
Added ة to make it feminine and specify 'the hereafter' (the life that comes after this world)
Added ٱلْ (the) at the start and ِ at the end to show this is the object of فِى (in) from earlier in the verse
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This is a particle that excludes something from what was mentioned before.
Added alif at the end to help connect this word to what comes after it (النَّارُ - the Fire). This is a common pattern in Arabic to make words flow together.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
The verb 'to shine' became the noun 'fire' by changing و to ا in the middle
Added shadda to emphasize the intensity of the fire
Added damma to show this is the subject
Added the definite article ال to specify this particular fire
This root describes things losing their value or becoming useless

steps.m: it became worthless (referring to مَا صَنَعُوا۟ - what they did)
steps.n: Added vowels to show this happened in the past. This word describes how their deeds (مَا صَنَعُوا۟) became worthless.

steps.m: and it became worthless (referring to مَا صَنَعُوا۟ - what they did)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before, showing this is another thing that happened to their deeds

This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of the sentence. Here it links 'became worthless' (حَبِطَ) with 'they made/did' (صَنَعُوا۟), showing that what became worthless was the thing that they made/did.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past

steps.m: they made/created (refers to what the disbelievers did)
steps.n: Added وا with ُ to show many people did this action. The small ۟ mark (called a high stop sign) helps with proper recitation.
Basic preposition meaning 'in'. In this ayah, it shows that something is inside or within something else.

steps.m: in it (referring to النَّارُ - the fire)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to النَّارُ (the fire) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows what is being referred to as 'in it'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed from verb 'became false' to noun meaning 'false thing'
Adds 'and' to connect this word to what came before it
Adds double damma to make the word indefinite

steps.m: that which (refers to what they made/did)
steps.n: Basic relative pronoun that connects to the verb صَنَعُوا (they made/did) to refer to the things they made/did

steps.m: that which (refers to what they made/did, with emphasis)
steps.n: Added emphasis by doubling the م with a shadda mark. This emphasizes the connection between what was done and its worthlessness mentioned in the verse.
Added fatha marks to connect the letters. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak middle letter - this makes it easier to pronounce while keeping the same meaning.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action. This word connects to يَعْمَلُونَ (they work/do) to tell us who was doing the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense form

steps.m: they work (referring to those who disbelieve in the ayah)
steps.n: The ون ending shows this refers to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to those who disbelieve and their actions.
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Added فَ to show this question follows from previous information

steps.m: Then is the one who...? (asking about someone)
steps.n: Added مَن to ask about a person or people. This introduces the main subject of the question.
Added fatha marks to show past tense and make it mean 'he was'

steps.m: he was (introduces a description in this ayah)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to pronounce.
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition that shows one thing is positioned above another

The small alif (ٰ) is added as a pronunciation guide to show the ى is pronounced as a long 'aa' sound. This word connects to بَيِّنَةٍ (clear evidence) in this ayah, showing someone is upon clear evidence.
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to make it an adjective meaning 'clear'
Added ة to make it a feminine noun meaning 'clear proof'
Added the two dots below (tanwin) to show it's 'a clear proof' after the word 'upon'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. It doesn't come from a root.
Added kasra under م to show it connects to the word after it. Here it connects to رَّبِّهِۦ (his Lord).
Added shadda on م to show it's pronounced strongly because it comes after a word ending in a vowel.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop

steps.n: Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete mastery
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a call/address to the Lord
Adding هِ (hi) at the end changes 'my Lord' to 'his Lord', and the doubled ر (r) shows it's connected to the word before it (مِن)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changes from past tense 'he recited' to present tense 'he recites' by adding يَ at the start and changing the ending vowels

steps.m: he recites it (acted on by شَاهِدٌ)
steps.n: Adds هُ to show what is being recited. This word is connected to شَاهِدٌ (a witness) who is doing the reciting.
Adds وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who witnesses (subject of يَتْلُوهُ/follows it)
steps.n: Changed to active participle form meaning 'one who witnesses'. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يتلو (follows/recites).
Added tanween dammah to make the word indefinite
Basic preposition showing source or origin
Added pronoun هُ at the end to show who is being referred to
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in a vowel sound. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or someone. Here it connects to قَبْلِهِۦ (before him/it) to show what came before.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This forms a noun that works like an adverb to tell when something happened
The ُ at the end shows this word is being used adverbially to tell when something happened
Added kasra to show this connects to the word after it
Added هِ to show this refers to 'him' or 'it'
Added small ي after هِ to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the first vowel to kasra and added alif between second and third letters to create a noun for the result of writing
Added a small diagonal line (damma) on top at the end to show this word is doing the action
This is a proper noun (name) that comes from Hebrew. It refers to the prophet Moses whose book (Torah) is mentioned just before this word in the ayah.

steps.m: to lead or be in front

steps.n: Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'one who leads'
Added ًا at the end to show this describes how the Book of Moses served - it served as a leader. Like saying 'I appointed him (as) a leader'.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters together

steps.m: a specific act of kindness and care (mercy)
steps.n: The ة at the end makes this a noun meaning one instance or type of mercy
Adds و (and) at the beginning
The ً (tanween) at the end shows this word is a direct object - something is receiving the mercy
This is the basic form of the word used to point to things. It's like saying 'those' in English
Added the ئِ to show we're pointing to something far away

steps.m: those (pointing to something far) (subject of يُؤْمِنُونَ)

Makes word feminine plural to refer to multiple righteous actions/deeds
Adds definite article and doubles the ص with shadda
Adds kasra at end to show this is the object of the verb وَعَمِلُوا (they did/performed)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding hamza and sukun changes the meaning to show entering into this state
Adding وا changes the subject from 'he' to 'they'

steps.m: and they became humble and submissive (connected to ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding و connects this action to the previous actions of believing and doing good deeds mentioned in the ayah
Basic preposition showing direction or destination
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is connecting to the next word رَبِّهِمْ (their Lord). This is showing the direction 'to their Lord'
Added basic vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: lord (one who nurtures and cares completely)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) emphasizes the complete and constant nature of the nurturing, making it describe someone who does this as their essential nature
Added kasra to show this word is connected to what comes after it

steps.m: their Lord (the one who nurtures and cares for them)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which shows this belongs to them (referring to those who believe and do good deeds mentioned earlier in the verse)
This is the base form that means 'those'
The alif shows this refers to multiple people or things
The ئِ shows these people or things are far away (not close by)

steps.m: those (plural, far away, referring to the believers mentioned before)
steps.n: The كَ at the end points back to something already mentioned - in this case, the believers who did good deeds

steps.m: to accompany or be close to someone

steps.n: Added the vowels 'a' and 'i' to make it mean 'he accompanied' in the past
Changed to show the person who does the action
Changed to plural form showing multiple companions
Changed to show these companions are the main doers in the sentence, with a longer 'aa' sound and 'u' ending
Adding the short 'a' vowels (fatha) turns the root letters into 'he covered' in the past tense
Added ة to make it a noun referring to a place that is covered/concealed, which came to mean 'garden' since gardens were typically enclosed spaces
Added kasra to show this word is connected to something else in the sentence

steps.m: the Paradise (referring to the eternal garden in the afterlife)
steps.n: Added the definite article to specify this is referring to Paradise specifically, not just any garden

steps.m: they/them (referring to أَصْحٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ - the companions of Paradise)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers back to 'the companions of Paradise' mentioned in the previous part of the verse. It shows who will be in Paradise forever.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'
Added ها at the end to refer back to الجنة (Paradise) which was mentioned earlier. Since الجنة is feminine, we use the feminine pronoun ها.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Added ون to show it's talking about multiple people. This word describes هم (they) in the sentence, telling us that they are the ones who will last forever in Paradise.

steps.m: to be like something or give an example of something

steps.n: Root letters م-ث-ل became a noun pattern مَثَل meaning 'example' or 'parable'
The U ending shows this word is the subject that starts the sentence. It's being compared to something else later.
The ي makes it mean 'a distinct group that is separated from others'

steps.m: two distinct groups (that are being compared in this example)
steps.n: The ين ending shows there are exactly two groups being discussed. This word follows مَثَلُ (example/likeness) which shows these groups are being compared.

steps.m: the two distinct groups (being compared: the blind/deaf versus the seeing/hearing)
steps.n: Adding ال specifies which two groups - referring to the groups mentioned right after: the blind/deaf group versus the seeing/hearing group
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed to adjective form describing someone who is blind
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific blind person or type of person

steps.m: like the blind one (compared to ٱلْبَصِيرِ/the seeing one)
steps.n: Adding كَ creates a comparison with the seeing person mentioned later in the verse

steps.m: to block or close up (especially referring to ears)

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled middle letter (shadda) to make 'he became deaf' from root meaning 'block/close'
Added hamza at start to make it into an adjective describing someone with this quality
Added و to connect this word with الْأَعْمَىٰ (the blind) that comes before it
Added ال to make it refer to a specific deaf person being compared in this example

steps.m: and the deaf one (paired with الْأَعْمَىٰ)
steps.n: Added kasra to match the case of الْأَعْمَىٰ that it's paired with
Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who does this action intensely
Adding ال (the) makes it specifically refer to Allah as The All-Seeing One

steps.m: and the one who sees/perceives (intensely) (contrasted with ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ)
steps.n: The letter و (meaning 'and') was added to the beginning to connect this word with ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ (the blind), showing a comparison between those who see and those who don't
Adding vowels (َ ِ َ) changes the root letters into 'he heard' in the past tense
Changed from past tense verb 'heard' to intensive doer form by adding ي, making it mean 'one who hears a lot'
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the س to show it's a divine attribute
Added و to connect with previous word (وَٱلْبَصِيرِ - and the All-Seeing)
Added kasra at the end to show it's connected to another word in the sentence
This word turns the statement 'they are equal' (يَسْتَوِيَانِ) into a yes/no question 'are they equal?' It's like adding a question mark at the start of an English sentence.
Basic root meaning showing the concept of equality or being level
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Added است at the start to show something gradually becoming level/straight/complete over time (like a plant growing straight up)
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing the ending vowel
Added انِ at the end to show two people/things (dual form) instead of one
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it a noun meaning 'example'

steps.m: an example (object of يَسْتَوِيَانِ/are they equal)
steps.n: Added tanween (ًا) to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, it answers the question 'equal in what way?' - they are equal in example.
Starting with the basic negation word لا
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement

steps.m: so will you not? (connected to تَذَكَّرُونَ - you remember)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to turn the statement into a question. This question is connected to the next word تَذَكَّرُونَ (you remember), asking 'will you not remember?'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled middle letter to show intense self-reflection
Added ون ending to show multiple people doing the action in present tense
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of this word that shows certainty
Added ل at the beginning to add extra emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at beginning makes the action more direct or causative, and changes first vowel to sukun
Adding نَا changes the subject to 'we' and shows this is what we did to نُوحًا (Noah)
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to show standard pronunciation of the name

steps.m: Noah (as the object of أَرْسَلْنَا - we sent)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) at the end to show Noah is receiving the action of being sent. This connects to the verb أَرْسَلْنَا (we sent) that comes before it in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects 'Nuh' to 'his people', showing he was sent to them.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands/gathers together'
Added kasra to show this word is connected to something after it
Added 'his' and the elongation mark to show possession. This word follows إِلَىٰ (to) in the phrase 'to his people'
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English

steps.m: indeed I (speaking to قَوْمِهِ - his people)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show 'I' am speaking. This connects to the next part where Nuh is speaking to his people.

steps.m: indeed I (speaking to قَوْمِهِ - his people)
steps.n: The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added ك with damma to show it refers to multiple people being addressed
Added م with sukun to complete the plural 'you' ending. This word shows who is being warned by نَذِيرٌ (warner) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: a warner (someone whose job is to warn others)
steps.n: Changed the word pattern to show someone who does this action as their role or job
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action. This describes the warner (نَذِيرٌ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added shadda on م for pronunciation after previous word ends with ل
This is a basic connecting word that introduces what someone says or does
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not'
The shadda (ّ) makes the word stronger. Here it's used with تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ (worship) to say 'do not worship'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added ت at start to show 'you', changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you (all) worship (connected to أَن لَّا meaning 'that not')
steps.n: Added وا with long alif and small و above to show plural 'you all'. This verb follows أَن لَّا which means 'that not', making the complete phrase mean 'that you all do not worship'
Starting with the two original words that combined to make this particle: إِنْ (if) and لَا (not)
The two words merged together and changed meaning. The ن and ل combined to make لّ (with shadda). This word connects to تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ (you worship) to mean 'worship none except...'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who is the object of يَعْبُدُوا/worship)
steps.n: The word for 'god' (ilah) became the special name 'Allah' by adding 'al' (the), merging letters, and making the 'l' stronger. The 'a' at the end shows Allah is receiving the action of worship
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to statements
Added ي at the end to show this refers to 'I'. This word connects to أَخَافُ (I fear) in this ayah, showing who is doing the fearing.
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing convention and doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle letter و changes to ا, which is common with this type of word.
Added أَ at the beginning to show 'I' and ُ at the end to show it's happening now
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ when adding a suffix
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to create a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is what is being feared in the phrase 'I fear for you'
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this word is indefinite (a day) and is possessed by something (in this case, it's part of the phrase 'punishment of a painful day')
Added vowel marks and ي to create an intensive adjective. This word describes the punishment (عَذَابَ) mentioned in the ayah, showing how severe it is.
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is describing the punishment mentioned earlier in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form before weak letter changes
The middle letter waw changed to alif to make it easier to say

steps.m: and then he said (after what came before in the story)
steps.n: Adding فَ at the beginning shows this happened as a result or sequence after something else in the story

steps.m: to gather, assemble (especially as nobles or leaders)

steps.n: Adding the vowel marks (fatha) turns the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he gathered'

steps.m: the assembly, the chiefs (subject of قَالَ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to a specific group of chiefs. This group is the subject (doer) of قَالَ (said) in this ayah.

steps.m: the assembly, the chiefs (subject of قَالَ)
steps.n: The ُ ending shows this word is doing the action in the sentence (saying/قَالَ)
This is the basic form used to point to someone or something we're describing
Added ال (the) at the start to make it mean 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Changed to plural form by adding ين at the end. In this ayah, it refers to the people who disbelieved.

steps.m: to reject, cover up, or deny truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. This word describes the action of those who spoke (قَالَ) in this verse.
Added وا to show multiple people (the chiefs/leaders) did this action together. The small mark after وا (۟) helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: from (connects قَوْمِهِۦ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a helper word that connects قَوْمِهِۦ (his people) to what came before it. It shows where something or someone comes from, like saying 'from his people'.
Root meaning 'stand' becomes noun for group of people who stand together as one
The kasra (ِ) shows this word belongs to something after it
Adds هِ to show it means 'his people'. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark. This whole phrase comes after مِن (from) in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb نَرَىٰكَ (we see you) into its opposite meaning 'we do not see you'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' and changed to present tense. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's at the end of the word
Added the alif (ٰ) mark and second person masculine singular pronoun كَ to show 'you' as the object being seen
