This is a fundamental particle that has no root. It connects the verb نَرَىٰكَ (we see you) with what is being seen بَشَرًا (a human), showing exclusion - 'we only see you as a human'.
Adding fathas creates a noun meaning 'human' since humans are creatures with visible skin/flesh

steps.m: a human being (object of نَرَىٰكَ - we see you)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being seen in the phrase 'we do not see you except as a human like us'
Adding the kasra (ِ) changes this from a verb root into a noun meaning 'similar to'

steps.m: like us (describes بَشَرًا - a human being)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to mean 'us'. This word describes بَشَرًا (a human being) in the sentence.
Combines 'and' (و) with the negative particle (ما)
Added vowels to connect the root letters together. This makes the basic past tense verb.
The word changes from 'he saw' to 'we see' by adding ن at the start. The ي at the end becomes ى because it's at the end - this is a common spelling change.
Added ك at the end to show who is being seen. The alif above the ى (ٰ) helps with pronunciation.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added ا at start and doubled the ت with shadda to show intensive following

steps.m: followed you (object of نَرَىٰ - we see)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show 'you' as the object being followed. This word is the object of نَرَىٰ (we see) in this ayah.

steps.m: except (connects to نَرَىٰكَ - we see you)
steps.n: This is a complete particle that doesn't derive from a root. It consists of hamza with kasra (إِ), lam with shadda (لّ), and alif (ا). In this ayah, it's used to limit what the disbelievers see - they say they only see Muhammad (SAW) as a human like them.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ٱلْ (the) makes it mean 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding ينَ makes it refer to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to the group who disbelieved (كَفَرُوا)

steps.m: they/them (referring to أَرَاذِلُنَا - our lowest people)
steps.n: This is a personal pronoun that refers to multiple people (all male or mixed group). In this ayah, it refers to أَرَاذِلُنَا (our lowest people) who followed Prophet Nuh.

steps.m: to be worthless or low in status

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added أ at start to show comparison (like 'more' or 'most' in English)
Changed to plural form to show multiple people
Added ُ to show this is doing something in the sentence
Added نَا at the end to show these belong to 'us'
This root relates to something becoming apparent or visible
Added short vowels (fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'he appeared' in the past
Changed from verb 'appeared' to describe someone/something that appears

steps.m: to see or have an opinion

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'opinion'
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ر with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by or connected to something else in the sentence
Basic particle ما with fatha added. In this ayah, it acts as a negation particle for the verb نَرَىٰ (we see).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects two similar statements about what the disbelievers could not see in the Prophet.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense. The word gets shorter because it's a special 'seeing' verb that drops some letters
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by replacing يَ with نَ at the start
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show the final long vowel sound
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
Added fatha to help with pronunciation

steps.m: for you all (object of نَرَىٰ - we see)
steps.n: The word 'for' (لَ) gets كم added to make it 'for you all' (لَكُمْ). Like adding 'you all' to show who something is for
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'over'. Since this isn't from a root, we start with the word itself.
The ى changes to يْ before adding a suffix - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning
Added نا at the end to show this applies to 'us'
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or belonging. Here it connects 'his people' (قَوْمِهِۦ) to the previous phrase, showing who the disbelievers were from.
Added fatha and sukun to make it into a noun that means the result of surpassing or being extra
Added the tanween kasra ending to show this is describing 'any favor' and is connected to the word مِن (from) in the ayah
Shows possession/ownership with the -in ending

steps.m: rather/but rather (introduces the disbelievers' real opinion about نَظُنُّكُمْ كٰذِبِينَ - we think you are liars)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a statement that corrects or contradicts what came before. Here it shows the disbelievers are moving from listing their observations to stating their real opinion.

steps.m: to think/believe something without being completely sure

steps.n: When two identical letters come together, they combine with a shadda (ّ)
Changes from 'he thought' to 'we think' - adds 'we' (نَ) at start and changes to present tense

steps.m: we think you all (without being sure) (subject of كٰذِبِينَ)
steps.n: Adds 'you all' as the ones being thought about. This word connects to كٰذِبِينَ (liars) - we think you all are liars.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added alif after first letter and changed middle vowel to kasra to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to make it plural. The word is the object of نَظُنُّ (we think), showing who they believe to be liars.
Added short vowels (fatha) to make the past tense verb meaning 'he said'
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is common for hollow verbs

steps.m: to stand upright, rise up, establish

steps.n: Changed root meaning into a noun for a group. The و with sukun (وْ) helps make it a noun.

steps.m: O my people! (being called by نوح)
steps.n: Added يٰ at start to call out and ِ at end to show it belongs to the speaker (نوح). This is how نوح addresses his people in this ayah.
Changed from basic root form to past tense by adding 'a' sounds (fatha) on first two letters and a silent 'y' at the end
Added أَ at the beginning to turn it into a question
Added تَ at the end to show you (masculine) are doing the action
Added تُمْ to show this is addressing multiple people. This word is connected to قَالَ (he said) in the verse.

This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it starts a sentence where Nuh is saying 'if I was upon clear proof...' - the word كُنتُ that follows completes the condition.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter و gets removed and vowels change to make the command 'be!'
Adding تُ at the end shows that 'I' did this action in the past
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. It has no root since it's a particle.
Added the superscript alif (ىٰ) which extends the sound at the end but doesn't change the meaning. This word connects to بَيِّنَةٍ (clear evidence) in the ayah.
Changed from a verb root (to make clear) into an adjective (clear) by doubling the middle letter with a shadda mark
Added ة to turn the adjective 'clear' into a noun meaning 'clear proof/evidence'

steps.m: a clear evidence (connected to مِّن رَّبِّى)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite and connects to 'from my Lord' in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. It's like saying 'from' or 'of' in English.
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a pause in speech. This makes it easier to pronounce clearly. It connects to the word رَّبِّى (my Lord) to show something comes from the Lord.

steps.m: to nurture, raise, take care of

steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) emphasizes the complete and constant nature of the nurturing
Adding kasra at the end makes it a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
Adding ى at the end shows possession - that this is MY Lord
Added shadda on first letter because this word comes after a word ending in a vowel (يٰ in يٰقَوْمِ)
Changed from basic 'come' root to Form IV verb meaning 'to give/bring something to someone'
Added 'and' to connect this word with what came before it
Added a long vowel ا after first letter to extend the sound
Added نِى at the end to show who received the action (me)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together and make it a noun

steps.m: a specific act of kindness and care
steps.n: Added ة to show we're talking about one specific instance of mercy

steps.m: a mercy (that Allah gave to Nuh)
steps.n: Added ً (tanween) at the end to show this is 'a' mercy, not 'the' mercy
This is a basic preposition that connects two words, showing where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it shows the mercy comes from the Lord.

steps.m: from (connecting رَحْمَةً with رَّبِّى, with emphasis)
steps.n: The letter ن merges with the next letter to make a stronger sound, showing the strong connection between mercy and its source
Added kasra and sukun to make it a preposition showing location or possession
Added kasra at the end because it's being possessed
Added هِۦ to show 'him/his', with the small ۦ mark emphasizing the 'him' meaning

steps.m: to be unclear or hidden from sight

steps.n: The root letters were connected with vowels to make a past tense verb meaning 'it became unclear/blind'
Changed to passive form by adding damma at start and doubling middle letter
Added ت at the end to show it's talking about something feminine (like 'she' or 'it' for feminine things)

steps.m: so it was made unclear (referring to the evidence/sign mentioned)
steps.n: Added فَ (fa) at beginning to show this happened as a result of something
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to a pronoun
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to

steps.m: to stick to, to be necessary

steps.n: Changes from base form to present tense 'we' do it form
The suffix كم means 'you all' as the object of the verb

steps.m: we compel you all (to accept) it
steps.n: The و connects two pronouns (you all + it), and ها means 'it'

steps.m: shall we compel you all to accept it?
steps.n: أَ makes it a question
This is a pronoun meaning 'you all' that refers to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, it's the subject (doer) of كٰرِهُونَ (dislikers/haters).
وَ means 'and'
This is a preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added هَا which refers to something feminine mentioned before (in this case, it refers back to the evidence/sign that was mentioned)
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb showing a feeling
Changed the word to describe someone who does the action of disliking
Added ending to show this describes multiple people. This word describes 'you all' (أَنتُمْ) in the verse.

steps.m: to stand together, establish, rise up

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect letters. The root meaning of standing together became associated with a group that stands as one.
Added يٰ to directly address the people being spoken to
Added kasra at end to show possession (my people)
This is a basic negative particle used to negate what follows it. In this ayah, it negates أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ (I ask you), making the meaning 'I do not ask you'.
Added fatha (َ) on the ل to show how it's pronounced.
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This doesn't change the meaning, just the pronunciation.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense first person (I) by adding أَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: I ask you all (object of asking in this ayah)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being asked - it means 'you all' and refers to the people Noah is speaking to
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun
Added هِ which means 'him'. In this ayah, it refers to the reward or payment being discussed
Added vowel marks and the weak letter و changed to ا, which is a common change in Arabic words

steps.m: wealth/money (as the object of أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ - I ask you)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of asking in the phrase 'I do not ask you for wealth'
This is a conditional particle that shows uncertainty or possibility. Here it introduces the condition for the reward (أَجْرِىَ) mentioned in the ayah.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added ي at the end to show possession (my)
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being asked about in the phrase 'I do not ask you for reward'
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, making the word stronger
When إِ is added to لَّا (no/not), it becomes إِلَّا (except/only) - like changing from 'not' to 'except'
This is a basic preposition showing something being on top of, based on, or dependent on something else. It's like saying 'on' or 'upon' in English.

steps.m: for (it) (referring to payment/reward mentioned in أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ مَالًا)
steps.n: In this ayah, عَلَى is used with the meaning of 'for' when talking about payment or reward. It connects to the phrase 'I do not ask you for wealth' (لَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ مَالًا), showing what the payment would be for.
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one supreme deity
The word changed to show 'of' or 'belonging to' by adding a kasra (small diagonal line) at the end
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation. This word can mean either 'what' (question word) or 'not' (negation) depending on context. Here it means 'not' because it's being used to deny something.
Added alif with madda (آ) to show the long 'aa' sound. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this phrase to the previous statement 'I do not ask you'. The full meaning is 'and I am not...'

steps.m: I (subject of أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ - I ask you)
steps.n: This is the basic personal pronoun meaning 'I'. In this ayah, it's being used to emphasize that the speaker himself is not asking for payment, connecting to the verb أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ (I ask you).
The small mark (۠) is called a superscript alif. It helps show the correct pronunciation and adds a slight emphasis to the word.
This is the basic meaning of the root letters
Changes from action (verb) to doer of action (noun)

steps.m: with/by one who drives away (connected to أَنَا۠ - I am)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with/by' and kasra at end because it's connected to another word
Basic relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things, like saying 'the one who' in English
Added ين to make it plural - now it means 'those who' for groups instead of just 'who' for one

steps.m: those who (referring to the believers mentioned after)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The ل combines with ذ to make a stronger ذ sound (shown by ّ). The believers (ءَامَنُوٓا۟) mentioned right after this word are the specific group being referred to.
Connected the root letters together

steps.m: to cause to be safe/secure, to believe
steps.n: Adding آ at start changes meaning to 'make something happen'
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word is the subject of مُّلٰقُوا۟ (they will meet) in the next part of the ayah.
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed'. This emphasizes what comes after it.
Added هُ (him/it) to show who is being talked about

steps.m: indeed they (referring to those who believed)
steps.n: Changed هُ (him/it) to هُم (them) to show we're talking about multiple people. This refers back to 'those who believed' (ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟) mentioned in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added مُ at start and alif after first root letter to show who does the action
Added و at end to make it plural (more than one person who meets)
Added ا after و to complete the plural form
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter to show intensity of meaning
Adding kasra at the end makes it a call/address to the Lord

steps.m: their Lord (referring to the believers mentioned in ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: The هِمْ shows this is talking about multiple people's Lord - specifically referring back to the believers mentioned earlier in the verse
Starting with the basic particle that shows contrast. This word connects different ideas by showing how they differ.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this contrast with the previous statement about not asking for payment
Added ي (ya) at the end to mean 'I', changing 'but' to 'but I'. Works with أَرَىٰكُمْ to say 'but I see you'
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to cause to see' or 'to show'
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif above) at the end to show this is a weak verb ending
Added كُمْ at the end to show who is being seen (you all)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the noun form meaning 'people'
Added tanween alif to show this word is the object being seen in the phrase 'I see you as a people'
This is the basic concept of being ignorant or lacking knowledge
Added vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb describing a state of being ignorant
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the beginning
Added ونَ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed

steps.m: to stand together, gather, rise up

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The root meaning of standing/gathering together became associated with a group of people who gather as one.
The يٰ with alif above it (يٰ) is added at the start to call out to someone, like saying 'O!' in English

steps.m: And O my people! (connecting to previous statement)
steps.n: Adding وَ (and) to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: And O my people! (in possessive/genitive case)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end is how we call out to 'my people' in Arabic - it's like saying 'Hey, my people!'
This is a question word asking about people. In this ayah, it's used by Prophet Nuh to ask who would help/protect him, connecting to the verb يَنصُرُنِى (will help me) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Prefix ي indicates 3rd person masculine singular present tense
The ني suffix shows that 'I' am the object receiving the help/support
This is a basic word that shows something comes from somewhere else or belongs to something. In this ayah, it connects 'who will help me' with 'Allah', showing who the help would be from.
Added َ (fatha) at the end to connect smoothly with the next word اللَّهِ (Allah). This happens when مِن comes before ال.
Added shadda on ل to emphasize that this is the one and only God
The word 'Allah' gets a special mark (ٱ) at the start and an 'i' sound (-ِ) at the end because it comes after the word 'from' (مِنَ)

steps.m: if (introduces condition for طَرَدتُّهُمْ - 'I were to drive them away')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it connects two ideas: 'who would help me against Allah' and 'if I were to drive them away.' It shows that the first part depends on the second part happening.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was driven away (them)
The ت and د merged into تّ (with a shadda), but the meaning stays the same. هُمْ was already there in both words.
This letter turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Added فَ to show this question follows from what was said before

steps.m: then will you not? (connects to تَذَكَّرُونَ - you remember)
steps.n: Added لا to make the question negative. This word connects to تَذَكَّرُونَ (you remember) in the ayah, asking 'Then will you not remember?'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show doing the action more strongly or repeatedly
Adding تَ at the beginning shows doing the action to oneself - changing from reminding others to remembering oneself
Changed from past tense (he remembered) to present tense (remembers) by changing the ending vowel
Adding ونَ shows that multiple people are doing the action and changes it to addressing them directly (you all)
This is the basic negative particle لا that means 'not'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added fatha on لَ and changed ا to آ for emphasis in pronunciation. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed from past tense (he said) to present tense (say) by changing the vowels. The middle letter و that was hidden in قَالَ comes back in قُولُ
Added أَ at the beginning to show 'I' am doing the action
Added fatha to show this is connected to what follows

steps.m: to/for you all (object of أَقُولُ - I say)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed to multiple people being addressed
This is a common preposition in Arabic that comes from the idea of being present with something
The root letters get vowel marks (ـِ and ـْ) to make it a preposition meaning 'at' or 'with'

steps.m: near to, at (ready to connect to something)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show it connects to what comes next

steps.m: with me, in my possession (object of أَقُولُ)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this is talking about what I have or what's with me. This word is the object of أَقُولُ (I say) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes the word into a broken plural form showing multiple treasures or storehouses
The word gets a damma (ُ) at the end to show it's doing the action. It's connected to عِندِى (meaning 'I have') in this sentence.
Changed to the unique name for The One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show possession or ownership. In this ayah, it shows Allah is the owner of the treasures mentioned before.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Extended the alif to show emphasis in pronunciation

steps.m: and not (connected to أَقُولُ - I say)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. This connects to the verb أَقُولُ (I say) in the ayah.
Adding the vowels 'a' and 'i' turns the root into 'he knew' in the past tense
Added أَ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative/superlative form, meaning 'more/most knowing'
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun referring to something hidden
Added ال to specify we're talking about a particular unseen thing
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb أَعْلَمُ (I know)
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added alif with madda to show long vowel sound

steps.m: and not (connected to أَقُولُ - I say)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this negation to the verb أَقُولُ (I say) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed vowels to make present tense. The middle و combines with the vowels to make a longer sound.
Added أَ at the beginning to show 'I' am doing the action
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English
Added ى at the end to show 'I' am speaking. In this ayah, a prophet is speaking to Allah.
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding fathas, making it mean 'angel' - a special being created by God to carry out His commands
Added tanween damma to show this is indefinite - meaning 'an angel' rather than 'the angel'
This is a negative particle that negates the verb أَقُولُ (I say) that comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous statement, showing these are parts of a list of things being denied
Added the elongation mark (آ) to show the extended pronunciation of the final vowel
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a 'hollow' verb.
Changed from 'he said' to 'I say' by adding أ at start and changing middle vowel to show present tense
Added ال to make it specific. The middle letter gets a shadda to show emphasis.
Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than one)
Added لِ at the beginning to show purpose or direction - like saying 'for' or 'to'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to 'looking down upon' by adding ا at start and adding د after ز (for easier pronunciation when used with ا)
Added تَ prefix to show present tense for 'she' or 'you'

steps.m: she looks down upon (your eyes أَعْيُنُكُمْ)
steps.n: Added elongation mark on final ي to show proper pronunciation. This verb refers to how 'your eyes' (أَعْيُنُكُمْ) look down upon others.

steps.m: eye, to see with the eye

steps.n: Forms the basic singular noun meaning 'eye'
Changes from singular 'eye' to plural 'eyes' by using the pattern أَفْعُل, a common way to make plurals in Arabic

steps.m: your (all of you) eyes (subject of تَزْدَرِى - looking down upon)
steps.n: Adds كُمْ at the end to show the eyes belong to multiple people being spoken to. These eyes are doing the action of looking down upon others.
This is a negative particle that makes something impossible in the future. In this ayah, it negates Allah giving them something good.
Changed to Form IV (causative) to show making something come/giving something to someone
Present tense form of 'to give' - starts with يُ (yu) to show it's happening now. The middle letter changes from ا to ؤ.

steps.m: he gives them (object of لَن in this ayah)
steps.n: The هُمُ suffix means 'them' for plural masculine. The fatha (َ) connects the pronoun to the verb. This word is the object of لَن (will not) in this ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Root letters connected with vowels to make the word meaning 'good' or 'better'

steps.m: any good (object of يُؤْتِيَهُمُ - give them)
steps.n: The ًا shows this word is receiving the action of giving in the phrase 'Allah will not give them any good'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
The ُ at the end shows this is in the nominative case
This is a combination of the preposition بِ (with/by) and the relative pronoun ما (what)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it shows that something is located within their souls/selves.
Added an alif mark (ٓ) after ى to show proper pronunciation when continuing to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be precious, valuable, or essential
steps.n: The root relates to the essence or self of something
Root letters ن-ف-س got vowels (َ and ْ) to make the common noun meaning 'self/soul'
Changed from single soul/self to plural form (like changing 'self' to 'selves')
Added kasra to show possession
Added pronoun suffix meaning 'their'
This is a basic emphasis particle that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English
Added ي at the end to show this is about 'me'. This word is connected to أَقُولُ (I say) in this ayah, showing what is being said.
Changed the writing style of ي to ى with a small alif (ٓ) on top. This doesn't change the meaning, it's just a different way of writing the same sound.
This is a basic particle that shows time or consequence

steps.m: therefore/in that case (shows consequence of لَن يُؤْتِيَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ خَيْرًا)
steps.n: Adding ًا makes it specifically show a result or consequence. Here it shows the consequence of saying 'Allah will not give them any good'
Starting with the basic preposition 'from' and adding a connecting vowel to help it flow with the next word
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
The ل gets a shadda because it combines with another ل from the previous word. This word connects to الظَّالِمِينَ (the wrongdoers) to say 'surely from the wrongdoers'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
This pattern shows who does the action, like changing 'wrong' to 'wrongdoer'
Adding ين makes it plural (three or more)
Adding ال makes it specific, like saying 'the' in English. The shadda (ّ) appears because ظ is a sun letter.

steps.m: he said (subject of يٰنُوحُ قَدْ جٰدَلْتَنَا)
steps.n: Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The و from the root changes to ا in this word. This word shows what happened before Noah's people said 'O Noah, you have argued with us'

steps.m: they said (the people speaking to Noah)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people (Noah's people) did the action together
Added final damma to show this is the subject form of the name
Added يٰ at the beginning to show direct address - like saying 'Hey Noah!' or 'O Noah!'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to the past tense verb that follows it (جٰدَلْتَنَا meaning 'you argued with us'). It shows that something definitely happened.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on ق and sukun (ْ) on د
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif to show the arguing goes both ways - like when two people are debating with each other
Added تَ to show 'you' did the action instead of 'he'
Added نَا at the end to show who was argued with (us), and changed the alif to a long alif (ٰ) for proper writing
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added hamza at start to show causing something to happen
Added ta at end to show 'you' did the action

steps.m: and you made (something) increase (in arguing with نُوحُ)
steps.n: Added fa at start to connect with previous statement about arguing with Noah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a noun that means 'the act of arguing' by adding alif after second root letter

steps.m: our arguing (object of أَكْثَرْتَ - you increased)
steps.n: Adds first person plural possessive suffix. This word is the direct object (what is being increased) of the verb فَأَكْثَرْتَ which means 'you have increased'.
Root letters أ-ت-ي were joined together with vowel marks to make a present tense verb meaning 'coming'
Changed from 'coming' to a command by dropping the last letter ي
Added نَا to show who the action is directed towards
Added فَ to show this action follows from what was said before (they had been arguing with Noah)
Starting with the basic relative pronoun ما which means 'what'
Added the preposition بِ (with) to show how something is done. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَعِدُ (he promises) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by: 1) Adding ت at start to show 'you', 2) Changing middle vowel to kasra, 3) Adding damma at end, 4) Dropping و because it's weak
Added نَآ at the end to show who receives the promise (us)
This is a basic particle that introduces conditional statements. In this ayah, it connects the condition 'if you are truthful' to what was mentioned before.
Added kasra (ِ) under the إ to show correct pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters. The ُ shows it's a noun form.
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did this in the past. The و is dropped because it's a weak letter. This is addressing Prophet Noah in response to his claims.
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الصَّادِقِينَ (the truthful ones). This combination means 'from the truthful ones'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra after second letter to make it describe someone who does the action
Added ين to show multiple people. The word comes after مِنَ (from/of) which requires this ending.
Added ال to make it specific and shadda on ص because it comes after ال. The word is part of the phrase مِنَ ٱلصَّادِقِينَ (from the truthful ones).

steps.m: he said (subject of يَأْتِيكُم - he brings to you)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it past tense and show who did the action. This word is connected to what comes next - it introduces what someone said.
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ by replacing the weak letter و with ا - this is a common change with words containing و
This is an emphatic particle that adds emphasis to what follows
Adding مَا changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction, showing that the following action (Allah bringing something) is the only possibility
Connected the root letters together
Added يَ at the start and changed vowels to show 'he' does the action now or in future
Added كُم at the end to show the action is directed towards multiple people being addressed
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is the preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'

steps.m: with it/him (referring to what اللَّهُ brings)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show what/who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to whatever Allah brings.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject

steps.m: if (introduces condition for شَآءَ - He wills)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces the condition 'if He wills'. It connects two ideas - showing that the second part depends on the first part happening.
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) to the root letters to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past
The weak letter ي changes to ا and combines with the added ا
Added fatha to show the basic pronunciation of the negation particle
Added alif with madda to show lengthened pronunciation
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is the basic form of the pronoun used when talking to one male person
Adding م at the end makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Adding مُ at the start and ِ in the middle makes it describe someone who does the action. This pattern is common for making doer nouns.
The ين ending makes it plural (more than two). This word describes what 'you all' are not in the sentence.

steps.m: able to make (something) impossible (as a negative with أنتم)
steps.n: The بِ here combines with the negative to mean 'not able to'. This is a common Arabic expression using بِ with negatives to show inability.

steps.m: and not - joins two ideas and makes the second one negative
steps.n: This is made up of two parts: و meaning 'and' plus لا meaning 'not'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The verb is changed to show it's happening now or in the future

steps.m: he helps/benefits you all (in response to نُصْحِىٓ - my advice)
steps.n: This word shows who receives Nuh's advice - it's connected to the word نُصْحِىٓ (my advice) in the ayah
Changed vowel pattern to make it a noun meaning 'advice' instead of a verb
Added ي at the end to show the advice belongs to me
Changed the regular ي to ى with small alif above to show possession

This is a conditional particle that shows the advice (نُصْحِىٓ) being helpful depends on Allah willing to guide them. It connects two parts: the condition (if Allah wants to misguide you) and the result (my advice won't help).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start changes 'want' to 'intend/aim for' something
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did it in the past. The long ا is dropped because it's followed by two consonants.
The ت and د merged together with a shadda since they are similar sounds next to each other

steps.m: that (connects أَرَدتُّ 'I wanted' to أَنصَحَ 'to advise')
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links two verbs together. Here it connects 'I wanted' with 'to advise', showing that advising is what was wanted. It's like saying 'I wanted to advise you' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose

steps.m: to/for you all (object of أَنصَحَ - I advise)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed to multiple people. This word shows who is being advised in the phrase 'I advise you all'.

steps.m: if (introduces the condition 'if I wanted' before 'my advice would not benefit you')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that connects two parts: the condition (if I wanted to advise you) and the result (my advice would not benefit you). It shows that the second part depends on the first part happening.
Adding the vowels 'fatha' (َ) turns the root letters into 'he was/existed' in the past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of يُرِيدُ (wants/wills) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Added يُ at start to show present tense, and changed vowel on middle letter to kasra
The middle letter و changes to ي, which is a common change in Arabic verbs. The word gets a damma (u sound) at the end to show it's doing the action of wanting.

This particle connects the previous verb يُرِيدُ (He wants) to the next clause يُغْوِيَكُمْ (to lead you astray), showing what Allah wants to do. It works like 'to' in English when we say 'He wants to do something'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he misleads you all (object of يُرِيدُ - wants to)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being misled. This word is what Allah wants (يُرِيدُ) to do in this ayah.

steps.m: He (referring to Allah in this context)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it refers back to Allah mentioned in the previous verse, emphasizing that He is your Lord.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping

steps.m: the one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: Adding ُ shows this is the main subject being talked about

steps.m: your caring Lord (the one being discussed in يُرِيدُ)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this Lord belongs to or is connected to all of you. This word is being discussed in the verb يُرِيدُ (he wants/wills)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for adding the next part
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'to him'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This phrase tells where you will return in the next word تُرْجَعُونَ (you will be returned)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from active 'he returned' to passive 'you are returned' - adds 'tu' at start to show someone else is doing the returning
Adds masculine plural suffix to indicate multiple addressees

steps.m: or (asking a question to challenge what was said before)
steps.n: This is a conjunction particle that connects two parts of a question or shows alternatives
Added vowels to connect the root letters. The middle و changes to ا in this form.
Added يَ at the beginning and changed vowels to show present tense. The middle letter returns to و.
Added ونَ to show multiple people (they) are doing the action. This word comes after أَمْ (or) in the ayah.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Form VIII verb pattern showing someone doing something deliberately for themselves

steps.m: he fabricated it (object of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Adding هُ at the end shows what was fabricated. This whole phrase is what 'they say' in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: say! (as a response to يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Changed to command form by adding damma on first letter and sukun on last letter. The middle weak letter و is dropped in this form. This word is used as a direct command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to respond to what 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ) in the ayah.
Basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it starts the phrase 'if I have invented it' in response to their claim.
Added kasra at the end because it's followed by a word starting with a connecting hamza
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added اِفْتَ to show he did the action to himself - means he deliberately made something up

steps.m: I fabricated it (responding to their claim in أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ)
steps.n: Adds هُ (hu) at the end to mean 'it', showing what was fabricated. Responds to their claim mentioned earlier in the verse.
Starting with the basic preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this follows from the previous statement about 'if I made it up' in the ayah

steps.m: then upon me (connected to إِجْرَامِى/my crime)
steps.n: Adding يَّ with shadda at the end shows this affects 'me' specifically. This connects to the word إِجْرَامِى (my crime) that follows it in the ayah.

steps.m: to do wrong or commit a crime

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun that represents the action itself
Added ى at the end to show possession (my)
Basic personal pronoun meaning 'I'. This pronoun is the subject of بَرِىٓءٌ (innocent/free) in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it
Added small alif (۠) above the final alif. This is just a writing mark to show this is the pronoun 'I' and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be free from blame or responsibility

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make a past tense verb meaning 'he was free/innocent'
Added ي to show this describes someone who has this quality

steps.m: innocent, free (from what تُجْرِمُونَ you commit of crimes)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this is the main subject. The ى and ٓ are just spelling variations of ي.
Combined the two basic words مِن (from) and مَا (what)
The ن changes into another م with a shadda (ّ) because it's easier to say. This is like how 'in memory' sounds like 'im-memory' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding ت changes it from 'he committed' to 'you commit'
Changed from 'you' (singular) to 'you all' (plural) by adding ون at the end and changing vowels
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to passive voice to show the revelation was received rather than given
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it to previous statement. The revelation was specifically given to Noah.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects 'was revealed' (أُوحِىَ) to 'Noah' (نُوحٍ), showing that the revelation was directed to Noah.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic form of the prophet's name Noah
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end because the word follows إِلَىٰ (to). This shows Noah is receiving the action.
Adding shadda makes the word stronger and adds emphasis
Added هُ at the end to show who the word is talking about. This connects to the verb يُؤْمِنَ (will believe) in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يُؤْمِنَ - 'believe') impossible in the future. In this ayah, it's saying that certain people will definitely not believe.

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure

steps.n: Adding vowels to make a basic past tense verb expressing a feeling of security
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels - shows ongoing belief/trust
The أ changes to ؤ after ُ - this is just a spelling rule, not a meaning change

steps.m: from (connecting قَوْمِكَ to the previous phrase)
steps.n: This is a preposition that connects two parts of the sentence, showing that something comes from or originates from something else. Here it connects 'your people' (قَوْمِكَ) to the previous statement about who will believe.
Connected the root letters with vowels to form a noun meaning a group of people who stand/gather together
Adds an 'i' sound at the end to show possession or belonging to

steps.m: your people (connected to مِن/from in this verse)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show these are 'your' people. This word follows مِن (from) in the verse.
This is a basic particle used to exclude something from what was mentioned before. Not derived from a root.

steps.m: except (excluding those who already believed from نُوحٍ's people)
steps.n: The shadda and alif are added to give emphasis to the exclusion. In this ayah, it's excluding the believers from Noah's people who were mentioned before.
This is a special word that refers to people in a general way. In this ayah, it refers to those who have already believed, as shown by the following phrase قَدْ ءَامَنَ (have already believed).
This particle is used to emphasize that something has definitely happened in the past. Here it emphasizes that some people have already believed (ءَامَنَ).
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to make the meaning stronger and more specific to faith/belief
Starting with the connecting particle that shows what follows is a result

steps.m: so not (connected to يُؤْمِنَ - will believe)
steps.n: Added the negation particle لا to show this is a negative result. This negates the verb يُؤْمِنَ (will believe) in the previous part of the ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to present tense addressing 'you' by adding تَ at start and adjusting vowels
Added ت and changed vowels to make it mean 'don't be sad' instead of just 'feel sad'. Like telling Noah 'don't make yourself upset' about his people not believing.
The basic preposition ب shows how something is done or what it's connected to
Added kasra to help pronunciation
Added ما which means 'what' to show this connects to the action they were doing. This combination بِمَا often introduces the reason or way something happened.
Added fathas to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Added وا at the end to change 'he was' to 'they were'. This word is connected to يَفْعَلُونَ (they do) in the ayah, showing what they were doing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they do/are doing (referring to the disbelievers in نُوحٍ's people)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This refers to the actions of the disbelievers mentioned in the previous part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he made' to 'make!' by removing the last vowel and adding alif at the start - this turns it into a command to one person

steps.m: and make! (command to Noah to build the ark)
steps.n: Added و (and) at start and ِ at end to connect it to the next word in the ayah (الْفُلْكَ - the ark)

steps.m: to orbit, rotate, or move in a circular path
ship (because it moves in paths through water)
steps.n: The root letters that mean 'circular motion' become a noun meaning 'ship' by adding the vowel marks ُ and ْ
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific - referring to the particular ship Noah was commanded to build

steps.m: the ship (object of وَٱصْنَعِ - and construct)
steps.n: The َ shows this word is being acted upon - it's what Noah is commanded to construct in the phrase 'and construct the ship'
Root letters joined with vowels to make the simple noun meaning 'eye'
Changes singular 'eye' to plural form for small number of eyes
Added نَا at the end to show these are 'our' eyes

steps.m: under our watchful eyes (watching over the construction of the ark)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to show this is about being under Allah's watchful care while building the ark
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the revelation itself
Added نَا at the end to show it belongs to us
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
This is a negative particle that tells someone not to do something. Here it's telling Noah not to speak about something.
Adding و connects this command to the previous command to build the ark (وَٱصْنَعِ). It shows these are related instructions given to Noah.

steps.m: to speak to or address someone

steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show interaction between two parties
Added sukun at end to make it jussive mood

steps.m: you speak to me (don't speak to me!)
steps.n: Added نِى at the end to show 'me' as object. The alif is written as ٰ (dagger alif) in this form. This is a prohibition (don't!) in context with لا before it in the ayah.
Basic preposition that shows location or being inside something
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how it's pronounced
Changed the final ي to ى which is just a writing style variation - doesn't change meaning or pronunciation
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to things. Changed to the form used for connecting descriptions.
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ال to make it specific and definite
Added proper pronunciation marks and connected to the verb that follows it (ظَلَمُوٓا۟) which describes what these people did

steps.m: to do wrong or act unfairly to someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word refers to the wrongdoers who will be drowned (مُّغْرَقُونَ) in the flood.
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action together. These are the people Noah was building the ark to save others from.
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger to show extra emphasis.
Added هُم at the end to show who is being talked about - in this case 'they' refers to the wrongdoers who will be drowned. This connects to the next word مُّغْرَقُونَ (drowned).

steps.m: to sink or submerge in water

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added مُ at start to make it passive (something done to someone), changing it from 'he sank' to 'being drowned'
Added ون to show we're talking about multiple people
Added a small mark (َ) at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this is happening in the present. The ُ at the end shows 'he' is doing it.
Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement. This word refers to Noah building the ark.

steps.m: to orbit, circle, or move in a curved path

steps.n: Changes from verb meaning 'to orbit' into noun meaning 'ship'
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to show this is talking about a specific ship - the one Noah was building.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object (the thing being built) in the sentence.
Forms noun meaning 'all' or 'every' by adding damma and shadda
Adds conjunction to connect with previous phrase
Adds fatha to show this word is being acted upon
Adding ما changes meaning from 'all' to 'whenever', connecting to the verb مَرَّ (passed by)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it past tense
The doubled ر with shadda shows emphasis. This word connects to عَلَيْهِ (upon him) in the ayah to show who was passed by.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns

steps.m: upon him (object of يَصْنَعُ - he builds)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to mean 'him'. This shows who is being built upon in the phrase 'he builds upon him'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form a noun meaning 'group'

steps.m: chiefs, nobles (subject of سَخِرُوا - they mocked)
steps.n: Added two dots (tanween) to show this is a complete subject/noun - these are the chiefs who did the mocking
This is a basic particle that shows something comes from or originates from something else
The shadda (ّ) appears because this word connects to the next word قَوْمِهِۦ (his people). It shows a strong connection between 'from' and 'his people'
Changed root meaning from an action (standing) to a group of people who stand together as a community
Adding kasra shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Adding هِ ('his') at the end shows who the people belong to. The small mark ۦ is just for proper pronunciation.

steps.m: to mock or make fun of
he made fun (of مِنْهُ - of him)
steps.n: Added vowels to make a past tense verb. In this ayah, this mocking is directed at someone specific (مِنْهُ - of him).

steps.m: they made fun (of مِنْهُ - of him)
steps.n: Added وا to show many people did this action together. The small mark (۟) above وا is just to help with pronunciation.
Added sukun to show the end of the word part meaning 'from'

steps.m: from him (who is being mocked in سَخِرُوا)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who is being referred to. This connects to the word سَخِرُوا (they mocked) in the ayah, showing who was being mocked.
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is an intermediate step before the weak letter changes.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
This is a conditional word that starts an if-then statement. Here it introduces 'if you mock us' as a condition.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense with تَ prefix and adjusted vowels

steps.m: you all mock (in response to سَخِرُوا۟ - they mocked)
steps.n: Added plural marker وا to show multiple people are doing the action. This word appears in response to سَخِرُوا۟ (they mocked) earlier in the verse.
This is a preposition that shows something coming from or originating from something else
Added نا at the end to show this is happening to 'us'. The ن gets doubled (with shadda) when combining with نا because they are similar sounds meeting each other.
This is the basic emphatic particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what happened before
Added نا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word connects to نَسْخَرُ (we mock) in the next part of the verse.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and show this happened in the past
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed the vowels to show it's happening now
Added ُ at the end because it's a present tense verb showing what 'we' are doing
Basic preposition meaning 'from'

steps.m: from you all (who are mocking نوح)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this is happening to multiple people being spoken to. In this ayah, it refers to the people who were mocking Prophet Nuh while he built the ark.
This is a comparison particle that shows something is similar to something else

steps.m: just as (referring to how they mock in تَسْخَرُونَ)
steps.n: Adding ما makes the comparison more specific and complete. Here it connects to how they mock (تَسْخَرُونَ) in the next part of the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense and second person (you) by adding تَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you all mock (referring to multiple people)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show that multiple people are doing the action
This is a basic particle that shows something will happen in the future. Added vowel marks to show how it's pronounced.

steps.m: then soon/will (connects to تَعْلَمُونَ - you will know)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of something before it. It connects this future action to what was mentioned earlier.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The prefix تَ changes it from 'he knew' to 'you know', and makes it happen in the present/future

steps.m: you (all) will know (referring to the disbelievers who will soon know about the punishment mentioned in مَن يَأْتِيهِ عَذَابٌ)
steps.n: Changed from 'you know' (singular) to 'you all will know' by adding ون at the end. The word سَوْفَ before it makes it about the future.

steps.m: whoever (subject of يَأْتِيهِ - comes to him)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun meaning 'whoever' that introduces a conditional clause. In this ayah, it refers to the person who will receive the punishment mentioned after.
Connected the root letters together
Added يَ at the start to show 'he' is doing the action in present time

steps.m: comes to him (referring to عَذَابٌ - punishment)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show the object (him) who receives the action. This refers back to the person who will receive the punishment (عَذَابٌ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and make a past tense verb

steps.m: punishment (subject of يَأْتِيهِ - comes to him)
steps.n: Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the action. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يأتي (comes/arrives).

steps.m: a punishment (subject of يَأْتِيهِ - comes to him)
steps.n: Adding tanween dammah makes the noun indefinite
Root letters were connected with vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he was disgraced'
Added يُ at the beginning to show who is doing the action, and changed the vowels to show it's happening to someone else
Added هِ at the end to show who is being disgraced. This word describes what the punishment (عَذَابٌ) does to someone.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now/future, and the middle letter gets a special mark (shadda) to show emphasis. The word connects with عَلَيْهِ to mean 'descends upon'

steps.m: and he settles/will settle (descends upon عَلَيْهِ)
steps.n: The وَ at the start means 'and'
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning
Added هِ at the end to mean 'him'. This shows who the punishment falls upon in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif to create a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is what's doing the action of 'coming' (يَأْتِيهِ) in this verse.
The double-damma (ٌ) shows this is any punishment, not a specific one. It's in the subject position because it's doing the action of 'coming' (يَأْتِيهِ).
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start to show 'one who does' the action. Middle letter changed to ي to make it easier to say
Added shadda on م to connect with previous word. Added tanween to show it's indefinite. This word describes عَذَابٌ (punishment) in the ayah, showing the punishment is lasting/permanent.
This is a basic particle that shows a limit or endpoint. Added vowel marks to show pronunciation.
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning and pronunciation.
Added ى at the end to show reaching an endpoint or limit.
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a long vowel. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added a small vertical line (ٓ) to show a slight pause in reading. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
This is a time word that introduces a condition. The ا at the end makes it specifically mean 'when' something happens. In this ayah, it introduces what happens 'when Our command came.'
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) to the root letters to make it mean 'he came' in the past tense
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter
Extended the alif to show emphasis/intensity of coming
Changed from action to thing. This word is what 'came' in the phrase 'Our command came'
Added damma to show this is the doer of the action in the sentence
Added 'our' to show the command belongs to Allah and the angels speaking
Connected the letters and added fatha marks. The middle و becomes ا in this form.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it in the verse
This is a borrowed word that entered Arabic from other ancient languages

steps.m: the oven (subject of فَارَ - it overflowed)
steps.n: Added ٱل at the beginning to show this is talking about a specific oven. This word is the subject of فَارَ (it overflowed) in this ayah.

steps.m: the oven (subject of فَارَ - it overflowed)
steps.n: Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing the action in the sentence
Command form meaning 'Say!' - a short, direct order to speak. The root letters ق-و-ل are shortened to just قُلْ.
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action in the past. This word appears in a sequence of events, showing what happened after Allah's command came and the oven overflowed.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he carried' to 'carry!' by changing middle vowel to 'i' and dropping last vowel - makes it a command
Added the command prefix alif that's required for most command forms. This word is Allah commanding Noah to carry pairs of each animal onto the ark.
Added kasra vowel mark to show the correct pronunciation
Added ها at the end to show what is being referred to. The ها refers to the ark (سفينة) which is feminine in Arabic. This word connects to احمل (carry) in the phrase 'carry in it'.
This is a basic particle that shows where something starts or comes from

steps.m: from (connects كُلٍّ 'each' to what comes after)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how it connects with the next word كُلٍّ (each). In this ayah, it's showing what Nuh should take 'from' each kind of creature.
Added damma and shadda to make the basic noun form meaning 'every' or 'all'
Added tanween kasra to show this word is possessed by what comes after it (pairs/couples). This connects it grammatically to زَوْجَيْنِ (pairs) in the ayah.

steps.m: to join things together as pairs

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to pair' into noun meaning 'a pair'
Added ين ending to show exactly two pairs. This is what Noah is commanded to carry (احْمِلْ) in the ship.
Added alif at beginning to create number form
Added dual ending to show this describes pairs of things. The ِ at the end shows it's describing زَوْجَيْنِ (pair).
Basic root letters أ-ه-ل get short vowels (َ and ْ) to make the common noun 'family'
Added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon
Added كَ at end to show this belongs to 'you' (masculine singular)

steps.m: and your family (object of اِحْمِلْ - carry)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this with previous command. This word is what Noah was commanded to carry onto the ark.
This is the basic form of the exception particle, made up of إ + ل
Adding shadda (ّ) on the lam makes the meaning stronger
Adding the alif (ا) at the end and vowel marks (ِ َ) helps connect this word to what comes after it. In this ayah, it connects to قَلِيلٌ (few) to mean 'except a few'
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to those who were condemned. The word connects to the verb سَبَقَ (preceded) that comes after it.

steps.m: to go ahead or come before something else
he went ahead (of عَلَيْهِ ٱلْقَوْلُ - the word/decree against him)
steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word refers to how Allah's decree had already gone ahead against some people, meaning they were destined for punishment.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation of basic preposition
Changed ى to يْ to prepare for attaching the pronoun. This is a spelling change that happens before pronouns.
Added هِ (hi) at the end to mean 'him'
Changed from a basic root (say) into a noun meaning 'a saying' by adding the vowel marks
Added ال to make it specific
Added ُ to show this is the doer of the action in سَبَقَ (came before/preceded)
This is a question word or relative pronoun that means 'who' or 'whoever'. It's used to refer to people in general.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the first letter to hamza and added a long alif, which makes the meaning causative - changing from just feeling trust to actively believing
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added alif with madda to show long vowel sound
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
Changed root letters into past tense verb meaning 'he' by adding the vowels a-a-a
Changed the first letter to hamza and added alif, which makes the meaning about actively putting trust in something/someone rather than just being safe
This is a preposition that shows something is happening together with something else. It's one of the basic prepositions in Arabic.
The ه at the end means 'him' and refers back to the ark mentioned earlier in the verse. The ۥٓ marks are pronunciation helpers that don't change the meaning.

steps.m: except (connects to مَن who/whoever in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a basic word used to show exceptions. In this ayah, it's used with مَن (who/whoever) to mean 'except who' - showing that some people or things are not included in what was mentioned before.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: a small amount/few (describing مَنْ ءَامَنَ - those who believed)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (to become little) into an adjective (little/few) to describe the believers
Added past tense pattern. The و (weak letter) changes to ا in this common word. This word is connected to ٱرْكَبُوا۟ (embark/ride) - it tells us who gave this command.
Adding وَ at the start connects this to what happened before. Here it connects this statement to previous events in the story.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he rode' to 'ride!' by adding اِ at start and removing last vowel - makes it a command to one person

steps.m: ride!/board! (commanding a group) (followed by فِيهَا - in it)
steps.n: Changed اِ to ٱ and added وا at the end to make it a command for many people. This is Nuh commanding his people to board the ark.
This is a preposition that shows location or containment
Added ها which refers back to the ship that was mentioned in the previous words (اركبوا = embark/ride). This connects the meaning to show 'in the ship'.
The root letters س-م-و become the word 'name' by dropping the last letter و and connecting س and م together with a sukun mark
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'with' or 'by' something (in this case, with/by Allah's name)
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, showing this is 'the name of Allah'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this a unique name for The One God
Added kasra at the end to show this belongs to something else (the 'name' in بِسْمِ). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to flow or move like water

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Added مَ at the start to show where something happens, like how 'pray' becomes 'prayer place' (mosque)
Changed the ending to show it's a special type of word ending in ى
Added هَا at the end to show it belongs to the ship mentioned earlier in the verse

steps.m: to anchor or make something stay in place

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added مُ at the start to show where something happens, and changed the end to ى to show it's a place noun
Added هَا at the end to show this is the ark's anchoring place

steps.m: and its place of anchoring (paired with مَجْر۪ىٰهَا)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this with مَجْر۪ىٰهَا (its sailing), showing both aspects of the ark's movement

This is a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes 'my Lord' (رَبِّى). The shadda makes the emphasis stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' instead of just 'indeed'.

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled ب (shown by ّ) makes it mean the one who does this action perfectly

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow (connected to إِنَّ)
steps.n: The ِ shows this word is connected to إِنَّ (indeed) that comes before it
Adding ى at the end shows this is MY Lord, like saying 'my book' or 'my house'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who forgives a lot
Added لَ at the beginning to emphasize that this is definitely true. This word describes رَبِّى (my Lord) in this ayah.
Added tanween dammah (ٌ) to show this is describing رَبِّى (my Lord)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Added ي to show this is a strong quality that someone has
Added tanween dammah to show this is any merciful one, not a specific one
The shadda (double letter) was added to make the 'r' sound stronger, showing extra mercy. The tanween ending matches غَفُورٌ (forgiving) since they go together to describe Allah.
This is a basic feminine pronoun used to refer to something feminine. In this ayah, it refers to Noah's ark (which is feminine in Arabic).
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it. This word refers to Noah's ark which is being described as it sails through the waves.

steps.m: to flow/run (like water or a ship)

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels - means something flowed/ran
Added ت at start to show present tense, changed vowels to show continuous action
This is the basic pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' for a group of males or mixed gender
The vowel changes from ُ to ِ because it will be connected to a preposition

steps.m: with/by them (object of تَجْرِى - it sails)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to mean 'with' or 'by'. This shows how the ship sails with/by them. Also added sukun at end to show end of word.

steps.m: in/within (location in مَعْزِلٍ - a place apart)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing location or position. In this ayah, it shows Nuh's son was physically located in a place separate from the ark.

steps.m: in/within (location in مَعْزِلٍ - a place apart)
steps.n: Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how the word is pronounced

steps.m: in/within (location in مَعْزِلٍ - a place apart)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و becomes silent with a sukun.

steps.m: a wave (in مَوْجٍ كَٱلْجِبَالِ - waves like mountains)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is an indefinite noun that is being described by what comes after it (like mountains)
Connected the letters with fatha marks to form the basic word for a single mountain
Changed the vowel pattern to make it plural, showing many mountains
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it follows a preposition

steps.m: like the mountains (describing the size of the waves in مَوْجٍ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the beginning to compare the waves to mountains in size. This word describes how huge the waves were in the story of Prophet Nuh.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound

steps.m: and he called out loudly (subject of this verb is نُوحٌ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this action with what happened before. In this ayah, Noah (نُوحٌ) is the one doing the calling out.
This is the base form of the proper name Noah
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to show this is the subject doing the action of calling
Added double damma (ٌ) at the end to show this is a complete subject not connected to anything after it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

These are two completely different words - ابن means 'son' and بنى means 'to build'. They just happen to share similar letters but aren't related. Think of 'cat' and 'cap' in English - similar spelling but different meanings!
Added هُ to show it's his son. The small و (ۥ) is just a pronunciation helper.
Added fatha vowels to make past tense. The middle و becomes ا because it's a weak letter.
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
This is a basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it connects the word مَوْجٍ (waves) to show where something is happening.
Added kasra (ِ) under the ف to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'fee'
Changed the regular ي to ى (alif maqsurah) at the end because it's at the end of the word

steps.m: a place of separation (from نُوحٌ and the believers)
steps.n: Transforms the root into a noun showing where the separation happened. In this ayah, it refers to the place where Noah's son separated himself from his father and the believers.

steps.m: in a place apart (from نُوحٌ and the believers)
steps.n: The tanween shows this is any place of separation, not a specific one. Here it shows where Noah's son chose to be - in any place away from the believers.
Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb
Changing vowels to show something was done to it rather than it doing something
Added يا (yaa) at start to call out to someone + doubled ي (yaa) at end to show affection, making it mean 'O my dear little son'
Added fatha mark at the end to show the correct grammatical case
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: Board! (command to one person, from نُوحٌ to his son)
steps.n: Changed to a command form. This is Noah commanding his son to board the ark with them.
Added emphasis by doubling the first letter. This happens because the word comes after و, which affects pronunciation.
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'with us'. From context, Noah is calling to his son to join them in the ark.
This is a negative particle used here to give a command to stop or prevent something. In this ayah, it's part of Nuh telling his son not to be with the disbelievers.

steps.m: and do not (command to نُوحٌ's son)
steps.n: The و connects this command to the previous command (اركب/ride) that Nuh gave his son, showing these are related instructions: 'ride with us and do not be with the disbelievers'
Changed from basic root letters to a command form by adding the vowel 'oo' in the middle, making it mean 'Be!'
Added تَ at the beginning to show it's happening now and refers to 'you'

steps.m: you be (in the negative command لَا تَكُن)
steps.n: Dropped the و because it comes after لَا (don't) in the ayah, making it a negative command meaning 'don't be'
This is a basic preposition showing accompaniment. It's not derived from a root.
The shadda adds emphasis to show strong connection with the people being referred to. This word connects to نَا (us) in مَّعَنَا (with us) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Makes the word plural (more than one). This form is used because it comes after the word مَعَ (with)
Adds 'ال' (the) at the start to make it specific, and adds a vowel at the end because it follows the word مَعَ (with)
Basic root letters ق-و-ل got short 'a' vowels (َ) added between them to make قَوَلَ meaning 'he said' in the past
The و (weak letter) changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
Changed the root letters أوي into a first person 'I' verb by adding a long aa sound at start and kasra vowel in middle

steps.m: I will take shelter (response to قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added سَ at the beginning to show 'will' (future). The آ changed to أ. The ى at the end means 'I' am doing the action. This is answering someone who said something.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.

steps.m: to, towards (connecting to جَبَلٍ - a mountain)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it shows direction towards the mountain that Noah's son was trying to reach.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the basic noun form

steps.m: a mountain (that he was seeking refuge on)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is an indefinite noun. In this ayah, it refers to the mountain that Noah's son was trying to climb to escape the flood.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense voweling
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he protects me (from مِنَ ٱلْمَآءِ - from the water)
steps.n: Adds نِى at the end to show that 'me' is being protected. In this ayah, it's specifically about protection from the flood waters.
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from, being part of, or away from something else
Added fatha at the end to connect it smoothly to the next word (the water). In this ayah, it's used to show what someone is trying to be protected from.
The word for water follows this pattern but with hamza at end
Adding ال makes the word specific, like saying 'the water' instead of just any water
The ِ at the end shows this comes after the word مِنَ (from) in the ayah. The آ is just a spelling variation of ا
Added fathas to make it past tense. This is the theoretical form before the weak letter change.
This is a common change when و comes after fatha
This is a negative particle that negates the word عَاصِمَ (protector) that comes after it, meaning 'there is no protector'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show who does the action (like changing 'protect' to 'protector')
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of لا (no) in the phrase 'there is no protector'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a proper word. The fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter make it a noun meaning 'day'
Added ٱلْ at beginning to make it specific and َ at end to show it's the object being acted upon in the sentence

This is a preposition showing something coming from or being protected from something else. In this ayah, it connects 'protects me' (يَعْصِمُنِى) with 'the water' (ٱلْمَآءِ) to mean 'protects me from the water'
Changed from basic root meaning 'command' into the noun 'a command' by adding marks (fatha-sukun pattern)
