Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'command of Allah'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to the One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the word أَمْرِ (command). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'

steps.m: except (introduces the exception مَن رَّحِمَ - whoever He has mercy on)
steps.n: Adding إِ at the beginning and a shadda changes this from a negative particle to an exception particle. In this ayah, it introduces who is excepted from having no protection - those whom Allah has mercy on.
This is a special word that refers to people. In this ayah, it connects to the verb رَّحِمَ (has mercy), showing who receives Allah's mercy.

steps.m: to show mercy, be compassionate, care for others

steps.n: Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he showed mercy'
The shadda (ّ) makes the ر sound stronger, which adds emphasis to show this is an important act of mercy
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: it came between (subject: الْمَوْجُ - the wave)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha and comes between two moving letters. The wave (الْمَوْجُ) is what came between them.

steps.m: and it came between (subject: الْمَوْجُ - the wave)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement about there being no protector

steps.m: to be separate or distinct from something else

steps.n: Adding vowels changes it from a root into a preposition meaning 'between'

steps.m: between them both (separating the son and his father)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show this refers to exactly two things (in this case, the son and his father mentioned in the verse)

steps.m: to move in waves, surge up and down

steps.n: Added vowels to the root letters م-و-ج to make the noun meaning 'wave'
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific wave (in this case, the flood waters threatening Noah's son)

steps.m: the wave (subject of came between them)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this wave is doing the action of coming between them in the story
The middle letter و becomes ا to make it easier to say. This changes 'to be' into 'he was' - a very common change in past tense verbs.

steps.m: so he was (connected to لَا عَاصِمَ - no protector)
steps.n: The ف shows this is a result of what came before - because there was no protector, so he was among the drowned
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from, like saying 'from the water' in this verse.
Added َ (fatha) at the end to help connect it smoothly to the next word ٱلْمَآءِ (the water).
This root is related to being submerged in water
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added hamza at start to show causing someone else to drown
Changed from 'he drowned someone' to 'someone who was drowned' - shows who got drowned instead of who did the drowning
Added ين to show multiple people
Added ال to specify these particular drowned ones and َ at the end because it follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changing the first vowel to kasra makes it passive voice
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together and make it a proper word
Added the ُ ending to show this is the main subject being addressed

steps.m: O earth! (being directly commanded in وَقِيلَ يٰٓأَرْضُ ٱبْلَعِى - 'and it was said: O earth, swallow')
steps.n: Added يٰٓ at the beginning to show this is calling out to the earth directly, like saying 'Hey earth!' or 'O earth!'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he swallowed' to 'swallow!' by adding alif at the start and dropping the last vowel to make it a command
Added feminine marker ى with kasra because the command is directed to أرض (earth), which is feminine in Arabic
The root letters م-و-ه change to make the simple word 'water'. The middle letter و turns into ا, which is a common change in Arabic words.
The long آ is added to emphasize the word 'water', making it stronger
The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon. Here, the earth (يٰٓأَرْضُ) is being commanded to swallow this water.
The كِ suffix shows this water belongs to the earth, which is being addressed as feminine because أَرْض (earth) is a feminine word

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Added يٰ at the beginning to call out to the sky directly
Added و with fatha to connect this phrase with what came before (يٰٓأَرْضُ - O earth)
Added alif after م to make the word longer and more emphatic when calling out to the sky
Added damma (ُ) to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to stop or cease doing something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make something do the action'
Added ى and changed vowels to make it a feminine command, addressing the earth (which is feminine in Arabic)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed the first vowel from fatha to kasra to show something was done to it

steps.m: and it was decreased (connected to أَقْلِعِى)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect it to the previous command in the verse (أَقْلِعِى - desist)
The root letters م-و-ه change to form ماء (water) - a common way Arabic makes simple nouns
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific water rather than just any water. The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's a long vowel.
The ُ (damma) shows this word is doing something in the sentence (subject)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show something was done to it rather than it doing something

steps.m: and it was decided/settled (connected to وَغِيضَ ٱلْمَآءُ - and the water subsided)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with the previous phrase about the water subsiding
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to change from action to noun

steps.m: the matter (subject of قُضِىَ - was settled)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's what was settled in this ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Added است at the beginning to show something becoming level/straight by itself, without someone else doing it
Added ت to show feminine subject (referring to Noah's ark)

steps.m: and it (the ark) became settled (on الْجُودِيِّ)
steps.n: Added و to connect to previous phrase about the water receding
This is the basic concept of being high or elevated

steps.m: on, upon, over (of الْجُودِىِّ - Mount Judi)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing location above something. In this ayah, it shows the ark rested on Mount Judi.
Changed the action into a noun representing the quality itself
Adds 'the' (ال) and changes the ending to show it's a specific high place or mountain

steps.m: Mount Judi (the elevated mountain where Noah's ark rested)
steps.n: Adds the relative adjective ending to make it a proper noun for the specific mountain
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show that someone unknown did the action (passive voice)

steps.m: and it was said (connected to وَغِيضَ ٱلْمَآءُ - and the water subsided)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous one about the water subsiding
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the state of being far

steps.m: away with! (expressing rejection of الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ - the wrongdoing people)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to make it an exclamation of rejection. This is used in the ayah to express strong disapproval of the wrongdoing people.

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: Basic noun form showing a group of people who stand together
Added ال to make it specific
Added لِ at beginning to show 'for/to' and ِ at end because it follows لِ
Added shadda on ل for emphasis
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
This pattern shows someone who does the action. The word describes القوم (the people).
Changed from singular 'wrongdoer' to plural 'wrongdoers' by adding ين at the end, like changing 'thief' to 'thieves'. It describes the word القوم (people) in the sentence.
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding a-vowels (fatha) between letters
Added alif after first root letter to make Form III verb, which shows doing an action to or with someone else
Added و at beginning to connect with previous statement
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) above the final ى to show it's pronounced as a long vowel
This is a proper name that doesn't come from a root. The tanwin (double damma) shows it's indefinite
Added damma (ُ) on first letter to help with pronunciation
Added double damma (ٌ) at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in نَادَىٰ (called out)

steps.m: to care for, nurture, raise up

steps.n: Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete mastery/lordship
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being called by Noah
Added هُ at the end to show it belongs to him (Noah)

steps.m: his Lord (object being called by Noah)
steps.n: Added a double letter mark (shadda) on first letter because it connects with the word before it (نُوحٌ), and added a small و after هُ to show how to say it correctly
Added fatha vowels to make it past tense
Changed to common past tense form with alif

steps.m: then he said (after Nuh called upon his Lord)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after Nuh called upon his Lord in the previous part of the verse

steps.m: to care for, raise, develop to perfection

steps.n: The doubled ب (shadda) shows this is an intensive form, meaning the ultimate or complete form of caring and nurturing
Adding kasra at the end makes this a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what follows. Here it emphasizes 'Your promise' (وَعْدَكَ) in Noah's speech to Allah. The shadda (ّ) doubles the ن to make the emphasis stronger.

steps.m: to build or establish family connections

steps.n: Root 'build/establish' becomes 'son' by adding alif at start and making middle letter silent
Changed initial alif to hamzat wasl and added ي at end to show possession. This word refers to Noah's son in this ayah.
This is a preposition that shows ٱبْنِى (my son) is from أَهْلِى (my family). In this ayah, Nuh is explaining that his son is part of his family.
Changed from a verb about settling to a noun meaning the people who belong together
Added kasra to show this word will be possessed by something
Added ى at the end to show this is my family. This word appears in the story of Prophet Nuh talking to Allah about his son.
This is an emphasis particle that makes statements stronger. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.

steps.m: and indeed (connects to فَقَالَ/so he said)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it in the ayah (after فَقَالَ/so he said). It shows this is part of what Nuh said.

steps.m: to promise or make a commitment

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun by adding vowel marks. This shows the actual promise itself rather than the act of promising.

steps.m: Your promise (object of إِنَّ in Nuh's prayer to Allah)
steps.n: Adds كَ (your) at the end to show the promise belongs to Allah, since this is part of Prophet Nuh's prayer to Allah. The َ (fatha) before ك shows this word is affected by إِنَّ earlier in the sentence.
The doubled letter (shadda) makes it stronger and turns it into a noun meaning 'truth'
Adding ال makes it specific - 'the truth' instead of just any truth
The ُ at the end shows this word is doing something in the sentence
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation of the basic pronoun meaning 'you'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the sentence

steps.m: to judge wisely or make wise decisions

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Added أَ at start and made middle letter silent to make it mean 'more/most' - this is called the comparative form
Added ُ to show this describes 'You' (أَنتَ) in the sentence

steps.m: to judge wisely, make wise decisions

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this from a basic root into a word meaning 'someone who is wise'
Changes from 'wise one' to 'judges/those who make wise judgments' by adding ين ending to make it plural. The long ā (ٰ) is also added
Adds 'the' to show we're talking about specific judges
Shows this word comes after أَحْكَمُ (most) in the phrase 'most wise of judges'
Basic root letters ق-و-ل got vowels (َ) to make قَوَلَ meaning 'he said' in the past
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter, making it easier to say
Added vowel marks to properly pronounce the name Noah. The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is the main subject.

steps.m: O Noah! (being called by Allah in قَالَ)
steps.n: Added يٰ at the beginning to show someone is calling out to Noah. This word comes after قَالَ (He said) showing that Allah is addressing Noah directly.
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphasis word 'indeed'
Added pronoun هُۥ (he/it) to show what is being emphasized. In this ayah, it refers to the 'deed' (عَمَلٌ) that follows.
This is a special word used to say something is not true or does not exist
Adding vowels to make it a usable word meaning 'is not'

steps.m: from (connecting أَهْلِكَ to show 'from your family')
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects with أَهْلِكَ (your family) to show that someone is 'from your family'
Connected the root letters together with basic vowel marks to form the noun
Added i-sound at the end because it follows مِنْ (from/of), showing it belongs to what comes after it
Added كَ at the end to show the family belongs to 'you' (masculine singular)
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed'. This word emphasizes what comes after it.
Adding هُ at the end shows who or what is being talked about (in this case, Noah's son)
The ۥ mark is just a pronunciation helper that shows the هُ should be stretched slightly when read
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create a noun meaning the result of working/acting
Added tanween damma to show this is any general work/deed, not a specific one
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create the basic noun form
Added damma at the end to show this word is describing صٰلِحٍ (righteous) in the ayah
Changes from verb 'to be good' to noun meaning 'one who is good'
Added tanwin kasra to show this is describing the word عَمَلٌ (deed) in the ayah. The alif is written as a long vowel mark (ٰ) in this style.
This particle connects this statement to what came before, showing a result or sequence. Here it connects to Allah telling Nuh that his son is not from his family.

steps.m: so do not (connecting to تَسْـَٔلْنِ - ask me)
steps.n: Added لا which means 'not' to create a negative command. This combines with the following verb تَسْـَٔلْنِ (ask me) to mean 'so do not ask me'.
This is the basic concept of asking or requesting information
The word changed to 'you' form with ta- prefix, and the sukun ending makes it negative ('do not ask')
Adding نِ at the end makes the request more emphatic, like saying 'definitely ask'
Changing the vowel to a sukun (stop mark) turns 'you ask' into 'do not ask me' - like adding 'don't' in English
The نِ means 'me' in both words. The first word has a 'do not' meaning because it's a command to stop asking. The second word just states 'you ask me' like normal talking.

steps.m: what (asking about something that is not knowledge/علْمٌ)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about something. In this ayah, it's asking about something that Allah says is not knowledge (علْمٌ).
This is a special negative verb that has no clear root. It's used to negate sentences in the present tense.
This special verb is used to say something is not true in the present. In this ayah, it's saying that someone is not from Noah's family.
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose, like saying something is 'to' or 'for' someone

steps.m: for you (connected to لَيْسَ - is not)
steps.n: Added the pronoun 'you' to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to لَيْسَ (is not) in the ayah, showing what is not for/belonging to the person being addressed.
Basic preposition indicating means or accompaniment (connected to عِلْمٌ in this ayah)
Adds third person masculine singular pronoun to show what we're talking about. Here it refers back to عِلْمٌ (knowledge) in the ayah.
Adds a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters together
Added kasra under first letter and sukun on second letter to change from action (knowing) to thing (knowledge)
Added double-damma (tanwin) at the end to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a knowledge' instead of 'the knowledge') and functions as a subject in the sentence
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more certain
Added ي at the end to show this is about 'me/I'. The kasra (ِ) helps connect it. This word is the subject of أَعِظُكَ (I advise you) in this ayah.
Changed the writing style of ي to ىٓ with an alif mark (ٓ) on top. This doesn't change the meaning, just the way it looks.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he advised' to 'I advise' by adding أ at the start to show 'I' am doing the action. The و drops because it's a weak letter that can't stay at the start.
Added كَ to show who is being advised
This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects the warning (أَعِظُكَ - 'I warn you') with what is being warned against (تَكُونَ - 'you become')
Added fatha (َ) on the first letter to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'anna'
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Command form changes the first vowel to 'u' sound (damma) to make it mean 'be!' instead of 'was'

steps.m: that you be (after أَن in this ayah)
steps.n: Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' as the subject. The word follows أَن (that) which puts it in the subjunctive mood.
Drops the final -a sound because it comes after أَن (that)
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from
Added َ at the end because it comes before the definite noun الجٰهِلِينَ (the ignorant ones). This whole phrase tells us what Nuh might become - one of the ignorant ones.

steps.m: to not know, to act without knowledge
someone who doesn't know or understand (doer)
steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'not knowing' to describe a person who doesn't know by adding alif after first letter - like changing 'ignore' to 'ignorant person'

steps.m: people who don't know or understand (after مِنَ)
steps.n: The ين ending makes it plural. It comes after مِنَ (from/of) in this ayah.

steps.m: the people who don't know or understand (after مِنَ)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific - referring to particular ignorant people. The final َ shows it follows مِنَ.

steps.m: he said (introduces what Nuh عليه السلام says next)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word introduces what Prophet Nuh says in his prayer.

steps.m: he said (introduces what Nuh عليه السلام says next)
steps.n: The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas - this is a standard Arabic rule for hollow verbs

steps.m: to nurture and care for completely

steps.n: Doubling the ب shows complete/intense nurturing and care
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger - like adding an exclamation mark in English
The word gets a kasra (ِ) at the end to connect with 'I' (meaning 'Indeed I')
Added ى at the end to show 'I' - this is written as a special form of ي when it comes at the end of a word
Added the small alif (ٓ) above the ى to show it's being stretched in pronunciation when speaking to Allah
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed from 'he sought refuge' to 'I seek refuge' by changing the first vowel to 'u' sound to show I am doing it
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action

steps.m: with/by (connects to أَعُوذُ - I seek refuge)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows how the seeking refuge happens. It connects to the verb أَعُوذُ (I seek refuge) earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: with You (object of seeking refuge in أَعُوذُ)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows this refers to Allah (you) as the one being sought for refuge. This connects to the verb أَعُوذُ (I seek refuge) earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: that (connects to أَسْـَٔلَكَ - I ask you)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence together. Here it connects 'I seek refuge with You' to 'I ask you', showing what the speaker wants to avoid doing.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he asked' to present tense 'I ask' by adding أَ at start and changing vowels. The ُ at the end shows it's 'I' doing the action

steps.m: I ask you (object of أَعُوذُ - I seek refuge)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show you are being asked. This word explains what the speaker is seeking refuge from in أَعُوذُ (I seek refuge).

steps.m: what (object of أَسْـَٔلَكَ - I ask you)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about things. In this ayah, it's the object of the verb أَسْـَٔلَكَ (I ask you), showing what Nuh (AS) is saying he won't ask about.
This is a special root that only appears in specific forms to show negation.
This adds the basic vowels to make it a working verb meaning 'is not'
This is a preposition showing direction or purpose. Here it connects to the verb تَغْفِرْ (forgive) to show who is being forgiven.
Adding ى shows this means 'me'. In this ayah, it's showing who is asking to be forgiven - 'forgive me'.
This is a preposition showing how the seeking refuge (أَعُوذُ) is done
Adding هِ shows this refers to something mentioned before (him/it)
The small ۦ is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Changed the verb (to know) into a noun (knowledge) by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added double damma at the end to show this is the main subject being talked about
Added fatha to connect this word to what follows
Added إِ which introduces a condition

steps.m: and if not (connects to تَغْفِرْ - forgive)
steps.n: Added لَّا which adds the meaning of 'not/except'. This word connects to تَغْفِرْ (forgive) in the next part of the ayah, creating the meaning 'and if you do not forgive me'
Added basic past tense pattern with fatha marks
Changed from 'he forgave' to 'you forgive' by adding تَ at start. The sukun ending makes it a command/request. Used with 'if' in this ayah.
Added kasra to the letter ل to help with pronunciation
Added ى at the end to show this means 'me'. In this ayah, it's used to say something doesn't belong to the speaker (لَيْسَ لِى means 'is not for me')
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Adding ت at start makes it 'you' instead of 'he'
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this is 'she' doing the action in present tense, not 'you'
The َ (fatha) at the end makes this a subjunctive verb, used after words like 'that' or 'so that'
Changed from normal present tense to jussive mood (shorter ending) because it comes after 'unless/if not'
Added نِي to show that 'me' is receiving the mercy

steps.m: and you show mercy to me (connected to تَغْفِرْ)
steps.n: Added و (and) to connect with the previous verb تَغْفِرْ (you forgive)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed first vowel to 'oo' sound and dropped middle vowel to make it 'to be' in present tense
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action
The word is shortened by dropping the و at the end after a special word came before it
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts or what group it belongs to
The shadda (ّ) makes the م sound stronger to emphasize the word
The fatha (َ) at the end connects this word to the next word الْخٰسِرِينَ (the losers), showing it means 'from among the losers'
This is the basic past tense form
Adding the vowels (fatha-kasra-fatha) changes it from the root 'to lose' into 'he lost' in the past tense
Added alif after first letter to make it describe someone who does the action
Added ين to make it plural. This comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Added ال to make it definite and fatha at end because it comes after مِنَ
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense form
Changing the first vowel to kasra (ِ) starts making it passive
Adding yaa (ي) between first and second root letters completes the passive form
This is the proper name Noah, which comes from a root meaning 'to rest or settle'
The damma at the end shows this is the subject of the verb قِيلَ (it was said)
The يٰ at the beginning is used to call out to someone directly, like saying 'Hey Noah!' or 'O Noah!'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense form
Changed to command form by adding ٱ at start, putting sukun on ب and ط, and kasra between them. This is Allah commanding Noah to descend.

steps.m: to be safe and at peace

steps.n: Added an alif after the second letter to make it mean the state of peace itself, not just the action
Added بِ at the beginning to show this peace comes with or accompanies something (in this case, Noah's landing)

steps.m: with (a special kind of divine) peace (that comes from Allah)
steps.n: Added tanwin (two kasras) at the end to show this is a special kind of divine peace, and changed the alif to a special long alif (ٰ) which is common in Quranic writing
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added نا at the end to show this means 'from us' (referring to Allah in this context)
Added shadda on the meem for emphasis and easier pronunciation when connected to previous word

steps.m: to increase, grow, or bring forth good

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added ة to make it into a noun for one instance of blessing
Changed to feminine plural form by replacing ة with ات
Added و to connect this word to what came before it
Added two dots under (tanween kasra) to show these blessings are being received - like when we say 'peace and blessings upon the Prophet'
The ى at the end changes to ي with a sukun when something is attached to it
Added ك at the end to show this applies to 'you' (singular)
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) to show the extended sound
Added vertical fatḥah to show the extended sound
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: to be a mother/source/origin of something

steps.n: Changes to plural form showing multiple groups/communities
Adds two dots under the last letter to show this word is part of a longer phrase, like saying 'of nations' or 'from nations'
Starting with the basic preposition مِنْ which shows where something comes from
Added مَن which means 'who' or 'whom' to specify who something is from
When مِنْ comes before a word starting with م, the ن combines with the م making a stronger م sound (shown by the ّ mark)
This is the basic word meaning 'with' - it's a particle that shows something is together with something else
Added ك at the end to show this means 'with you' instead of just 'with'
Added shadda on م because it comes after a word ending in ن or م, making the sound blend together (this is called assimilation)
Added damma and fatha vowels to create the plural form meaning 'nations'
Added tanween dammah to make the word indefinite

steps.m: and nations (connected to بَرَكٰتٍ/blessings in the ayah)
steps.n: Adds conjunction 'and' to connect this word with 'blessings' mentioned earlier in the verse
Added shadda to show intensity of giving enjoyment
Changed to present tense with 'we' as subject
Added future marker to show this will happen later
Added pronoun to show who receives the enjoyment
Basic particle that connects events happening one after another. It doesn't come from a root word.

steps.m: then (connecting what happens to أُمَمٌ with what came before)
steps.n: Added marks to show how to say 'then' correctly - it connects what happens to one group with what happened before
Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb form
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and doubling the middle س letter
Added هُم to show who is being touched
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or moves away from

steps.m: from us (connected to بِسَلٰمٍ - with peace)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show this means 'us'. This connects to the word بِسَلٰمٍ (with peace) to show the peace comes from us.

steps.m: from us (connected to بِسَلٰمٍ - with peace)
steps.n: The shadda on initial م is added because this word comes after a vowel in speech. It doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding alif after second root letter turns the action into a noun meaning the result of that action

steps.m: a punishment (that will come to أُمَمٌ)
steps.n: Adding tanween shows this is an undefined punishment that will affect the nations (أُمَمٌ) mentioned in the ayah
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters together
Adding ي makes the quality stronger and more intense
Adding double dammah makes it indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English)
This is the basic form of the feminine demonstrative pronoun used to point to something

steps.m: that (feminine, pointing to something far away)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the end shows we're pointing to something far away. This word is pointing to the stories mentioned in the previous verses.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'news' (أَنۢبَآءِ) with 'before' (قَبْلِ) to show these stories come from before this time.
Root letters get vowels (fat-ha) to make one piece of news
Changes from singular 'news' to plural 'news items' by adding extra letters

steps.m: of the news (plural) (possessed by الْغَيْبِ - the unseen)
steps.n: Shows this word is connected to الْغَيْبِ (the unseen) that comes after it in the ayah
Changes from an action (being hidden) to a noun (hidden thing). This word is connected to أَنۢبَآءِ (news/stories) in the ayah.
Adding the definite article makes it specific

steps.m: of the unseen (part of أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْغَيْبِ - news of the unseen)
steps.n: The ِ ending shows this word belongs to أَنۢبَآءِ (news), making the phrase 'news of the unseen'

steps.m: to reveal or inspire divine guidance

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: we reveal it (referring to these stories of the unseen)
steps.n: Added feminine object pronoun هَآ to show what is being revealed (referring back to أَنۢبَآءِ - the stories/news mentioned earlier)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
The ى changes to ي before adding a pronoun suffix

steps.m: to you (the one being told about the stories)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this means 'to you' (speaking to one male person)
This is a negative particle that makes the verb كُنتَ (you were) into 'you were not'. It's like adding 'not' in English to make something negative.
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
The middle weak letter و drops out and the first vowel changes to match common pattern for this type of verb
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did this action in the past
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding ت at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you know it/her (referring to the stories/news mentioned before)
steps.n: Added هَآ at the end to show what is being known - refers back to the stories/news (أَنۢبَآءِ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is the basic form used to build second person pronouns

steps.m: you (masculine singular) (subject of كُنتَ - you were)
steps.n: These are completely different words - أَنْ means 'that' while أَنتَ is the word for 'you'. They just look similar but have different meanings and uses.
This is the basic negation word that stops something from being true

steps.m: and not (connecting to the previous statement تَعْلَمُهَآ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous statement about 'knowing it'
Changed the root meaning of standing together into a noun meaning the group that stands together (people/folk)
Added ُ (damma) to show this word is doing something in the sentence
Added كَ at the end to show these are your people
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'news/stories' to what follows, showing these are stories specifically from the unseen.
Added sukun (ْ) at the end because it's followed by another word. This helps with pronunciation when connecting words.

steps.m: to face, to accept, to come before

steps.n: Root meaning 'face/accept' becomes a simple word for time - 'before'
Added kasra to show this word connects to 'this' (هٰذَا) that comes after it, making it mean 'before this'
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby that you can see or refer to
The small alif (ٰ) above the ه adds emphasis to show clear pointing
The fatha and alif at the end show this is pointing to one masculine thing
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to command form by adding ٱ at start and changing vowels. This tells someone to be patient.
Added فَ at beginning to show this command comes as a result of what was mentioned before - the stories of the unseen that were revealed.
Adding shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional particle meaning 'if' to an emphasis particle meaning 'indeed'. This word emphasizes the certainty of what follows in the ayah.
Added vowels and alif to create active participle pattern
Added feminine ending to create noun for result/outcome
Added 'the' at beginning and accusative case ending because it's the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to make 'one who' does the action of protecting

steps.m: one who guards against evil/is pious
steps.n: The ت and ق merged together with a shadda (ّ), making it mean someone who strongly guards against evil

steps.m: those who are mindful of God
steps.n: This makes it plural for masculine nouns
Adding 'ال' (al) at the start makes it mean 'the' pious ones instead of just pious ones

steps.m: for the pious ones (end of description of العٰقِبَة)
steps.n: Adds لِ at beginning to show this describes who the good end (العٰقِبَة) mentioned in the ayah is for
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it shows the direction of the message to the people of 'Ad.
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif above) at the end to show proper spelling of this preposition
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: Aad (name of ancient tribe mentioned in the Quran)
steps.n: Forms the name of an ancient tribe that was known for repeatedly returning to disbelief despite warnings. This word appears in a story about Prophet Hud being sent to them.
The word stays the same as the previous step since it's already in the correct form
Changed the final weak letter و to ا when adding a suffix
Added هُمْ at the end to show possession - 'their brother'. This word is the object (receiver) of the verb 'sent' in this ayah.
Adding damma makes it into a proper noun referring to the people of Prophet Hud

steps.m: Hud (as their brother who was sent to them)
steps.n: Added an -an ending (tanween) to show Hud is being described as someone (their brother) rather than being used as just a name
Added fathas to make past tense form
Common change where و becomes ا in hollow verbs
Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The meaning shifted from the action of standing together to refer to the group that does this.

steps.m: O my people! (being called by هُود)
steps.n: Added يٰ at start to show calling out to someone, and ِ at end to show possession (my people). This is Hud calling out to his people in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he worshipped' to a command 'worship!' by adding U sound at start and changing middle vowel
Changed to address multiple people by adding وا at the end. This is a command from هُود to his قَوْمِ (people) to worship Allah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from 'a god' to 'The God' by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows emphasis.

steps.m: Allah (as the object of اعْبُدُوا - worship)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because Allah is the object being worshipped in the phrase 'worship Allah'
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. Here it's saying 'you do not have' (مَا لَكُم).
This particle shows direction or purpose. Here it connects to هُودًا (Hud) to show he is speaking to them.
The suffix كُم shows this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, Prophet Hud (هُودًا) is speaking to his people.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or that it's part of something. Here it connects to the word إِلٰهٍ (god) to show 'from/of any god'.
The م gets a shadda (double mark) because it comes after the word ما, which is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic to make it flow better when speaking.

steps.m: to be worshipped, to be taken as divine

steps.n: Changes from basic root into noun meaning 'a god'
The alif is written as a small mark above to show long 'aa' sound
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite and follows the word مِنْ (from/of) in the ayah
Basic noun form meaning 'other than' or 'different from'
Added damma to show this is the subject
Added pronoun suffix to show who is being referred to
This is a negative particle that works with إِلَّا (except/only) to say something is not true. Here it's saying 'you are not except inventors [of falsehood]'
This is the basic form for addressing one male person directly
Adding مْ with a ُ before it changes this from speaking to one person to speaking to many people

steps.m: except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a complete particle that shows exception - it tells us that what comes after it is not included in what was said before. In this ayah, it connects to مُفْتَرُونَ (inventors of falsehood) to say that the people are 'nothing except/but inventors of falsehood'.

steps.m: to make up or invent falsely

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb

steps.m: one who makes up false things
steps.n: Added مُ at start and changed to اسم فاعل pattern (doer form) to change from 'made up' to 'one who makes up', like changing 'lie' to 'liar'

steps.m: those who make up false things (subject of أَنتُمْ)
steps.n: Added ون to make it plural, showing it's talking about multiple people. This word describes the subject 'you all' (أَنتُمْ) in the ayah.

steps.m: to stand together, gather, rise up

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The root meaning of standing together became associated with a group that stands as one.
Added يٰ (yaa) at the beginning to call out to the people directly, like saying 'Hey people!' or 'O people!'

steps.m: O my people! (addressing his own community)
steps.n: Added ِ (kasra) at the end to show these are the speaker's own people, like saying 'my people' instead of just 'people'
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'. In this ayah, it negates the verb أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ (I ask you), making it mean 'I do not ask you'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Changed alif to alif madd (آ) to show long vowel sound
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and make the past tense form
Added أ at beginning to show 'I' and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: I ask you all (object of أَسْـَٔلُ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show the action is done to 'you all'
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun
Added هِ at the end to mean 'him'. This word connects to أَجْرًا (reward) in the ayah, showing 'reward upon him'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: a reward (object of أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ - I ask you)
steps.n: Changed to a noun meaning 'reward'. The ًا ending shows this is the thing being asked for in the phrase 'I do not ask you for a reward.'

steps.m: indeed (emphasizing that the speaker's reward is only from Allah)
steps.n: This is an emphasis particle that strengthens the meaning of what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the speaker's reward comes only from Allah who created him.
Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'reward' by adding the vowels
Added ي at the end to show it belongs to me
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is doing something (like 'my reward' is giving or receiving)
This is a basic exception word that shows something is being left out from what was mentioned before
Added emphasis by doubling the ل with shadda and adding ا at the end. This makes the exception stronger and clearer.

steps.m: upon (connecting أَجْرًا to the following phrase)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows one thing is connected to or dependent on another. Here it connects the word أَجْرًا (reward) to what comes after it, showing where the reward comes from.
This is the basic demonstrative form meaning 'this one' (masculine singular)
Adding ٱلْ with a shadda changes this from pointing to something ('this') to connecting parts of a sentence. Here it connects to فَطَرَنِى (created me), describing who did the creating.

steps.m: to create from nothing, to bring into existence

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he created me (referring to Allah's creation)
steps.n: Added نِي at the end to show that 'I' am the one being created. This word appears in a statement about Allah's creation.

steps.m: he created me (referring to Allah's creation)
steps.n: Added the elongation mark (ٓ) after ى to show proper Quranic spelling, but this doesn't change the meaning
This particle turns a statement into a question, showing surprise or drawing attention to what follows
Added فَ to show this question follows from what was said before

steps.m: then will you not? (connected to تَعْقِلُونَ - use reason)
steps.n: Added لا to make the question negative. This combination questions why people are not doing something (using reason, in this case) when they should be.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Vowels changed from 'a-a' to 'i-u' to show it's happening now instead of in the past
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at the end to show many people are doing it

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: The root meaning of standing/rising became associated with a group of people who stand together
Adding يا makes it a call or address to people
The kasra at the end shows possession - meaning 'my people'
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this phrase to what came before it in the ayah, where Hud continues speaking to his people
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ٱسْتَ at the start changes 'he forgave' into 'ask for forgiveness'. Also changes from past tense to a command.

steps.m: all of you seek forgiveness! (command to group before رَبَّكُمْ/your Lord)
steps.n: Adding وا makes this a command to a group instead of just one person. This word is connected to رَبَّكُمْ (your Lord) which comes right after it.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps grow (master/lord)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who really does this action completely
The fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb استغفروا (ask forgiveness) that comes before it
The كُمْ at the end shows this is your (plural) lord - meaning the lord of all the people being spoken to
This word connects two commands: first 'seek forgiveness' (استغفروا) and then 'turn in repentance' (توبوا). It shows these should happen in order - first seek forgiveness, then turn back to Allah.

steps.m: to return back (to what is right)
all of you return back! (plural command)
steps.n: Word is already in the correct form with وا suffix for plural command
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for the attached pronoun
Added the pronoun هِ meaning 'him/it' to show who the action is directed towards
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added يُ at the start to show it's happening now (present), and changed middle vowels to match present tense pattern
Added kasra at the end to show this is in jussive mood (used after certain words like لَمْ)

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Added ء at the end and kept the long ا to make it into a noun meaning 'the elevated thing'
Added ٱل at beginning to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda

steps.m: the sky (as the object being sent)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show it's the object receiving the action of sending in this ayah
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for attaching the pronoun. This is a common preposition that shows something is on top of or directed at something else.

steps.m: upon you all (object of يُرْسِلِ - he sends)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. In this ayah, it shows who will receive the rain that Allah sends.
Added a shadda (double letter) and vowels to make the root into a past-tense verb meaning 'it poured'
Changed to a noun meaning 'lots of pouring' - like heavy rain
Added مِ to show it's a thing that does lots of pouring (like rain)

steps.m: one that pours out heavily (describing السَّمَآءَ/the sky)
steps.n: Added emphasis on the م because it comes after a vowel in the previous word

steps.m: heavily pouring (describing how the sky sends rain)
steps.n: Added ًا (an) at the end to make it describe how the rain falls - like saying 'heavily' or 'abundantly'

steps.m: to increase or add to something

steps.n: Added يَ at beginning to show present tense. The ي in the middle drops because it's a weak letter between two vowels
Added كُم at the end to show who receives the action (you all). Added sukun before كُم to help pronunciation
Added وَ at beginning to connect this word to what came before it. Added final sukun for proper pronunciation
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create intensity
Added ة to make it into a specific instance of power/strength

steps.m: a power, a strength (as the object being added to)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object receiving the action of 'adding to' in the phrase 'adding to your strength'
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it's used with هِ (him) to show 'to Him' referring to Allah.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a spelling variation - it doesn't change the meaning
Root that expresses the concept of strength and power
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning strength
Added ة to make it into a specific instance of strength
Added kasra to show this strength belongs to something
Added كُمْ to show this strength belongs to multiple people being addressed
Starting with the basic negation particle that stops or prevents something
Added و at the beginning to connect this negative command to the previous statements in the verse

steps.m: to turn away or take charge
steps.n: Root expressing the concept of turning away or following
Added shadda to show intensive meaning of turning away completely
Added تَ to show doing action to oneself
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' as subject
Added وْا to show plural 'you all'

steps.m: to commit wrong or cross boundaries
steps.n: Root expressing the concept of committing crimes or sins
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and ِ in middle to show who does the action

steps.m: those who commit wrong (being turned away from)
steps.n: Added ين to show multiple people. This word is being acted upon by the verb تَتَوَلَّوْا (turn away) in the ayah.
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb pattern
Changed قَوَلَ to قَالَ (flipped و to ا) and added وا at the end to show many people did the action. In this ayah, this refers to the people speaking to هُودُ.

steps.m: to guide, to return to the right path

steps.n: The root letters form the name Hud, which refers to a prophet and his people mentioned in the Quran
Adding يٰ at the start is a way of calling out to someone in Arabic
This is a negative particle used here to negate the verb that follows it. In this ayah, it's saying 'you have not come' rather than 'you have come'.
Connected the root letters and added alif due to hollow root pattern
Changed vowels and added ت to show 'you' did the action
Added نا at the end to show the action was directed towards 'us'
Changes the root 'make clear' into a feminine noun meaning 'a clear sign/proof'

steps.m: with a clear proof (connected to جِئْتَنَا - you came to us)
steps.n: The preposition بِ connects this word to the verb جِئْتَنَا, showing what you came with

steps.m: with a clear proof (object of جِئْتَنَا through بِ)
steps.n: The word gets a double dot (tanween) at the end to show it's connected to 'you came to us' through the word 'with'
This is a special word that can mean different things. Here it means 'not' because it's being used to negate نَحْنُ بِتَارِكِىٓ (we are leaving) in the ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added vowel marks to show this is a subject pronoun meaning 'we'. In this ayah, it's used to emphasize that the speakers are the subject who are not believing.
Changes from verb 'to leave' to 'one who leaves'
Preposition بِ meaning 'by/with' attached as a prefix

steps.m: to be worshipped as a deity

steps.n: Changed from a verb meaning 'to worship' into a noun meaning 'a god' by adding vowel marks
Changed from masculine 'god' to feminine 'goddess' by adding ة at the end
Changes to broken plural form for feminine words
This is a preposition that shows movement away from or separation from something. In this ayah, it shows they are turning away from what is being said.
Added vowels to make the root into a noun meaning 'speech' or 'saying'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it
Added كَ at the end to show this is addressing Prophet Hud, as his people are responding to him in this ayah
This is a negation particle that makes the statement negative. In this ayah, it's working with نَحْنُ (we) to say 'we are not'
The و connects this negative statement to what came before it, joining two related ideas
This is a standalone pronoun that doesn't come from a root. It's used to refer to a group that includes the speaker.
Added vowel marks to show this is the subject doing the action: fatha on ن, sukun on ح, and damma on final ن. In this ayah, it shows who is not leaving their gods.
This is a basic preposition that shows possession or purpose
Added ك with fatha (كَ) at the end to show this is directed to one male person being spoken to
This is the basic meaning that all words from this root share
Added مُ at start and changed middle to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many believers
Added بِ at start to mean 'with/by', and added َ (fatha) at end because it comes after نَحْنُ
This is a helper word that shows what follows is a condition - like saying 'if' in English. In this ayah, it connects to نَّقُولُ (we say) to mean 'if we say'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning
Changed from past tense (قَالَ) to present tense (قُولُ) by changing the vowels. The long ا disappears because it can't come after the u-sound (damma).
Added ن at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Word is already in the correct form نَقُولُ (we say)
This is a basic particle that shows an exception - it tells us that what comes after it is different from what came before
Adding shadda and alif makes the exception stronger. In this ayah, it's used to contrast with what was said before (نَّقُولُ - we say), showing the only thing they're saying
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form
Added reflexive pattern with اِ prefix and تَ after first root letter to show the action happens by itself
The final و changes to ى with an alif above it (ىٰ) because it's at the end of the word

steps.m: he came upon you (object of بعض)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show the action affects 'you'. The initial اِ becomes ٱ. This word follows بعض (some of) in the sentence.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'
Added damma at the end to show this word is doing the action in اعترى (struck/afflicted) our gods
Forms the basic noun for something that is worshipped
Adds feminine marker ة to make it refer to a female deity
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple goddesses
Changed the verb into a noun representing the concept of evil or harm
Added the preposition بِ to show 'with' or 'by means of'
Added marks (ٍ and ٓ) to show this is talking about a specific kind of harm - the word is stretched out to emphasize how bad the harm could be
Added short vowels 'a' to make it mean 'he said' in the past

steps.m: he said (in response to their accusation)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic for middle-weak verbs.
This is a basic emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'definitely' or 'truly' in English
The word changes to show it will connect to 'I' - like saying 'Indeed I' instead of just 'Indeed'
Added ي at the end to show this means 'I'
The ي is written as ى and elongated with ٓ for recitation purposes
Adding vowels to make a past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to make it present tense 'I witness'

steps.m: I call to witness (acted on الله)
steps.n: Added أُ at beginning to show first person ('I') and make the meaning causative ('make someone witness'). In this ayah, الله is being called as a witness.
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God

steps.m: Allah (as the object of أُشْهِدُ - I call to witness)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is the object being witnessed
This root carries the basic meaning of observing or attesting to something
Changed from 'I witness' to 'witness!' by adding hamza (أ) at start - makes it 'I witness/testify'
Added وا at the end to make it plural (addressing multiple people)
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', with appropriate vowel marks and elongation signs
This is a basic particle that introduces emphasis and connects clauses. In this ayah, it's used by the speaker to emphasize what follows.

steps.m: indeed I (speaking to قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added ي (which becomes ى) at the end to show the speaker is talking about themselves. The kasra (ِ) appears before ى to help pronunciation.
Changed from basic root to a word showing someone's state of being free/innocent

steps.m: one who is free from blame
steps.n: Added ي to show this describes someone who has this quality
Added tanween dammah to show this is indefinite and in the nominative case
Combined the two basic words مِن (from) and مَا (what)
The ن merges into the م, making it doubled (shown by the ّ mark). This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic when ن is followed by م.
Connected root letters with vowels to form basic past tense verb
Adding ت at the start changes it from past tense 'he joined' to present tense 'you/she join(s)'
Changed from 'you/she' (تَ) to 'you all' (تُ) and added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. In this context, it refers to associating partners with Allah.
This is a basic preposition showing separation or distance. In this ayah, it's used with دُونِهِ (besides Him) to show moving away from something.
Added damma on first letter to create the preposition meaning 'besides' or 'other than'
Added -i sound at the end because it's owned by/connected to something after it

steps.m: besides Him/other than Him (owned, after مِن)
steps.n: Added هِۦ at the end to show this means 'besides Him' specifically. The ۦ is just a pronunciation mark for the هِ.
Adding the kasra changes it from a verb (to plot) into a noun (a plot)
Makes the word nominative case
Adds a plural masculine ending
Adds نِ at the end to show 'me' as the target of the plotting (like saying 'plot against ME!')
Adds فَ to show this is a result or sequence, and ى as an alternate spelling of the final ِ
Drops the extra ي at the end but keeps same meaning - 'so plot against me' (command to a group)

steps.m: to gather or bring things together

steps.n: Changed root letters to make جَمِيع (jamee') meaning 'all together/complete' - describes how the plot should include everyone
Added ًا at the end to show this word describes how the plotting should be done - with everyone working together
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with some time in between. In this ayah, it connects what happens after فَكِيدُونِى (so plot against me).
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates تُنظِرُونِ (give respite), making it mean 'do not give respite'
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Added تُ prefix for present tense 'you' and changed middle vowel to kasra
Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed
Added نِ at the end to show the action is being done to 'me'
This is the basic emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
The نّ gets a kasra (ِ) to prepare for the ي coming after it

steps.m: indeed I (speaking before تَوَكَّلْتُ - I have relied)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show 'I' am speaking. This connects to the next word تَوَكَّلْتُ (I have relied) where I am also the speaker
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding تَ at start and doubling the middle letter shows doing the action to/for oneself
Changed from a statement (he trusted) to a command (trust!)

steps.m: I put my complete trust (connected to عَلَى ٱللَّهِ - in Allah)
steps.n: Added تُ at end to show 'I' did this action in the past. This word connects to عَلَى ٱللَّهِ showing who was trusted.

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added ال to make it definite - 'the' god instead of 'a' god
The word 'Allah' comes from 'al-ilah' (the god), but became a special name for the One True God. The 'i' sound at the end shows it connects to the word 'upon' before it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows intensity, indicating complete mastery and lordship. This word is part of a phrase describing Allah's complete control.
Added kasra before ي and the ي shows possession (my). This word is the subject of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in this ayah.
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to create the noun meaning 'lord'
Adds the conjunction to connect this word to previous phrase
Added possessive pronoun suffix كم meaning 'your (plural)'
This is the basic negative particle 'ma' that can mean 'what', 'that', or work as a negation word like 'no/not'

This is a basic word that shows where something starts from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects to the word دَآبَّةٍ (creature) to show 'of/from any creature'
Doubling the middle letter adds emphasis to the walking/movement
Adding ة makes it refer to a female creature or animal in general
The alif is written longer to emphasize the meaning of constant movement - like how animals are always moving around
Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object of the word مِن (from/of) in the ayah

steps.m: except (excluding what comes after from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Adding the alif (ا) at the end completes the particle and makes it ready to connect with what comes after it. In this ayah, it's connecting to هُوَ (He) to exclude Allah from all other beings mentioned before.

steps.m: He (referring to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a pronoun used to refer back to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah. It connects the previous statement about trusting in Allah with the following description of His power.

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of

steps.n: Changed the أ to ء and added vowels to show who does the action. This word connects to بِنَاصِيَتِهَآ (by its forelock) in the ayah.
The added tanween (ٌۢ) makes this indefinite, showing Allah's general quality as a grasper/holder rather than a specific instance of grasping

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of

steps.n: Basic vowels (fatha) added between root letters to make past tense verb meaning 'he grasped'
Forms active participle pattern showing who does the action

steps.m: forelock (the hair at front of head)
steps.n: Adds ة (taa marboota) at the end to make it a specific noun - the forelock of hair that can be grasped
Adds preposition ب meaning 'by/with'

steps.m: by/with its forelock (referring to every creature mentioned before)
steps.n: Added feminine possessive pronoun هَآ to show ownership, referring back to every creature (دَآبَّةٍ) mentioned in the ayah
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif to show proper pronunciation
Added shadda (ّ) on the ن to add emphasis to the meaning. This particle introduces important statements with certainty, like saying 'Listen up!' before saying something important.
The doubled ب (shown by ّ) emphasizes the complete and constant nature of the nurturing quality

steps.m: my Lord (in relation to اللَّهِ - Allah)
steps.n: Adding ي shows possession ('my'). In this ayah, رَبِّي refers specifically to Allah as the speaker's Lord.
This is a basic preposition showing something is on top of, upon, or following something else. In this ayah, it connects to the word صِرٰطٍ (path) to show being upon a path.
Added an alif mark (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a long vowel sound. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.
Adding vowels to make it a proper noun meaning 'path'
Adding ا makes it the common noun form for 'path' used in the Quran
Changed alif to alif khanjariya (dagger alif) - this is just a writing variation with same meaning
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a path' instead of 'the path')
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added shadda for pronunciation after connecting with previous word
The tanween kasra shows this word describes the path (صِرٰطٍ) mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a possible situation
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This word introduces the condition 'if they turn away' (تَوَلَّوْا۟)
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
The ت at the beginning and doubled ل show doing the action to oneself

steps.m: they turned themselves away (subject of فَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُم)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action. This word is connected to what comes after it (فَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُم - 'then I have conveyed to you')
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the base word قَدْ which shows certainty
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before (if they turn away). The فَ shows this is the result of that condition.
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action more active and direct - like the difference between 'reach' and 'make reach'
The تُ at the end shows that 'I' did this action in the past

steps.m: I delivered to you all (referring to the message mentioned in مَّآ أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِۦٓ)
steps.n: The كُم shows this was delivered to many people. In this ayah, it refers specifically to delivering the message that Allah sent.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it refers to the message that was sent.
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show importance of what is being referred to

steps.m: what/that which (emphasized, referring to the message sent)
steps.n: The alif is lengthened to آ to show a pause in pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.

Changed to passive voice to show the action is being done to someone rather than by someone. The أُ at the start and ِ in the middle are special markers that show this is happening to someone rather than someone doing it.

steps.m: I was sent (as a messenger to you all)
steps.n: The تُ at the end shows this happened to 'me' (the prophet) in the past. This connects to إِلَيْكُمْ (to you all) later in the ayah.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'

steps.m: with it (referring to what was sent in أُرْسِلْتُ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ (it) to show what the preposition applies to. This refers back to the message mentioned in أُرْسِلْتُ (I was sent)
Added pronunciation helpers (ۦٓ) that don't change the meaning
Basic preposition showing direction or destination
When adding pronouns, إِلَى changes to إِلَيْ
Added كُمْ which means 'you all' to show who the action is directed towards
Added vowels to make it 'he' past tense
Added است at the start to show someone making/causing another person to take over or succeed - like appointing a successor
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added و to connect with previous statement. This word shows what 'my Lord' (رَبِّى) will do.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps others grow
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this person does a lot of caring and helping

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow (connected to another word)
steps.n: The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it
Adding ى at the end shows this Lord belongs to me or is my Lord. This Lord is described as حَفِيظٌ (watchful) in this ayah.

steps.m: to stand or rise up together

steps.n: Root meaning 'stand' becomes noun for group that stands together as one people

steps.m: a people (who will be brought as replacement)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this refers to any people, not specific ones. This word is what's being acted upon in the phrase 'He will replace with another people'.

steps.m: to change or be different from

steps.n: Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added كُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'you all' (plural). The َ (fatha) before كُمْ is added to connect the sounds smoothly.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word negates the verb تَضُرُّونَهُ (you harm him) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Added تَ prefix for 'you' and changed vowels to present tense. Middle letter gets shadda due to identical consonants meeting.
Added plural marker ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added هُ suffix to show who receives the action (him)

steps.m: to be a thing or exist

steps.n: Root meaning 'thing' gets vowels to become the noun 'a thing'
Adding tanween fatha (ًا) shows this word is receiving the action of the verb تَضُرُّونَ (you harm). The hamza is written small with a placeholder dot below it.
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle that makes what comes after it more certain
Adding the shadda makes the emphasis stronger. This word is emphasizing 'my Lord' (رَبِّى) in this ayah

steps.m: to care for, nurture, and sustain completely

steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) transforms this into a title emphasizing complete and perfect nurturing
Adding the 'i' sound at the end shows this word is connected to something else, like saying 'Lord of' or 'my Lord'

steps.m: my Lord (the one who nurtures and sustains me)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' Lord, making a personal connection
This is the base form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition showing position or authority over something

steps.m: upon, over (relating to كُلِّ شَىْءٍ - everything)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is the final form. This word connects to كُلِّ شَىْءٍ (everything) in this ayah, showing Allah's authority over all things.
Added damma (ُ) and doubled the ل to show it means absolutely everything with no exceptions
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word is connected to شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, showing it means 'of all things'
The root letters combine to make the common noun 'thing'. Here it is the object being harmed in the phrase.
The dots under the last letter (kasratin) show this word is being acted upon, while also making it mean 'any thing' instead of just 'a thing'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from a past-tense verb (he protected) to a strong form meaning 'intense protector' by adding ي and making it a describing word
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this clause with what came before. This word introduces what happened when something occurred - in this case, when Allah's command came.
Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding the long 'aa' sound in middle

steps.m: he came (subject of أَمْرُنَا) with emphasis
steps.n: The long alif shows this coming was significant or emphatic
Changed from the root meaning 'to command' into the noun 'command' by adding vowel marks - like turning 'to run' into 'a run'
Added damma to show this is the subject (doer) of جَآءَ (came)
Added نَا to show possession. This word is the subject doing the action of 'came' in the phrase 'when our command came'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added shadda to show extra strength in the action, and the weak letter ا changed to ى
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word acts on هُودًا (Hud) in the ayah, showing who was rescued.
This is a proper name referring to the prophet sent to the people of 'Ad
Adding the damma vowel mark to show this is a proper name

steps.m: Prophet Hud (the object being saved in نَجَّيْنَا)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being saved in the phrase 'We saved Hud'
Starting with the base demonstrative word that points to something
Adding ال at the beginning and ي at the end changes it from pointing at something to describing someone or something
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding و at the beginning connects this to the previous phrase. The word is followed by ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) to tell us what these people did.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added a long 'aa' sound at the start (ءَا) to change from 'feel safe' to 'believe/have faith in something'

steps.m: they believed (referring to those who believed with هُودًا)
steps.n: Added وا with small alif (۟) to show multiple people did this action. This refers to the believers who were saved along with Prophet Hud.
This is a common preposition that shows accompaniment. In this ayah, it's showing who was with Hud when he was saved.
The هُ at the end means 'him', referring back to Hud who was mentioned earlier. This shows who was accompanied when Allah saved them.
The small mark ۥ is just a pronunciation guide that doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changes from 'he showed kindness' (action) to 'an act of kindness' (thing) by adding ة at the end and changing middle vowel

steps.m: with an act of kindness (from مِّنَّا - from Us)
steps.n: Adds preposition ب meaning 'with/by'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to
Added نا at the end to show this means 'us'
Added shadda on م because it comes after another word ending in a vowel (وَنَجَّيْنٰهُم) in the ayah
Added shadda to middle root letter to create Form II intensive meaning
Added نَا to show 'we' did the action, with a sukun (ْ) before it to help pronunciation
Adding conjunction prefix و meaning 'and'
Added هُم to show 'them' (the people being saved), with an alif above the ن to help pronunciation
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'. The kasra (ِ) under meem and sukun (ْ) on noon show it's complete.

steps.m: from (connecting عَذَابٍ 'punishment' to what came before)
steps.n: The letter م gets doubled (gets a shadda) because it comes after a word ending in ن, making it blend together smoothly when reading
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to make it a noun meaning the result of punishment
Added tanween kasra because it comes after the preposition مِنْ (from) in the ayah
The pattern makes it an intensive adjective meaning very harsh/severe
The word is made to show it describes 'punishment' by adding the kasra (small diagonal line) at the end
This is the basic demonstrative word used to point to something far away that is feminine. Like saying 'that one over there' when talking about a feminine thing.

steps.m: and that (feminine, pointing to something far)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English. In this ayah, it's connecting to the previous story or example being discussed.

steps.m: to return or go back repeatedly

steps.n: Added alif after first letter to show who does the action. The و (weak letter) changes to ا in this pattern.
Added double damma to make it a proper noun

steps.m: to deny or reject truth despite knowing it is true
he denied/rejected (truth he knew) (subject of عَصَوْا۟)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense. This word is connected to عَصَوْا۟ (they disobeyed) in this ayah, showing how the people of 'Ad both denied and disobeyed.

steps.m: they denied/rejected (truth they knew) (subject of عَصَوْا۟)
steps.n: Changed to plural by adding وا to show many people (the people of 'Ad) did this action together

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: The root letters combine to form a noun with a long 'aa' sound (آ) at the start, making it mean 'a sign' instead of 'to show'
Changes the singular to plural by replacing ة with ات
Adds the preposition ب meaning 'with/by' to show how something was done

steps.m: with/by the signs (of رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
steps.n: Adds kasra at the end to show connection to 'their Lord' (رَبِّهِمْ) that follows it in the ayah

steps.m: to care for, nurture, and help grow
one who cares for and looks after others
steps.n: Root letters combined with fatha and doubled middle letter to make it mean 'the one who nurtures'
The kasra (small diagonal line) under ب connects this word to 'their' (هم) to mean 'their Lord'
Added هِمْ at the end to show it belongs to them
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added وْا to show multiple people did the action. The ى changes to و because it's followed by وا.

steps.m: and they disobeyed (connected to جَحَدُوا۟ - they denied)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this action with the previous action (they denied). The small mark ۟ shows the end of a word group.
Root 'send' becomes plural noun 'messengers' by adding vowel marks (like u-sounds) between each letter
Added fatha at the end to show this is being acted upon by the verb عصوا (they disobeyed)
Added هُ at the end to show these are his messengers. The small و (ۥ) is just a pronunciation helper.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Added ا at start and doubled the ت (with shadda) to show following something very closely and with purpose
Adding conjunction prefix

steps.m: and they followed closely/deliberately (subject of أَمْرَ - followed the command)
steps.n: Changed from 'he followed' to 'they followed' - multiple people following the command (أَمْرَ)
This root can mean both commanding and affairs/matters
Changed from a verb root meaning 'to command' into a noun meaning 'command' by adding vowel marks
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon - they followed the command/matter

steps.m: to be complete or surround entirely

steps.n: Added damma and shadda to create a word meaning totality. This word describes جَبَّارٍ (tyrant) in this ayah, showing it refers to 'every tyrant'.
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the sentence

steps.m: to force, compel, or make something happen against its will

steps.n: Added shadda (doubling) on ب and alif after it to show someone who does this action intensively
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this describes 'every/all' (كُلِّ) in the ayah, meaning 'every tyrant'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into an adjective describing someone who habitually turns away stubbornly. This word describes جَبَّارٍ (tyrant) in the ayah.

steps.m: stubbornly resistant (describing جَبَّارٍ - tyrant, in a possessive/descriptive phrase)
steps.n: Adds the genitive case ending because this word is part of a descriptive phrase كُلِّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ (every stubborn tyrant)
Adding the vowels 'fatha' and 'kasra' turns the root into 'he followed' in the past tense
Changed to passive form to show someone is being made to follow rather than following by themselves
Added وا to show it's talking about a group of people

steps.m: and they were made to follow
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement
This is a preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it connects to هٰذِهِ (this) to show 'in this'
Changed the final ي to ى which is a common writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning
Basic pointing word used to indicate something nearby
Added ذا to make it specifically point to something near
Changed the ending to هِ with a kasra (ِ) before it to make it feminine, pointing to something that is grammatically feminine
Forms the feminine adjective meaning 'nearest' which became used specifically for this worldly life since it's the nearest life compared to the afterlife
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific worldly life rather than any near thing
