steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Added اِتَّ at the beginning to show doing the action to oneself. The و from the root combines with ت making a stronger ت sound (shown by ّ)
Changed to a command for a group by adding وا at the end - like saying 'all of you protect yourselves!'

steps.m: so protect yourselves! (command to group in response to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a response or result of what came before. The ا changes to ٱ after فَ.
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this the unique name for The One God
Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object of the command 'fear/revere' (فَٱتَّقُوا۟) in this verse
Basic negative particle that negates the verb تُخْزُونِ (humiliate me) that comes after it

steps.m: and do not (negates تُخْزُونِ - humiliate me)
steps.n: Adding و means 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Added تُ prefix for 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The ي drops because it's a weak letter meeting ون
Added ِ at the end to show 'me' as the object of the verb
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it connects to 'my guests' to mean 'regarding my guests'
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the noun form
Adds kasra to make the noun genitive case
Adds first person possessive pronoun suffix. In this ayah, Prophet Lut is speaking to his people about his guests.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a usable word. The fatha and sukun pattern makes it work like a past tense verb, but it actually talks about the present.

steps.m: is there not? (asking about رَجُلٌ رَّشِيدٌ - a right-minded man)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question that expects the answer 'yes'. In this ayah, it's asking if there isn't a single right-minded person among them.
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Adding كُمْ shows this applies to multiple people being spoken to
The vowels change it from a verb into a noun meaning 'man'
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite (like 'a' in English) and is the subject being described as رَّشِيدٌ (right-minded)
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Pattern فعيل creates an adjective meaning 'one who has the quality of being rightly guided'
Adding tanween (double damma) makes the word indefinite, meaning 'a' wise one rather than 'the' wise one
Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The و (waw) changes to ا (alif) because it's a hollow verb. This word is connected to what comes next in the ayah - they're saying something to someone.
Added وا at the end to show many people (all male or mixed group) did the action. This shows it was a group speaking to someone.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced. قَدْ is a particle that shows something definitely happened.
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis. This word connects to عَلِمْتَ (you knew) in this ayah, emphasizing that they definitely knew something.
Adding the vowels 'a' and 'i' turns the root letters into 'he knew'

steps.m: you knew/understood (addressing someone in the ayah)
steps.n: The تَ at the end shows this is addressing someone directly. In this ayah, the people are speaking to Prophet Lut, telling him 'you already knew'.

steps.m: that which (object of لَتَعْلَمُ - you surely know)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that connects to the verb before it (لَتَعْلَمُ) to show what is being known. In this context, it means 'that which' rather than 'what?' because it's making a statement, not asking a question.
Starting with the basic preposition particle لَ which shows direction or purpose
Added نَا at the end to show this is happening to 'us'. In this ayah, the people are saying they have no right or interest in something.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. It connects بَنَاتِكَ (your daughters) to the rest of the sentence.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to build or create something from parts

steps.n: The root ب-ن-ي becomes بَنَا (banaa) meaning 'he built/constructed'. This is a past tense verb showing someone built something. The word for 'daughter' comes from a completely different root.
Adding ات to the end makes this word mean multiple daughters instead of just one
Added kasra because the word comes after في (in)
Added كَ to show these are your daughters
Basic preposition showing source/origin or type - not derived from a root. Here it connects to حَقٍّ (right/claim) to show what type of thing they don't have.
Adding the basic past tense vowel mark
Changed from the root 'to be true' into a noun meaning 'right/claim' with tanwin kasra to show it receives the action from مِنْ (from)
Starting with the emphatic particle إِنَّ which adds strong emphasis to what comes after it

steps.m: indeed you (as the subject of تَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is about 'you'. This word connects to تَعْلَمُ (you know) later in the ayah.

steps.m: and indeed you (as the subject of تَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at start to show present tense and 'you' as subject. Changed vowels to show present tense pattern.
Added ل at start to emphasize the statement, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'
The letter 'lam' at start means 'indeed/surely' + 'you know' (present tense)
In this ayah, مَا is being used as a question word meaning 'what'. It is the object of the verb عَلِمْتَ (you knew), showing what was known. The full phrase مَا لَنَا means 'what (right) we have'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added نُ at the start to show 'we' are doing the action. The و changes to ي because it's between two vowels. The ُ at the end shows it's happening now.
Added fatha vowels to make it past tense. This shows the action happened in the past.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: if only (expressing a wish that didn't happen)
steps.n: This word introduces Lut's wish for strength against his enemies, which he didn't have. It connects to قَالَ (he said) to show what Lut wished for.

steps.m: that/indeed (emphasizing لَوْ أَنَّ - if only that)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects and emphasizes what comes after it. Here it combines with لَوْ (if) to express a strong wish - 'if only that'
This shows ownership or who something is for

steps.m: for me (showing I wish I had قُوَّةً - strength)
steps.n: The ي at the end shows this is about 'me'. In this ayah, the speaker (Prophet Lut) is wishing he had strength, so this word connects 'strength' to himself.

steps.m: with/by/through (preposition showing how Lut wishes he had strength)
steps.n: This shows how something is done or what's used

steps.m: with you all (referring to the group Lut is addressing)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, Prophet Lut is expressing his wish for strength to deal with the group he's speaking to.
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning power
Added ة to make it into a specific instance of power

steps.m: a power/strength (that Lut wished he had)
steps.n: Added -an ending to show this is a strength/power that is being talked about directly (as an object)

This is a connecting word that shows Prophet Lut is wishing for either strength (قُوَّةً) or a strong support (رُكْنٍ) to help him against his people. It's like saying 'I wish I had strength OR could find refuge.'
Adding آ at start changes meaning from 'seek shelter' to 'give shelter'

steps.m: I shelter/retreat (connected to قَالَ - he said)
steps.n: Added elongation mark (ـٓ) after ى which helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning
Basic form of the word that shows direction or movement towards something
Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end to show this is connected to what comes after it (رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ - meaning 'strong support/refuge'). This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to support or rely on something

steps.n: Changed from a basic verb meaning 'to support' into a noun meaning 'a support/strong place' by adding vowel marks
Added tanwin to show this is any support, not a specific one. In this ayah, Prophet Lut is wishing he had some strong support against his enemies.
Changes the root 'strong' into a word describing something that is strong right now
Changed from a verb form to an adjective by adding ي, making it mean 'very strong' instead of just 'strong'
Added tanwin kasra to show this describes رُكْنٍ (support/power) in the phrase 'to a strong support'
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to common past tense form with alif
Added وا to show multiple people (the angels) are speaking to Prophet Lut
Added damma marks to show this is the subject form of the name

steps.m: O Lot! (being called by the angels)
steps.n: Added يٰ at the beginning to show someone is calling out to Lot. The angels are calling to him in this verse.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
Added ا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. In this ayah, this refers to the messengers (رُسُلُ) who are speaking to Prophet Lut.
Forms the singular noun for someone who carries messages
Changes the singular 'messenger' to plural form. This word is the subject of يَصِلُوٓا۟ (they will reach) in this ayah.

steps.m: to care for and help grow
one who cares for and helps others grow (possessed)
steps.n: The root means 'to nurture/care for', and when changed to رَبِّ it means 'my Lord' - the ِ (kasra) at the end shows I'm talking about my Lord

steps.m: your caring lord (possessed by رُسُلُ - messengers)
steps.n: Adding كَ shows this belongs to you (when speaking to one man). This word is owned by the word رُسُلُ (messengers) that came before it, showing these are 'messengers of your lord'
This is a special word that makes a future action impossible. Here it negates the verb يَصِلُوٓا۟ (they will reach), showing that they will absolutely not be able to reach Lut.

steps.m: to reach or arrive at something

steps.n: Added يَ at start to show present tense. The و drops because it's weak. Added ِ between ص and ل.
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show it's in the nominative case
Added و to make it plural - means multiple people are doing the action
Added ا to complete the plural ending وا - shows multiple people doing the action
This is a basic preposition showing direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي with sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added the pronoun كَ which means 'you' to show who is being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he traveled' to 'you travel!' by adding أ and changing the vowels to make it a command to one person
Added فَ at beginning to show this command follows from what was said before
Root letters got vowel marks (َ and ْ) to make the common noun meaning 'family'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the preposition بِ
Added ك at the end to show it's your family
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with'
Added kasra and sukun to make it mean 'a piece' - something that results from cutting
Added tanween kasratayn to show this is indefinite - like saying 'a piece' instead of 'the piece'

steps.m: with a piece (of ٱلَّيْلِ - the night)
steps.n: Added the preposition bi (بِ) to show this piece is being used as an instrument or means - connecting to 'of the night' that follows it
This is a preposition meaning 'from' or 'of' that connects words to show where something comes from or what it's made of
Added a shadda and kasra to emphasize the م. This happens because the word comes after another similar sound.
Added a fatha at the end to connect this word to the next word الَّيْلِ (the night), showing it means 'from the night'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make the basic word for 'night'
Added ٱل with shadda to make it specific. The shadda appears because the ل of ٱل combines with the first ل of the word.

steps.m: of the night (part of قِطْعٍ مِّنَ - a portion of)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word follows قِطْعٍ مِّنَ (a portion of) in the ayah
This is a negative particle that prevents something from happening. Here it's used with يَلْتَفِتْ to say 'do not look back'
The و at the start connects this command with the previous command (فَأَسْرِ - travel by night). So it's saying 'travel by night AND do not look back'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Form VIII makes it reflexive - doing action to oneself

steps.m: he turns/looks back (after وَلَا meaning 'and do not')
steps.n: Changed to present tense and added sukun at end because it comes after لَا (do not). This makes it a negative command - 'do not look back'
This is a preposition that shows something coming from somewhere or being part of something

steps.m: from you all (referring to Lut's family)
steps.n: The كُمْ at the end means 'you all' when talking to a group. In this ayah, it refers to Lut's family who are being told not to look back.
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make it a word meaning 'one'

steps.m: anyone (in the context of 'not anyone from you')
steps.n: The tanween (double damma) makes it indefinite, meaning 'any one person'. In this ayah, it's used with لا to say 'not anyone from you should look back'
This is a basic exception particle in Arabic. It's not derived from a root but is a fundamental word used to show exceptions.
Adding shadda emphasizes the word's meaning of exception
Adding alif at the end completes the word. In this ayah, it's making an exception for Lut's wife from those who should leave with him.
Added hamza with kasra at start and sukun on middle letter to form basic noun pattern
Added feminine marker ة to specify a single woman
ة changes to ت when connecting to another word
The word 'wife' got 'your' added to it (كَ at the end) and starts with a special 'joining' hamza (ٱ). It's being used after 'except' in this sentence.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what follows more certain and adds stress to the statement

steps.m: indeed it (referring to what will strike مُصِيبُهَا)
steps.n: Added هُ which means 'it/he' to show what is being emphasized. The ۥ mark shows the هُ is pronounced longer.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense pattern. The و changes to ا in this form.
Added مُ at start and changed middle vowel to ِ to show who/what is doing the action. The ا changes to ي in this pattern.

steps.m: what will reach/affect her (predicate of إِنَّ)
steps.n: Added ُ to show it's the predicate (khabar) of إِنَّ, and هَا meaning 'her/it'. In this ayah, it refers to the punishment that will affect the wife of Prophet Lut.
This is a basic particle word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to refer to things or connect parts of sentences.
The fatha mark doesn't change the meaning - ما still means 'what'. The fatha just shows how to pronounce it correctly as 'ma'
Changed regular alif to elongated alif (آ). This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
Added basic past tense vowels. The middle weak letter و changes to ا in this form.
Added hamza with fatha at beginning to show causing something to happen

steps.m: it struck/afflicted them (referring to ما - what will strike them)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُمْ to show who is being struck. This word relates to ما earlier in the verse which refers to the punishment that will strike them.
This is a basic particle that adds emphasis to statements. It doesn't come from a root.
The kasra (إِ) and shadda (نَّ) add emphasis to show this is a strong statement. In this ayah, it emphasizes that something will definitely happen.

steps.m: to promise or set an appointment

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning and changed vowels to make a word that means where or when something happens

steps.m: place or time of appointment (object form)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action
Added هُمُ at the end to show it belongs to them. This word is the object of إِنَّ (indeed) in this ayah.

steps.m: to shine, to begin (like morning light)

steps.n: Adding the vowels ُ and ْ changes this from an action into a thing - from 'to shine' into the time when light first appears

1. Adding ال makes it specific - 'the dawn' not just any dawn
2. The ل merges into ص making it stronger (with ّ)
3. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence
4. This word is the subject of أَلَيْسَ (is not) in the question 'Is not the dawn near?'
Connected the letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This is a special verb that means 'is not' in the present tense even though it looks like past tense.

steps.m: is it not? (asking about الصُّبْحُ - the morning)
steps.n: The prefix 'أَ' turns this into a question. In this ayah, it's asking about الصُّبْحُ (the morning) mentioned right before it.
Added vowel marks to turn the concept into a noun for the morning itself
Added ال (with proper spelling as ٱل) and shadda on ص because it comes after ال
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of مَوْعِدَهُمُ (their appointed time)
Root 'to be near' changed to describe 'near' as a quality, with tanwin showing it's any near thing/person
Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how close dawn is. This word relates to الصُّبْحُ (dawn) in the ayah.
This is a word that shows when something happens. Here it connects to the word جَآءَ (came) to show 'when something came'
The فَ (fa) adds the meaning of 'then' or 'and so', showing sequence or result
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) to make it mean 'he came' in the past
Changed to hollow verb pattern with alif, a common change for verbs with weak middle letters
Changed verb concept into a noun by adding fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added damma to show this is the doer of the action in the sentence
Added نا at the end to show it belongs to us. This word is doing the action of 'coming' in the verse.

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it 'he made' in the past tense
Changed the last vowel to a sukun (ْ) to prepare for adding the next part

steps.m: we made (عٰلِيَهَا سَافِلَهَا - its top its bottom)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. In this ayah, Allah is saying what He made the top of the city into its bottom.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show past tense. The و changes to ا at the end of words.
Changed to active participle form to describe something as being high

steps.m: its high part (object of جَعَلْنَا/we made)
steps.n: Added هَا to show this is the high part of something feminine. This word is what was acted upon by جَعَلْنَا (we made).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to active participle pattern فَاعِل to show the thing that is low/down. This word is what was turned upside down by جَعَلْنَا (we made).
Added هَا to show this is the bottom part of something feminine mentioned before. This is what Allah made/turned in the phrase 'We made its top its bottom'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added hamza with fatha at beginning and sukun on first root letter to show causative meaning

steps.m: and he caused to rain down
steps.n: Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this action with what came before

steps.m: and we caused to rain down (stones)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' as the subject. This verb acts on حِجَارَةً (stones) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. على is a preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else.
Changed the ending to prepare for adding the pronoun. The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns.

steps.m: upon it/her (referring to the city that was turned upside down)
steps.n: Added ها ('ha') at the end to mean 'upon it/her' - ها shows we're talking about something feminine like a city
Basic Form I past tense verb formation from root letters
Root letters connected with vowels (ِ and َ) to make the word for 'stone'
Adding ا makes this the plural form for 'stones'
Added ة at the end to make it mean 'a collection/group of stones' instead of just 'stones'

steps.m: stones (as the object being rained down)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object being rained down in the phrase 'وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهَا حِجَارَةً' (and We rained upon them stones)
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
The shadda (ّ) makes the 'م' sound stronger, which adds emphasis to show this is definitely or specifically from something
The doubled ج and added ي show this is something done with great force or intensity
Added the -in sound at the end (shown by two dots under) because it comes after the word 'from' (مِّن) in the sentence

steps.m: to stack or arrange in layers

steps.n: Shows something that has been stacked or arranged, like saying 'the stacked books' instead of just 'stack'
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound
Added tanween kasra to show this describes something general

steps.m: to mark or brand with a distinctive sign

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show intensive marking
Added مُ at the beginning to show this is something being marked, not doing the marking
Added ة to make it feminine
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after a silent letter, and the tanween ending (-an) shows it receives the action
This root carries the basic meaning of being present or near something

Adding the vowel marks changes this from a basic concept into a preposition showing location or proximity. In this ayah, it shows where the marked stones (مُّسَوَّمَةً) are - they are with/at/near something.
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to show complete mastery and authority

steps.m: your Lord (in relation to عِندَ meaning 'with/at')
steps.n: The word 'Lord' becomes 'your Lord' by adding كَ at the end. It shows who the Lord belongs to (you). The word pairs with عِندَ (with/at) in the sentence.
Starting with the base particle مَا which asks about or refers to things in a general way, like 'what' in English

steps.m: and what (connects to مُّسَوَّمَةً before it)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the word مُّسَوَّمَةً that comes before it
This is a basic feminine pronoun used to say 'she' or 'it' when talking about feminine things. It's like saying 'she' in English.
Added a fatha (َ) at the end to show this is being used as a subject in the sentence. In this ayah, it's saying that 'it' (referring to the punishment) is not far from the wrongdoers.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or showing a part of something
Added َ at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الظَّالِمِينَ (the wrongdoers). This combination means 'from the wrongdoers'

steps.m: to wrong, to be unjust, to oppress

steps.n: Active participle form shows the one who does the action
Adding ال causes the first letter to get a shadda due to assimilation
Adding ين makes it plural (more than one wrongdoer). The ين ending also shows it connects to بِبَعِيدٍ (not far from)
Added ي between second and third letters to show something has this quality strongly
Added بِ at the beginning to show connection or relation to something

steps.m: far from (something) (in the ayah: not far from the wrongdoers)
steps.n: Added the two dots under (tanween) to show this is describing something as 'not far' - like saying 'it's not a far thing'
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or destination
Added alif maksura (ى with two dots) at the end to show this is the end of the word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This word connects to 'Madyan' which comes after it in the ayah.
This is a proper noun naming a specific place
Basic root letters م-د-ن got vowel marks to make a simple noun meaning settlement
The و at the end is part of the root but acts special in this word
The root letters for 'brotherhood' are joined with vowel marks to make the word 'brother'
Added ا at the end to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is their brother
This is a proper noun formed from the root letters, used as the name of a prophet mentioned in the Quran

steps.m: Shuʿayb (as the object of أَرْسَلْنَا - we sent)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this name is receiving the action of being sent. It connects to the verb أَرْسَلْنَا (we sent) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is about something that happened in the past

steps.m: he said (introducing what شُعَيْب says next)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it has a fatha before and after it. This is a common change in words with و in the middle to make them easier to say.

steps.m: to stand together, gather, rise up
steps.n: Basic root meaning
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands/rises together'
Added يٰ to make it a call or address to people
Added ِ at the end to show possession - these are my people
Basic root meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he worshipped' to a command by adding 'u' at the start and making the middle and end quiet (with sukun)
Changed from commanding one person to commanding many people by adding وا at the end
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
This is the unique name for the One God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ه at the end makes it a proper name. The أ changed to ٱ at the beginning.
This is a negative particle that means 'not' or 'no'. In this ayah, it's used with لكم to say 'you do not have'.
Added fatha mark to show proper pronunciation. This word combines with لكم (for you) to mean 'you do not have'.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel sound of the preposition
Added ك with damma to show it refers to multiple people being addressed
Added م to complete the plural 'you' pronoun. This shows the word is addressing multiple people.
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it belongs to
Added shadda (doubling mark) on م because it comes after a word ending in a similar sound
Added sukun (rest mark) on final letter to show where the word stops

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to
steps.n: Basic root meaning
Changed أ to إِ and added ا after ل to form the noun meaning 'god' or 'deity'
Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to show this is indefinite and is the object of the word مِنْ (from) in the phrase مِنْ إِلٰهٍ (from any god)
Basic root meaning
Changed from basic root meaning 'change/differ' to noun meaning 'other than', with ُ ending showing it's doing something in the sentence
Added هُۥ at the end to mean 'Him'
Starting with the basic negation particle that stops something from happening
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous command to worship. The full meaning is 'worship Allah and do not...'
Basic root meaning
Adding basic past tense vowel pattern to root letters
Added تَ at start to change from 'he' to 'you', and changed middle vowel to ُ (u) for present tense
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: to measure or weigh out (especially grains)
steps.n: Basic root meaning
Root letters connected with vowels to make a simple noun meaning 'measuring'
Added مِ at start and ا before final letter to create noun for tool/instrument

steps.m: the measuring device (object of تَنقُصُوا - do not decrease)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it definite and َ at end because it's the object of the verb تَنقُصُوا (do not decrease)
Basic root meaning
Added مِي at beginning and ا after second root letter to create a noun for the tool used for weighing
Added ٱلْ to specify this particular scale/balance
Added وَ to connect this with the previous word ٱلْمِكْيَالَ (the measure)

steps.m: and the measuring scale/balance (object of تَنقُصُوا)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb تَنقُصُوا (do not decrease)
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to statements

steps.m: indeed I (subject of أَخَافُ - I fear)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show this is about 'me/I'. The نّ is doubled (with shadda) when connecting to the pronoun suffix.

steps.m: indeed I (subject of أَخَافُ - I fear)
steps.n: The final ي is written as ىٓ with an alif to show lengthening of the sound
Basic root meaning
Added fatha marks to show past tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
Adding أ at start changes meaning from 'see' to 'show/make see'
Added alif superscript (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound clearly
Added كُم at the end to show who is being seen (you all)
Basic root meaning
Root letters get vowels to make the noun meaning 'goodness'
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'in' and added tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this describes how the prophet sees the people. This connects to the verb أَرَىٰ (I see) in the ayah.
Starting with the emphasis word إِنَّ which adds strength to what comes after it
Added ى at the end to show this is about me (I/my)
Added the small alif (ٓ) above ى to show it's pronounced longer

steps.m: and indeed I (connected to أَخَافُ - I fear)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what comes before it. This word connects to أَخَافُ (I fear) which comes right after it in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
The أَ prefix shows this is first person (I) and the ُ ending shows it's present tense
Basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
When adding a pronoun after على, the ى changes to يْ to connect with the pronoun
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to transform the verb into a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is what is being feared in the phrase 'I fear for you'
Connected the letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun

steps.m: a day (object of عَذَابَ - torment of)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (any day) and is possessed by something (the torment). This word is connected to عَذَابَ (torment) in the phrase 'torment of a day'.
Basic root meaning
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to show who does the surrounding (like 'surrounder'). The middle letter changed from و to ي because of how Arabic handles certain letter combinations.
The shadda on م makes the meaning stronger, showing complete encompassing

steps.m: all-encompassing (describing عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ/punishment of a day)
steps.n: The tanwin kasra shows this word describes عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ (punishment of a day) in the ayah

steps.m: to stand together, gather, rise up

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters. The meaning shifted from the action of standing together to refer to the group that does this.
Added يٰ to make it a call or address to people
Added وَ (and) at start and ِ at end to show possession (my)

steps.m: to fulfill completely or give full measure

steps.n: Added vowels to make basic past tense form. The final ى replaces ي because it's at the end of the word.
Added أ at beginning to show doing something more completely or to its fullest extent
Changed to command form by dropping final ى and changing middle vowel to kasra
Added وا to make it a command to multiple people. The small circle (۟) shows this is a group command.
Root letters got vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'measuring' instead of the action 'to measure'
Added مِ at start to show this is a tool, and ا after ي to show it's for repeated use

steps.m: the measuring vessel (object of أَوْفُوا - fulfill/give in full)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to point to a specific measuring vessel, and َ at the end because it's receiving the action of أَوْفُوا (give in full)
Added مِ at the beginning and changed the middle letter to ي to make it mean 'a scale/balance used for weighing'
Added ا after second root letter to make the word complete
Added ٱلْ to point to a specific scale
Added وَ to connect it to the previous word, and َ at the end because it's receiving the action of أَوْفُوا (fulfill/give in full)

steps.m: to act justly, to be fair

steps.n: Root letters turned into a noun meaning 'justice' by adding the vowel marks kasra (i) and sukoon
Added definite article 'the' (ال) to make the noun definite
Added بِ at the beginning to show how the action is done. This connects to the verb أَوْفُوا (fulfill) to show how to fulfill the measure - with justice.
Added kasra (small diagonal line) at the end because it comes after the word bi (with/by)
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not' or 'do not' when used with commands

steps.m: and not/and do not (connects to تَبْخَسُوا۟)
steps.n: Added the connecting particle وَ (and) to connect this prohibition with the previous one in the ayah

steps.m: to reduce or decrease something unfairly

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Changed to present tense and second person by adding تَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: you all reduce unfairly (command not to do this)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to make it plural (talking to many people) and a command not to do this action
Added basic vowels to connect the letters together

steps.m: people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: Changed from 'he was social' to mean 'people' - those who socialize and live together in society
Added ال to point to specific people, and the ن gets a shadda because it comes after ال
Added fatha at the end because 'the people' is the object (receiving the action) of depriving
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'a single thing' - fatha on ش, sukoon on ي, and hamza at end
Forms plural using the pattern أَفْعَال to show multiple things
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb تَبْخَسُوا (do not deprive/diminish)

steps.m: their things (as object of تَبْخَسُوا - do not deprive people of)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show these things belong to them (the people mentioned)
This is the basic negation word used to say 'no' or 'do not' in Arabic. It's used here to tell people not to do something.
Added و at the beginning to connect this command with the previous one. This links 'do not defraud people' with what comes after it.
Changed from basic root letters to 'he' past tense by adding short 'a' sounds (fathas) and alif at end to mean 'he acted badly'
Added ت at start for 'you all' and و at end to show plural, changed vowels to make it present tense
Added ا at end to show this is for multiple people
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects with الْأَرْضِ to show where something happens - 'in the earth'.
Added marks (fatha and sukun) to make it readable as 'ard' meaning earth
Added ال (the) at the beginning and kasra at the end because it follows the word في (in/on)

steps.m: someone who spoils things (subject of تَعْثَوْا)
steps.n: Added مُ at the start and ِ in the middle to show who does the action. This word describes how people are acting in the verb تَعْثَوْا (act wickedly).

steps.m: people who spoil things (describes how they act)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people. This describes how the people are acting - they are acting as spoilers.

steps.m: to remain or be left over

steps.n: Added vowels to make basic past tense form
Added shadda and feminine ending to create noun meaning 'what remains'
Added damma at end to show this is the subject

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل with shadda to show this is the one true God. The ِ at the end shows this belongs to or connects with something else in the sentence.
Added vowels to the root letters to make it mean 'a good thing' instead of 'to be good'
Added double damma to show this is the subject doing the action. In this ayah, it describes بَقِيَّتُ (what remains)
Basic combination of 'for' (لَ) and 'you all' (كُمْ)
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show certainty or importance

This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition - it connects what comes before it to what comes after it. In this case, it connects 'Allah's lasting provision is better for you' with 'if you are believers'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he was' to a command 'be!' by dropping the middle letter و and changing vowels. Like telling someone 'Be quiet!'
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past
Added م to make it refer to multiple people. This word is connected to مُّؤْمِنِينَ (believers) in the ayah, showing 'if you all were believers'

steps.m: one who believes (someone who has faith)
steps.n: Added مُ at the start and vowel marks to show this is someone who does the action of believing
The doubled م (shown by ّ) makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: multiple people who truly believe (if you were talking about them)
steps.n: Added ين to show we're talking about many believers, not just one

steps.m: multiple people who truly believe (object of إِن كُنتُم - if you were)
steps.n: Added َ (fatha) at the end because this word is the object of 'if you were' - showing who you were
Added vowel mark to show pronunciation
Extended the alif to show emphasis in pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is the basic form of the word that means 'I' or 'me'. It starts with أ to point to yourself.
Adding ا at the end makes it specifically mean 'I' or 'me'
The small mark (۠) is just to help with pronunciation. This word is the subject of عَلَيْكُم (over you) in this ayah.
The root letters combine to form the preposition meaning 'upon' or 'over'. The final و changes to ى which is common in Arabic.
The ى changes to يْ before adding a suffix - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

steps.m: upon you all, over you all
steps.n: Adding كُم at the end shows this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
Changed the middle vowel and added ي to show this describes someone who does this action frequently

steps.m: as a guardian/protector (of عَلَيْكُم - over you)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to mean 'as' and ٍ at end to show it's being used with another word. This is connected to عَلَيْكُم (over you) in the ayah.
Adding short vowels 'a' after each letter to make 'he said' in past tense
Changed middle letter و to ا and added وا at the end to make it 'they said' instead of 'he said'
Added the ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the main subject being addressed
Added يٰ at the beginning to show we're calling out to Shuaib. This is like saying 'Hey Shuaib!' or 'O Shuaib!' in English
Forms the noun meaning 'ritual prayer' from the root
Changes from singular to plural by adding ات and bringing back the original و that was hidden in صلاة
Adds interrogative hamza to form a question
Adds damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Adds 'ka' (كَ) at the end to make it 'your prayer', and has a long 'aa' sound (ٰ) in the middle. Links to 'commands you' later in the verse.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he ordered' to 'you order' by adding ت at start and changing vowels to show present tense
Added ك suffix to show 'you' as the object being ordered. In this ayah, 'your prayer' (صَلَوٰتُكَ) is doing the ordering.
Basic particle that introduces what someone wants, thinks, or says. Here it connects to the verb نَّتْرُكَ (we leave/abandon) to show what their prayers are commanding.
Added fatha mark (َ) on أ to show correct pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to make it present tense - sukun on first letter, damma on second letter
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added shadda on ن because it comes after the word أَن in the ayah

steps.m: what (that which يَعْبُدُ our fathers worship)
steps.n: This is a basic word that can mean 'what' or 'that which' depending on context. Here it connects to يَعْبُدُ (worship) to mean 'that which our fathers worship'

steps.m: what (that which يَعْبُدُ our fathers worship)
steps.n: Added fatha mark on م to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, damma on second root letter, and damma at end

steps.m: to nurture/care for as a father

steps.n: Root letters أ-ب-و combined to make the simple word 'father'
Changed to broken plural form for multiple fathers/ancestors
Changed alif to alif madd for proper pronunciation
The ُ (damma) shows this word is doing the action in the sentence
Added first person plural possessive suffix to show ownership. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَعْبُدُ (worships) in this ayah.

This is a connecting word that shows there are different choices or possibilities. In this verse, it connects two things that the people are questioning about Shu'ayb's prayer - whether it commands them to leave their gods OR do something else with their wealth.
Added a sukun (ْ) on the و to show where to stop when pronouncing the word.
This is a connecting word that introduces what is being commanded. In this ayah, it connects تَأْمُرُكَ (commands you) with what is being commanded (نَّتْرُكَ - that we leave).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ن at start to show 'we' are doing it, and changed vowels to show it's happening now instead of in the past
Added shadda on ن because it comes after the word أَن (that) in the ayah
Basic preposition showing location or position. Here it connects what we do (نَّفْعَلَ) with where we do it (أَمْوٰلِنَا/our wealth)
Added elongation mark (ٓ) after ى to show proper Quranic spelling. This doesn't change the meaning, just helps with proper recitation.
Changes the word to mean 'multiple properties/wealth' by adding أ at the start and returning the original و. This is a common way to make plurals in Arabic.
Adds -i ending to show this wealth belongs to someone
Adds first person plural possessive pronoun

steps.m: what (referring to what يَعْبُدُ their fathers worshipped)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb يَعْبُدُ (worship) to ask about what their fathers used to worship. In the context of the ayah, the people are questioning Shuaib about leaving what their fathers worshipped.

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: The root letters connect with vowels. The middle letter ي turns into ا to make it easier to say. This makes it mean 'he wanted' instead of just 'want'
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed the ending vowel to ُ to show it's happening now

steps.m: we want/wish (in response to what they said about their wealth)
steps.n: Changed spelling to show the long ا with ٰ and added hamza above the و. The small circle at the end (۟) helps with pronunciation. These changes don't affect the meaning.
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphasis word 'indeed'
Added كَ at the end to show this is addressing 'you'. This word emphasizes the statement that follows (لَأَنتَ - 'you are')
This is the basic independent pronoun meaning 'you' (masculine singular). When used alone like this, it also carries the meaning of 'are' (like 'am/is/are' in English)

The لَ (called lām al-tawkīd) is added at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed' in English. This extra emphasis shows the speakers are very certain about what they're saying about Shu'ayb.

steps.m: one who is very patient and gentle
steps.n: Added ي between second and third letters and vowels to show this describes someone who strongly has this quality

steps.m: the one who is very patient and gentle
steps.n: ٱلْ makes the noun definite ('the')

steps.m: the one who is very patient and gentle (describing شُعَيْبُ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is describing Shu'ayb in this verse

steps.m: to guide in the right way

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'guide' to a special form with ي that means someone who has perfect wisdom
Added ال to show this is referring to Allah specifically, and ُ to show this is describing Allah directly
Added vowel marks (َ) to the root letters to make it mean 'he said' in the past tense
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Adds 'O!' at start to call out to someone
Adds 'my' meaning by adding kasra at end
Root 'to see' became past tense 'he saw' by adding the vowel marks fatHa
Changed from 'he saw' to 'I saw' by adding تُ at the end and changing the last letter ى to ي
Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by adding مْ (meem) at the end
This is a basic word that shows something depends on something else, like saying 'if I study, I will learn' in English. Here it connects with كُنتُ (I was) to show what depends on what.
Added the kasra mark (ِ) under the إ to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'in'
The middle weak letter و drops out, and the vowels change to match the pattern for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)

steps.m: I was (in response to أَرَءَيْتُمْ - 'do you see')
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action in the past. This word is part of the prophet's response to his people's question.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. It's not derived from a root.
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly - 'ala
Added the small alif (ٰ) to show that the final ى is pronounced as a long 'aa' sound. In this ayah, it connects to the word بَيِّنَةٍ (clear evidence) to show what someone is 'upon'.
The doubled ي with shadda makes the meaning stronger
Adding ة turns this into a noun meaning 'clear proof'

steps.m: a clear proof (connected to مِّن رَّبِّى - from my Lord)
steps.n: The -in ending shows this is 'a' clear proof (not 'the' clear proof) and links to 'from my Lord' after it

steps.m: from (connecting رَّبِّى to what comes before)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something comes from or originates

steps.m: from (connecting رَّبِّى to what comes before)
steps.n: Added shadda (doubling mark) on م because it comes before another word starting with ر. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.

steps.m: to nurture and care for completely
steps.n: Base root meaning nurturing or caring
Doubling the ب shows complete/perfect nurturing ability
Added shadda and kasra to show this is 'my lord' or 'the lord of' something
Added ى to show this is my Lord
Basic meaning of providing
Added و (meaning 'and') at the start to connect with previous part, turned basic verb into past tense 'he provided'
Added نِ to show the action was done to 'me'
Added ى to complete the 'me' suffix
Added sukun to show where the word pauses before connecting to the next part
Added هُ (him/it) to show who/what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to what was forbidden (ما أنهاكم)
Changed from action to thing by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added tanween (ًا) to show this is what was given. This word describes what Allah provided in رَزَقَنِى (He provided me).
Root meaning goodness or beauty
Adding vowels creates a simple adjective meaning 'good'

steps.m: good/beautiful (as a description of رِزْقًا - provision)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is describing the provision (رِزْقًا) mentioned earlier in the verse
Basic form of the interrogative/negative particle
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added elongation mark (آ) to show lengthened pronunciation at end of word, which is common in Quranic writing but doesn't change the meaning
Basic meaning of wanting or desiring something
Changes to first person ('I') present tense by adding أُ at start
This is a connecting word that links 'I want/desire' (أُرِيدُ) with what is wanted - 'to oppose you' (أُخَالِفَكُمْ). It works like 'to' in English when we say 'I want to do something.'

steps.m: to differ from or go against
steps.n: Root meaning difference or opposition

steps.m: to oppose or go against someone
steps.n: Form III shows interaction between two parties - someone opposing someone else

steps.m: I go against (object of أُرِيدُ/I want)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with أُ prefix showing 'I' as the doer. The word is the object of أُرِيدُ (I want) in the ayah, showing what the speaker does not want to do.

steps.m: I go against you all (object of أُرِيدُ/I want)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who is being opposed - 'you all'
This is a primary preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to 'what I forbid you from'
Added an alif symbol (ىٰ) at the end to show the long 'aa' sound clearly. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
Basic negative particle that negates the verb أُرِيدُ (I want) in this ayah
Added alif with madda (آ) which is a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to make someone do the action'
Added alif maksura (ى with mark above) at the end because this is a weak-ending verb
Added كُمْ at the end to show who is being prevented (you all)
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'away from'
Added pronoun هُ (him/it) to show what/who is being moved away from. Here it refers back to the provision (رِزْقًا) mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. Here it connects to the verb كُنتُ (I was) to say 'if I was...'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he wanted' to 'I want' by adding أُ at start
This is a particle used for exception - it limits what comes before it. Not derived from a root.
Added alif at the end to connect it to what follows. In this ayah, it's saying that the speaker only wants reform/improvement.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the word from a verb (the action of fixing) to a noun (the concept of fixing/improving)
Adding ال makes the word definite

steps.m: the fixing/improving (object of أُرِيدُ - I want)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb أُرِيدُ (I want/intend)
This is a particle that means 'as much as' or 'to the extent that' when it comes before a verb like استطعت (I was able). Together they mean 'as much as I was able'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to mean 'seeking/trying to have' the ability to do something. Like asking 'can I?' or 'am I able to?'
Changed طْوَ to طَا to make it easier to say. The ا shows ability/capability.
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did it in the past
This is a particle word that doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it's being used to connect ideas by introducing what comes after أُرِيدُ (I want).

steps.m: and what/and that (connects to أُرِيدُ - I want)
steps.n: The و (wa) is added at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this phrase to the previous statement. In this ayah, it's connecting two related ideas about what the speaker wants and doesn't want.

steps.m: to help succeed or guide to what's right

steps.n: Adding the short 'a' vowels (fathas) turns the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he helped'
Changes the verb 'helped' into a noun meaning 'the act of helping' by adding تَ at start and making middle letter long
Adds ي (my) at the end to show possession - 'my guidance'

steps.m: my success/guidance (in relation to إِلَّا بِٱللَّهِ - except by Allah)
steps.n: Elongates the final ي for emphasis and pronunciation. This word is connected to the phrase 'except by Allah' showing that guidance only comes through Allah's help.
This is the base form of the exception particle that means 'except' or 'only'
Adding shadda emphasizes the meaning

steps.m: except/only (in this ayah: showing that reform/improvement is his only goal)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end completes the word. In this ayah, it's used with إِنْ أُرِيدُ (I want) to show that reform/improvement is the only thing being sought.
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity/god
Added a double letter L (shadda) to change from 'a god' to 'Allah' - the special name for The One True God
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is being done with or by Allah's help

The word 'Allah' gets a kasra (i-sound) at the end because it comes after the word 'bi' (meaning 'by/with'). The regular alif changes to a special alif (wasl-alif) since it's not at the start of a sentence.
This is a preposition that shows something is above or on top of something else, like saying 'the book is on the table' in English
When adding a suffix, the ى changes to يْ to make pronunciation easier
Adding هِ shows this applies to 'him'. In this ayah, it connects to تَوَكَّلْتُ (I rely), showing what is being relied upon.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding تَ at start and doubling ك shows doing the action of trusting upon someone/something else (not oneself)

steps.m: I put my complete trust (in عَلَيْهِ - in Him)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action, and added sukun before it. This word connects to عَلَيْهِ (in Him) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination
The و adds the meaning of 'and' to connect this with what came before
The ى changes to ي with sukun before adding pronouns

steps.m: and towards him (connected to أُنِيبُ - I turn in repentance)
steps.n: The هِ at the end means 'him' - it shows who the direction is towards. This word connects to أُنِيبُ (I turn in repentance) in the next ayah, showing the direction of the repentance.

steps.m: to return or turn back (especially to something good)
steps.n: This is the basic meaning of returning or going back to something
The root letters were made into 'he returned' by adding vowel marks and changing و to ا in the middle
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'he made himself return' - used for returning to Allah in repentance

steps.m: to gather, rise up, or stand firm

steps.n: Root meaning 'stand/rise' becomes 'people' (like a standing group) by adding fatha-sukun pattern
Added يٰ to directly address the people being spoken to

steps.m: O my people! (direct address with possession)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show the speaker's connection to the people ('my people')

steps.m: And O my people! (connecting to previous statement)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
This is a basic negative particle that negates the verb that follows it. In this ayah, it negates يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ (let [something] cause you).
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to cause harm or drive away

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added نَّ for emphasis, showing this is a strong warning

steps.m: let not my disagreement cause harm to you all
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show who is being affected. In this ayah, it's a warning from the speaker to his people about letting their disagreement with him lead them to destruction.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to noun showing conflict between two parties
Added ي with kasra to show possession (my)
Changed ي to ى (different spelling, same meaning)

steps.m: that (introduces what might happen to يُصِيبَكُم)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces what might happen - in this case, it connects to يُصِيبَكُم (it befalls you). It's like saying 'I warn you that something might happen'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start. The و changes to ي because it comes between two vowels.

steps.m: he strikes/affects you all (object of لَا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ)
steps.n: Added كُم to show who is being affected. This word is connected to لَا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ (do not let... cause you) in this ayah.
Added vowels to make a noun meaning 'likeness' from the basic idea of being similar
Added damma at the end to show this word is the subject of the sentence
No additional changes needed
Added fatha to show this is a relative pronoun used to refer to things or actions
The alif is elongated (آ) to show a longer pronunciation. This word refers to what happened to the people of Noah mentioned later in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb. The middle weak letter و changes to ا.

steps.m: he afflicted/struck (subject of يُصِيبَكُم in the ayah)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show this is something that actively affected others. This word relates to يُصِيبَكُم (it afflicts you) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
This is a proper name with no root, shown in the genitive case with tanwin kasra (ٍ)

steps.m: Noah (being referred to as part of قَوْمَ/people of)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) to show this word follows قَوْمَ (people of) in the phrase 'people of Noah'

steps.m: or (connecting قَوْمَ نُوحٍ with قَوْمَ هُودٍ and قَوْمَ صٰلِحٍ)
steps.n: This is a basic connecting word that shows different choices or possibilities. Here it connects different groups of people who were punished, saying it could be like what happened to 'the people of Noah OR the people of Hud OR the people of Salih'

steps.m: to stand together, rise as one

steps.n: Changed the action of standing together into a noun meaning the group that stands together - a people or community
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb أَصَابَ (struck/afflicted). This is part of the phrase 'struck/afflicted the people of Noah'.

steps.m: to guide, lead, show the way

steps.n: Adding damma makes it into a proper noun referring to the people/tribe

steps.m: Prophet Hud's (people) (object of قَوْمَ - people of)
steps.n: Adding the kasra tanween shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else. Here it shows these are 'the people of Hud' (قَوْمَ هُودٍ)

steps.m: or (connecting قَوْمَ نُوحٍ with قَوْمَ هُودٍ)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows alternatives or choices. Here it connects 'the people of Nuh' with 'the people of Hud' to show they are similar examples.

steps.m: to stand firmly or rise up

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'people' or 'group' - the idea comes from people standing together as one unit
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being possessed by Noah (نُوحٍ) in the phrase 'people of Noah'

steps.m: to make something good or right

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from verb (he made good) to noun (one who is good)
Changed the ending to show this word belongs to or is possessed by another word (قَوْمَ meaning 'people')
Added fatha to show the correct pronunciation
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to the previous statement about not letting disagreement lead them to sin

steps.m: to stand together as a group

steps.n: Changed the root meaning of standing together into a noun meaning the people who stand together as a group
The ُ at the end shows this word is the subject (doer) of the verb يُصِيبَكُم (befalls you) in this ayah
Added special marks to show Lut belongs to/is connected to another word (his people)
This is a preposition that shows something coming from or originating from something else
The shadda (ّ) is added because this word comes after a word ending in ن, causing the sounds to merge together for easier pronunciation
Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being talked to
Added ي between second and third letters to show this is a strong quality
Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how something is in relation to قَوْمُ لُوطٍ (the people of Lot)
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English) and is being affected by the word before it
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make past tense 'he forgave'
Added ٱسْتَ at the beginning to show asking for forgiveness
Changed vowels to make it present tense 'I' form
Added وا at the end to change from 'I seek' to 'you all seek' - makes it a command for a group

steps.m: and seek forgiveness, all of you (command to رَبَّكُمْ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added و to connect this command with what comes before it

steps.m: to care for and help grow
he cared for and helped grow
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: one who cares for and helps grow completely
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone's main quality or role
Adding fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by something

steps.m: your (all of you) lord (object of اسْتَغْفِرُوا - seek forgiveness)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this belongs to multiple people being spoken to. This word is what's being acted upon by the verb اسْتَغْفِرُوا (seek forgiveness from)
This is the basic form of the connecting word that shows one thing happens after another
Added the damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) marks to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects two commands: first seek forgiveness, then repent.

steps.m: to turn back or return (especially to Allah)

steps.n: Changed the root into a command by adding damma (ُ) - like saying 'you, return!' to one person

steps.m: you all turn back! (command to group)
steps.n: Added وا to make it a command to multiple people. The alif (ا) is lengthened with a small و above it to show emphasis in the command.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'. It's one of the essential words in Arabic that doesn't come from a three-letter root.
The ى changes to ي with a sukun (ْ) when we add something to the end. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Adding هِ at the end makes it mean 'to him'. This connects to the previous words in the ayah about asking forgiveness and turning in repentance.
Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it (that my Lord is Most Merciful, Most Loving) stronger and more certain.

steps.m: to care for and develop something to completion
lord (one who cares completely for others)
steps.n: Adding the vowel and doubling the middle letter shows this is someone who does this action perfectly
Adding ى at the end shows this is 'my' Lord, connecting it to the speaker

steps.m: to show kindness and care to someone

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowels (َ ِ) to mean 'he showed mercy'
Added ي to change from an action (showing kindness) to describing someone who is always kind

steps.m: The Most Kind and Caring One (describing رَبِّى - my Lord)
steps.n: Added two dots on top (tanween) to show this is describing 'my Lord' as a quality - like saying 'My Lord is Most Kind'
Added vowels and the pattern with و to show this describes someone who has abundant, constant love as part of their nature. In this ayah, it describes Allah's loving nature.
Adding tanween shows this is one of Allah's attributes being used as a name/title

The tanween (double dammah) is already present in both words - the difference is in the meaning/context rather than form. The word changes from being a Divine attribute to a general adjective meaning 'loving'.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
This is a proper name, likely derived from the root ش-ع-ب which relates to 'branching' or 'division', though the exact etymology is debated
The ُ (damma) at the end shows Shuaib is being called or addressed directly
The يٰ at the beginning is used to call out to someone, like saying 'Hey!' in English
This is an interrogative particle used to ask 'what'. It is an indeclinable particle that does not have a root.
Added the fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation. This is a negative particle that comes before the verb نَفْقَهُ (we understand) to negate it.

steps.m: to understand deeply or comprehend fully

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha and kasra) to make it mean 'he understood deeply' in the past tense
Changed to present tense 'we' form and added object pronoun. This word follows مَا (what) in the ayah, showing what 'we' don't understand.
Added ي to show this is a quality or characteristic. This word describes 'what' (مِمَّا) in 'what you say'
Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this describes how something is done - in this case, how much they understand
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from' that connects one thing to another
Added ما meaning 'what'. The ن changes to م with a shadda because they're similar sounds. This refers to what Shu'ayb is saying in the ayah.
Added emphasis to the initial م with a shadda to strengthen pronunciation
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter between two fathas
Changed vowels to show ongoing action instead of past action. The و returns because it's not between two fathas anymore.
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what comes after it, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
Added ا at the end to show this is about 'we/us'
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it, like saying 'and'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added نَ prefix to show 'we' and changed to present tense form. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end
Added emphatic لَ to show certainty
Added كَ to show who is being seen
Added kasra vowel mark to show the basic preposition meaning

steps.m: in us (among the people being addressed in تَقُولُ - you say)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show this means 'us'. This word is connected to تَقُولُ (you say) in the ayah, showing who is being spoken to.
Adding the vowels fat-ha (َ) and damma (ُ) turns the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he became weak'
Changes verb into adjective by adding ي between second and third root letters

steps.m: weak (as the object being described in نَرَىٰكَ - we see you)
steps.n: Adds tanween fatHa to show this word is describing the object of the verb نَرَىٰكَ (we see you)
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical scenario and implies that something would have happened if not for what follows

steps.m: and if not for (رَهْطُكَ your clan)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. This links to رَهْطُكَ (your clan) which completes the condition.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun
Added ُ at the end to show this is the doer in the sentence
Added كَ at the end to show it belongs to the person being spoken to. This word is the subject (doer) in the sentence.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
The نا at the end shows 'we' did the action. This is a complete verb form and is not related to other words through case.
The ك suffix shows the object (person being stoned) is 'you'. This is a verb with an attached pronoun, not a noun that would have case endings.

steps.m: we would surely have stoned you (hypothetical consequence of لَوْلَا)
steps.n: The لَ prefix adds emphasis and shows this is a hypothetical result. This is the result clause of لَوْلَا (if it were not for) from earlier in the verse.
Basic negation particle that shows something is not true
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added elongation mark (آ) to emphasize the negation. This is a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning.
This is a basic personal pronoun meaning 'you' (when speaking to one male). In this ayah, it's the subject being described as not mighty/strong over us.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'on' or 'upon'
When adding pronouns after عَلَى, the ى changes to يْ to make it easier to connect
Added نَا at the end to show this applies to 'us'
Added ي between the second and third root letters and vowel marks to create an adjective showing someone or something has this quality strongly

steps.m: with/by mighty/powerful (describing your position علَيْنَا - over us)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show how you are seen by the people being spoken to. This connects to علَيْنَا (over us) in the ayah.

steps.m: with/by mighty/powerful (describing your position علَيْنَا - over us)
steps.n: Added the tanween (double dots) under to show this is an adjective describing something, like saying 'mighty' or 'powerful'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Changes verb root into a noun meaning 'a group that stands together'
Added يٰ at the beginning to call out to the people directly, like saying 'Hey people!' or 'O people!'
Added kasra at the end to show possession - like saying 'my people' instead of just 'people'
Basic noun pattern showing a group of people (between 3-10 men)
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question, and ِ at the end to prepare for possession
Added ى with a long vowel mark (ٓ) at the end to show possession (meaning 'my')
Connected the root letters and doubled the last letter to show intensity
Adding ي in the middle makes this an intensive adjective - showing someone/something has a lot of this quality
Added أَ at the beginning to make it comparative. In this ayah, it's comparing the strength/importance of something relative to 'you' (عَلَيْكُم)
Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing something in the sentence
This is a basic preposition showing something being above or on top of something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning

steps.m: upon you all (addressing the people in أَرَهْطِىٓ)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This connects to أَرَهْطِىٓ (my clan/people) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Basic particle showing source or origin - not derived from a root
Added shadda for emphasis and smooth pronunciation when connected to the next word (اللَّهِ)
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it the proper name for The One God
Added shadda on ل, changed initial alif to hamzat wasl, and added kasra at the end to show this word follows مِنَ (from/than) in the ayah

steps.m: to take or adopt something as one's own

steps.n: Added ت and doubled it (ت + ت = تّ) to show doing the action for yourself - like 'taking something for yourself'

steps.m: he took for himself, he adopted
steps.n: Added the ending -a sound to make it mean 'he took' instead of just 'to take'
Changed first letter from أ to اِ and added تَ at the end to change from 'he took' to 'you took'
Added وَ (and) at start + تُم at end to make it 'and you all took'
Added masculine singular object pronoun suffix هُ
Root letters transformed into a location word meaning 'behind' by adding vowel marks (fatha)
Added اء to complete the common noun meaning 'behind'
Added fatha to prepare for attachment of pronoun
Added plural 'you' pronoun to show who is being put behind

steps.m: to appear or be visible from behind something

steps.n: Changes the concept into a concrete noun for a physical thing
Makes an adjective meaning 'related to the back'
The ًا ending shows this word is used as an adverb describing how Allah was treated - like something thrown behind one's back and forgotten
Adding shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. This word emphasizes the importance of what comes after it (رَبِّى - my Lord).

steps.m: to take care of and help grow
one who takes complete care of others
steps.n: The doubled ب (shown by ّ) makes the meaning stronger, showing someone who really takes care of others completely

steps.m: one who takes complete care of others (connected to something after it)
steps.n: The ِ at the end shows this word connects to something coming after it

steps.m: my Lord (the one who takes complete care of me)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this Lord belongs to me or takes care of me specifically
This is the basic interrogative word meaning 'what'

Added بِ at the beginning to show how or by what means something is done. In this ayah, it connects to the word مُحِيطٌ (encompassing), showing how Allah encompasses or surrounds - it's by/with what they do.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The تَ at the start shows 'you' are doing the action in the present tense
ونَ shows that multiple people are being addressed

steps.m: to surround or protect by enclosing

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and ِ in middle to show who does the action. The و changed to ي because it comes after kasra.
Added tanwin damma (ٌ) to show this is describing Allah's attribute of being All-Encompassing of everything His servants do, as mentioned in the ayah (بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ)

steps.m: to stand together, rise up as one

steps.n: Root meaning 'stand/rise' becomes a noun meaning 'people who stand together' - like a community or nation
Added يا (yaa) at the start to call out 'O people!' - like saying 'Hey people!'
The kasra at the end shows possession - meaning 'my people'
و means 'and' when it comes at the start
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Adding اِ at start turns it into a command
Added plural masculine ending وا to make the command address multiple people
Basic preposition showing position or status. Here it connects to مَكَانَتِكُمْ to show 'upon your position/state', meaning 'continue in your current way'
Added an alif (ٰ) at the end to show the long 'aa' sound. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state/place

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to create a noun for where something exists or happens
Added ة to make it feminine and change meaning from physical place to abstract status
Added ِ to connect this word to what comes after it (showing possession)
Added كُمْ to show this refers to all of you. This word is what you should act upon (object) in the command 'work according to your status'
This is the basic emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
The نَّ changes to نِّ to prepare for adding the ي at the end
Adding ى at the end makes this about 'me' (first person). In Arabic, when you add 'I am' to إِنَّ, it shows that you're emphasizing something about yourself
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and changed middle vowel to kasra to show the doer of the action
Changed alif to alif with bar (special writing style) and added tanwin damma to show this is indefinite and in the nominative case. This word describes 'I' in the phrase إِنِّى (indeed I am)
This is a particle that indicates something will definitely happen in the future. It's stronger and more certain than just سَ. Here it modifies تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) to mean 'you will know'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The ت at the start shows this is happening now and the person doing it is 'you'. The vowel pattern changes to show present tense.

steps.m: you (all) know (in response to اعْمَلُوا - you all work)
steps.n: Adding ون at the end makes it plural - now talking to many people. This matches with اعْمَلُوا (you all work) earlier in the verse, showing the same group is being addressed.

steps.m: who/whoever (connects to يَأْتِيهِ - comes to him)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links two parts of the sentence together. It's asking about who will receive something in the future. In this ayah, it's specifically asking who will receive punishment.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future. The ْ (sukun) and ِ (kasra) help with pronunciation.
Added هِ at the end to show who is being affected by the action. This word is connected to عَذَابٌ (punishment) which is doing the action of coming.

steps.m: punishment, torment (subject of يَأْتِيهِ - comes to him)
steps.n: Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun meaning the result of causing pain/suffering. This word is what 'comes to him' (يَأْتِيهِ) in the verse.

steps.m: a punishment (subject of يَأْتِيهِ - comes to him)
steps.n: Adding the ٌ mark shows this is any punishment, not a specific one. It's what will come to the wrongdoer.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The يُ at the beginning shows this is happening now or in the future. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) and the last letter gets a kasra (ِ)
The هِ at the end means 'him'. In this ayah, this disgrace is what the punishment (عَذَابٌ) does to someone.
Combines 'and' with the relative pronoun 'whoever'
This is a personal pronoun meaning 'he'. In this ayah, it connects to the word كٰذِبٌ (liar) to mean 'he is a liar'.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the word to show who does the action instead of the action itself
Added tanwin damma to show this is indefinite. The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ). This word describes هُوَ (he) in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Makes the verb reflexive - doing the watching/waiting for oneself
Changed from past to present tense 'I wait', shown by the 'u' ending
Changed from 'I wait' to 'you all wait!' by adding وا ending to make it a command for a group
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect it to previous statement
This is a special word that adds emphasis to statements. Adding the shadda makes it stronger.
Added ي at the end to show this is about me. This word connects to عٰمِلٌ (worker/doing) to say 'indeed I am a worker/doer'.
This is a preposition that shows accompaniment

steps.m: with you all (addressing the people Shuaib is speaking to)
steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all' when talking to multiple people. In this ayah, Prophet Shuaib is addressing his people.
