Added و at beginning to connect with previous phrase. This word commands the establishment of prayer (الصَّلَوٰةَ)

steps.m: to establish prayer, perform ritual prayer

steps.n: Root letters ص-ل-و changed to make the noun 'prayer' by adding an alif and ة at the end
Adding ٱل makes it definite. The ص gets a shadda (doubled) after ٱل.
The middle letter changes from ا to و with a small alif (ٰ) on top - both spellings mean the same thing
Adding fatha at the end shows this is the object of the command 'establish' (أَقِمِ)
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make it a noun meaning 'end' or 'edge'
Added ى to show we're talking about two ends/edges

steps.m: at the two ends/edges (of النَّهَارِ - the day)
steps.n: Added ِ at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it (the day)
Added alif to create a noun for the time when light flows/streams
Added ال to specify we're talking about a particular day or daytime in general
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to طَرَفَىِ (two ends) in the phrase 'the two ends of the day'
Changes from basic root to plural noun showing multiple periods
Added ًا to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence

steps.m: and times of closeness (connected to طَرَفَىِ/ends)
steps.n: Added و to connect this word with طَرَفَىِ (ends) from earlier in the verse, showing when prayers should be performed
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from
The م is doubled because it comes after a word ending in ن, making it easier to pronounce (called assimilation). The meaning stays the same.
Added fatha at the end to show this connects to the next word ٱلَّيْلِ (the night)
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added ال to make it definite and kasra at the end because it follows مِنَ (from)
This is a basic emphasis particle that makes statements stronger. The kasra (ِ) is added under the first letter.
The shadda (ّ) makes the word stronger. This word emphasizes that the good deeds (ٱلْحَسَنٰتِ) mentioned after it definitely remove bad deeds.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changes from 'good' (verb) to 'a good deed' (feminine noun) by adding ة at the end
Forms regular feminine plural from feminine singular noun. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb يذهبن (they remove/eliminate).
Makes the word definite by adding ال, showing these are specific/known good deeds
Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he' form
Adding alif at start makes it causative - doing something to something else
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra (i sound)
Added نَ at the end to show multiple feminine subjects (referring to الْحَسَنٰتِ - good deeds)
Root س-و-أ changed to سَيِّئ by adding ي with shadda to make it mean 'very evil/bad' (like turning 'bad' into 'terrible')
Added ة at the end to make feminine noun meaning 'a bad thing/deed'

steps.m: evil deeds (that are removed by good deeds)
steps.n: Adding ات makes it plural. This word is what gets removed by good deeds in the phrase 'good deeds remove evil deeds'
Adding definite article makes the word definite
Added a small mark (kasra) at the end to show it connects to something after it
Basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for something close by
Added alif above ذ to show it's a demonstrative pronoun
Added ل with kasra to show distance - changes 'this' to 'that'
Added ك with fatha to show we're talking about one masculine thing
Added vowel marks to turn the action into a noun meaning the act itself
Added ى with alif on top to make it a special kind of reminder - something that helps people remember
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changes from a past tense verb (he remembered) to a noun meaning 'one who does the remembering' by adding an alif and changing the middle vowel
Adding ين makes it mean many people who remember. The word is in genitive case because it comes after لِ (for) and is possessed by ذكرى (reminder).
The ال causes the first letter to get a shadda (ّ)

steps.m: for the ones who remember/mention (connected to ذِكْرَىٰ)
steps.n: The لِ means 'for/to' and shows this word is connected to ذِكْرَىٰ (reminder) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he was patient' to 'be patient!' by adding alif at start and changing ending - makes it a command to one person

steps.m: and be patient! (connected to فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this command to what follows it - Allah's promise not to waste the reward of those who do good
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it

steps.m: for indeed (connects to وَٱصْبِرْ - and be patient)
steps.n: The فَ shows this is connected to the command 'be patient' that came before it. It's like saying 'be patient, for indeed...'
Changed to the proper name for The One True God by adding ال and merging letters

steps.m: Allah (as the object being referred to)
steps.n: The word 'Allah' gets a stronger L sound (shadda), starts with a connecting alif, and ends with a fatha mark to show it's being acted upon in the sentence
This is a basic negative word that makes the verb يُضِيعُ (he wastes/loses) into 'he does not waste/lose'
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed the vowels to show this happens in the present/future instead of the past
Added يُ at the beginning to show who is doing the action. In this ayah, Allah is the one who never wastes the reward.

steps.m: to give a reward or payment for work

steps.n: Changed from action to thing by adding vowel marks. This word is what's being acted on by يُضِيعُ (he wastes/loses) - Allah is saying He doesn't waste/lose this reward.

steps.m: reward, payment (being acted upon by يُضِيعُ)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يُضِيعُ (he wastes/loses)
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Adding م at the start and changing vowels turns this into a word describing someone who does the action

The ين at the end makes this plural, meaning more than one person who does good. This word is in the genitive case because it comes after أجر (reward) in a possession relationship, where the reward belongs to those who do good.

steps.m: the doers of good (object of يُضِيعُ/does not waste)
steps.n: The word gained 'the' (ال) at the start and became definite, showing it refers to specific doers of good. It's the object being acted upon (the thing that is not wasted)
This is a questioning particle that expresses 'why not' or 'if only'. It's used to ask why something didn't happen or to wish something had happened.

steps.m: so why not / then why didn't
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from what came before, like saying 'so' or 'then' in English
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed to common past tense form with weak middle letter
Basic preposition indicating source or origin
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلْقُرُونِ (the generations)

steps.m: to join together in time or space

steps.n: Root meaning 'join together' becomes a noun meaning 'a group of people living in the same time period'
Changes the word to show multiple generations

steps.m: the generations (possessed by مِن قَبْلِكُمْ - from before you)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning to make it specific, and ِ at the end to show it's possessed by or connected to something (in this case, the time 'before you')

Basic preposition showing that something comes from or originates from something else. Here it connects 'the generations' (ٱلْقُرُونِ) to the verb 'was' (كَانَ), showing which generations we're talking about.
Added vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'before' that can be used to tell when something happened

steps.m: before you all (referring to generations mentioned in ٱلْقُرُونِ)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'you all'. The kasra (ِ) connects the pronoun to the word. This refers back to the generations (ٱلْقُرُونِ) mentioned earlier in the verse.

This is a special plural noun that means 'people who have' or 'possessors of' something. It's always followed by something that is possessed. Here it refers to people who have بَقِيَّةٍ (righteousness/virtue).
Added small circles (۟) which are pronunciation marks to show proper recitation in the Quran. These don't change the meaning.
Adding vowels (fatha, kasra, fatha) to the root letters to make it mean 'he remained'
Doubling the last letter makes the meaning stronger
Added ة to turn the verb 'remained' into a noun meaning 'a remainder/what's left'
Added dots under the last letter to show this word is being affected by something else in the sentence
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added يَ at start to show present tense, and changed first vowel to sukun
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: away from (used with يَنْهَوْنَ - they prevent)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing separation or distance. Here it works with the verb يَنْهَوْنَ to show what they are preventing people from.

steps.m: away from (used with يَنْهَوْنَ - they prevent)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because the next word starts with ال. This makes it easier to pronounce.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding ا after second letter turns the verb (corrupted) into a noun (corruption)
Adding ال makes it specific corruption rather than corruption in general
Added ِ at the end because it comes after عَنِ (from/about). Also changed اَلْ to ٱلْ which is just a different way of writing the same thing.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows location within الْأَرْضِ (the earth).
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'fee'
Changed the final ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words. This doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
Root letters put together with marks to make the basic word for 'earth'
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
The kasra ending shows this word follows فِى (in/on) in the ayah
This is a fixed particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before. Not derived from a root.

steps.m: except (excluding قَلِيلًا/few from the previous statement)
steps.n: The long alif at the end helps extend the sound. In this ayah, it's excluding 'few' (قَلِيلًا) from the previous statement about people who prevented corruption.

steps.m: to be small in amount or number

steps.n: The ي makes it into an adjective describing something that has the quality of being small in amount

steps.m: a small amount (of those who forbade corruption)

Starting with the two separate words: مِنْ (from) and مَنْ (who/whoever). Both are particles that don't come from roots.
When مِنْ comes before مَنْ, the ن from مِنْ combines with the م from مَنْ, shown by the shadda (ّ) on the م. This is just a pronunciation change.
Added a shadda on the first م to show strong pronunciation at the start of the word. This is just a pronunciation helper.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes it mean 'cause someone to be rescued' instead of just 'rescue'
Adding نَا at the end shows that 'we' did the action
Combination of preposition مِن with plural masculine pronoun هُم
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense
Added اِ at start and doubled the ت with shadda to show doing action intensely for oneself
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and the اِ changes to ٱ after و
This is the base word used to point to someone or something being described
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people or things
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific and point to particular people, with shadda for emphasis. Added َ at the end to show it's connected to what comes after it.
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it past tense

steps.m: they wronged (subject of اتَّبَعَ - they followed)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word is connected to اتَّبَعَ (they followed) in the ayah, showing what the wrongdoers did.
This is a basic interrogative particle meaning 'what'. It's used to ask questions or introduce relative clauses.
The alif is elongated with a madda mark (آ) to show emphasis and lengthened pronunciation. In this ayah, it refers back to the luxuries or comforts mentioned in context.

steps.m: to live in ease and luxury

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: he was made to live in luxury
steps.n: Changed to passive form showing someone was made to do this by others

steps.m: they were made to live in luxury
steps.n: Added وا to show this happened to a group of people
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it
Added ه with kasra (هِ) at the end to show what is being referred to. The 'it' here refers back to the luxury/comfort mentioned in أُتْرِفُوا (they were given luxury) in this ayah.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: The root letters ك-و-ن became كَانَ by adding fatha marks and changing و to ا. This makes it mean 'he was' instead of just 'being'.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) instead of just one person (he)

steps.m: and they were (connected to ظَلَمُوا۟ - they wronged)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this word to the previous verb ظَلَمُوا۟ (they wronged), showing these actions happened together
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action
Added ين to make it plural, showing there are many criminals
This is a special word that negates the verb كَانَ (was) that comes after it in this ayah
The و connects this negation to what came before it, adding the meaning of 'and'

steps.m: to be in a state or condition
he was (past tense verb before رَبُّكَ)
steps.n: Added fathas (َ) after each letter to make it past tense. This word comes before رَبُّكَ (your Lord) to tell us about something in the past.
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show intensity of nurturing/lordship
Added damma to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb كَانَ (was) in this ayah
Added كَ to show this is your Lord. This word is still the subject of كَانَ (was) in the phrase 'and your Lord was not...'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: so that he destroys (purpose of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose/intent. This explains why something was happening in the ayah.
Added vowels to make the plural form for places where people settle

steps.m: the towns (object of لِيُهْلِكَ - to destroy)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific. This word is what is being destroyed in the phrase 'to destroy the towns'
Added damma and sukun to make the root into a noun meaning 'wrongdoing'

steps.m: with wrongdoing (describing how هلاك/destruction would happen)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'with' and tanwin at the end to show it's indefinite. This describes how the destruction mentioned in يُهْلِكَ (to destroy) would happen.
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'people' or 'inhabitants'
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence
Added ها to show these are the people of the towns (القرى) mentioned earlier

steps.m: and its people (referring to the towns)
steps.n: Added و to connect this word to what came before it
Forms the word for 'one who does good'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past-tense verb
Added مُ at the start and changed vowels to show someone who does the action

steps.m: those who fix/make things better (in the ayah: while they were doing good)
steps.n: Added ون to show it's talking about many people who do this action
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. لَوْ introduces hypothetical situations that didn't happen.

steps.m: and even if (connects to شَآءَ which follows)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. The full phrase وَلَوْ شَآءَ means 'and if [He] had willed'.
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) to turn the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
The weak letter hamza (ء) combines with alif to make a long 'aa' sound
The long alif (آ) makes the 'aa' sound even stronger

steps.m: to care for, develop, bring to completion

steps.n: The doubled letter (ّ) shows this describes someone who does this action perfectly and completely
The ُ shows this word is doing the action of شَآءَ (willed/wished) in this ayah
Adds possessive pronoun to indicate ownership/relation. Acts as the subject (doer) of the verb شَآءَ (willed/wished).

steps.m: he made/established (connected to شَآءَ - 'if He willed')
steps.n: Added fatha after each letter to show this happened in the past. This word is part of the result clause after 'if He willed' (شَآءَ) in this ayah.

steps.m: He would surely have made (result of شَآءَ - 'if He willed')
steps.n: The لَ at the start shows this is definitely what would happen. It's used here because this is telling us what would definitely happen if Allah had willed it.

steps.m: to be social or familiar with others

steps.n: Root meaning 'to be social' became the word for 'people' - humans are social beings who live together
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن letter for pronunciation
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of the verb لَجَعَلَ (he would have made)
Root أمم becomes plural أُمم (nations) by adding the damma marks
Added feminine ending to create noun for a group or community

steps.m: a community (object of لَجَعَلَ - would have made)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb لَجَعَلَ (would have made)
Added alif after first letter to show something that has this quality. This word describes أُمَّةً (nation/community) in the ayah.
Added ة to match with أُمَّةً which is feminine

steps.m: one (feminine, describes أُمَّةً in the accusative case)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha (ً) to show this word follows أُمَّةً in the accusative case. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this word.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word negates يَزَالُونَ (they cease) in this ayah, making it mean 'they do not cease'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make past tense. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a hollow verb.
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and changed end vowel to ُ
Added ونَ to show multiple people (they). This word connects to مُخْتَلِفِينَ (differing) to say 'they continue to differ'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and ت after first letter to show something is different on its own (like different colors or types)

steps.m: those who are differing (predicate of يَزَالُونَ)
steps.n: Added ين to show it's plural and in the accusative case. This word describes the state of ٱلنَّاسَ (the people) and follows يَزَالُونَ (they continue to be)

This is a word that means 'whoever' or 'the one who'. In this ayah, it refers to the people who receive mercy, as it's the subject of رَحِمَ (had mercy).
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy
The shadda is added because this word comes after إِلَّا مَن (except whom), causing the first letter to be doubled for pronunciation

steps.m: to take care of, raise, nurture

Adding كَ shows this Lord belongs to you (the person being spoken to). This Lord is the one showing mercy in the verb رَّحِمَ.
The ٰلِ shows this is pointing to something far away rather than nearby
The كَ makes it point to a specific thing being discussed
The لِ shows this is explaining the reason or purpose for something
The وَ connects this to what came before it in the verse
Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding the short 'a' sound (َ) after each letter

The ُ shows this word is doing the action in the sentence - here it's the subject of تَمَّتْ (was completed)

Adding kasra shows this word is possessed by كَلِمَةُ (word/decree) in the phrase كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ (the word of your Lord)

steps.m: Hell (object of لَأَمْلَأَنَّ - I will surely fill)

steps.m: of the group of jinn (after مِنَ)
steps.n: Shows this word comes after مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah

steps.m: and the people (connected to الْجِنَّةِ meaning 'jinn')

Adding the conjunction prefix to connect this phrase with previous content

steps.m: that which (connects to نُثَبِّتُ - we make firm)

The كَ at the end means 'you' (when speaking to one male)

The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

Added لِ with shadda at beginning to mean 'for/to'. This connects to the command اعْمَلُوا (work/do) in the ayah, showing who the command is directed to.
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe), making it mean 'they do not believe'.

steps.m: to work, to act, to do

steps.n: Changed to mean someone who does the action. This word comes after إنّا (indeed we) to show 'we are workers'.
Added ون with ُ and َ marks to show this refers to multiple people who work/act. Comes after إنّا to mean 'indeed we are workers'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Form VIII pattern changes meaning from basic 'looking' to more specific 'waiting/expecting'
Adding damma at the end makes it present tense

steps.m: and all of you wait! (in response to إِنَّا مُنتَظِرُونَ - indeed we are waiting)

steps.m: indeed we (emphasizes that we are doing مُنتَظِرُونَ - waiting)

Adding اِنْتَ changes the meaning to 'waiting specifically for something', like waiting for a friend or bus

Added وات to make it plural (more than one sky) - و is added before ات in this type of plural
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س (shown by ّ) because it comes after ال

Added short 'a' and 'sukoon' marks to make it readable as 'ard' (earth)
Added ال ('the') at the start and the kasra mark ( ِ ) at the end to show it's 'the earth' and is owned by something
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens) mentioned before it
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. It's one of the special words that doesn't come from a three-letter root.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for the attached pronoun
Added هِ at the end to show this means 'to him'. This word connects to يُرْجَعُ (is returned) in the ayah, showing that everything returns to Him (Allah).

steps.m: it is returned (subject: الْأَمْرُ - the matter)

Added damma and shadda to create the basic noun meaning 'all'
Added damma ending because this word is the subject of يُرْجَعُ (is returned)

steps.m: all of it (subject of يُرْجَعُ, describes الأمر)
steps.n: Added pronoun suffix هُۥ meaning 'it' referring back to الأمر (the matter/affair)

steps.m: trust! (as a command to one person)
and trust! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: This command appears in a sequence after فَٱعْبُدْهُ (so worship Him), connecting these two commands
This is a basic preposition showing something being on top of or over something else
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns

steps.m: upon him (connected to يُرْجَعُ - is returned)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this means 'him'. In this ayah, it's connected to يُرْجَعُ (is returned), showing that everything is returned to Him.
This is a particle that negates the verb يَعْمَلُونَ (they do) in this ayah
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous statement
Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete mastery/lordship
Added damma to show this is the doer in relation to بِغٰفِلٍ (unaware)
Added كَ to show this is your Lord. This word is the subject in the phrase 'Your Lord is not unaware' (وَمَا رَبُّكَ بِغٰفِلٍ).
This word describes what Allah is NOT - the ayah is saying 'Your Lord is NOT someone who ignores' what you do.

steps.m: unaware/neglectful of (describes what Allah is NOT)
steps.n: Adds 'bi' (meaning 'not at all') at the start to strongly deny that Allah is unaware/neglectful

steps.m: unaware/neglectful of (describes what Allah is NOT)
steps.n: The word already has بِ (bi) attached at the start - it wasn't added as a transformation. The بِ connects this word to 'not' (ما) earlier in the verse to say 'not unaware/neglectful'
This is a preposition that shows what something is about or concerning
Added ما which means 'what' to ask about something
The نْ combines with م to create a doubled م (shown with شدّة). This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic. This word asks about what is being done in تَعْمَلُونَ (you all do).
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters together
The تَ at the start shows 'you' are doing the action, and the ُ at the end shows it's happening now
The ون at the end shows multiple people (you all) are doing the action
